CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cm\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"path\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\tlibcontainercgroups \\\"github.com/opencontainers/cgroups\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\tutilerrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tv1qos \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper/qos\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tkubefeatures \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    34\u2192)\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192const (\\n    37\u2192\\tpodCgroupNamePrefix = \\\"pod\\\"\\n    38\u2192)\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192// podContainerManagerImpl implements podContainerManager interface.\\n    41\u2192// It is the general implementation which allows pod level container\\n    42\u2192// management if qos Cgroup is enabled.\\n    43\u2192type podContainerManagerImpl struct {\\n    44\u2192\\t// qosContainersInfo hold absolute paths of the top level qos containers\\n    45\u2192\\tqosContainersInfo QOSContainersInfo\\n    46\u2192\\t// Stores the mounted cgroup subsystems\\n    47\u2192\\tsubsystems *CgroupSubsystems\\n    48\u2192\\t// cgroupManager is the cgroup Manager Object responsible for managing all\\n    49\u2192\\t// pod cgroups.\\n    50\u2192\\tcgroupManager CgroupManager\\n    51\u2192\\t// Maximum number of pids in a pod\\n    52\u2192\\tpodPidsLimit int64\\n    53\u2192\\t// enforceCPULimits controls whether cfs quota is enforced or not\\n    54\u2192\\tenforceCPULimits bool\\n    55\u2192\\t// cpuCFSQuotaPeriod is the cfs period value, cfs_period_us, setting per\\n    56\u2192\\t// node for all containers in usec\\n    57\u2192\\tcpuCFSQuotaPeriod uint64\\n    58\u2192\\t// podContainerManager is the ContainerManager running on the machine\\n    59\u2192\\tpodContainerManager ContainerManager\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192// Make sure that podContainerManagerImpl implements the PodContainerManager interface\\n    63\u2192var _ PodContainerManager = &podContainerManagerImpl{}\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192// Exists checks if the pod's cgroup already exists\\n    66\u2192func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) Exists(pod *v1.Pod) bool {\\n    67\u2192\\tpodContainerName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)\\n    68\u2192\\treturn m.cgroupManager.Exists(podContainerName)\\n    69\u2192}\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192// EnsureExists takes a pod as argument and makes sure that\\n    72\u2192// pod cgroup exists if qos cgroup hierarchy flag is enabled.\\n    73\u2192// If the pod level container doesn't already exist it is created.\\n    74\u2192func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) EnsureExists(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod) error {\\n    75\u2192\\t// check if container already exist\\n    76\u2192\\talreadyExists := m.Exists(pod)\\n    77\u2192\\tif !alreadyExists {\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tenforceCPULimits := m.enforceCPULimits\\n    79\u2192\\t\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.DisableCPUQuotaWithExclusiveCPUs) && m.podContainerManager.PodHasExclusiveCPUs(pod) {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Disabled CFS quota\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod))\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\tenforceCPULimits = false\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tenforceMemoryQoS := false\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.MemoryQoS) &&\\n    85\u2192\\t\\t\\tlibcontainercgroups.IsCgroup2UnifiedMode() {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\t\\tenforceMemoryQoS = true\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t// Create the pod container\\n    89\u2192\\t\\tpodContainerName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)\\n    90\u2192\\t\\tcontainerConfig := &CgroupConfig{\\n    91\u2192\\t\\t\\tName:               podContainerName,\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tResourceParameters: ResourceConfigForPod(pod, enforceCPULimits, m.cpuCFSQuotaPeriod, enforceMemoryQoS),\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\t\\tif m.podPidsLimit > 0 {\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontainerConfig.ResourceParameters.PidsLimit = &m.podPidsLimit\\n    96\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    97\u2192\\t\\tif enforceMemoryQoS {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"MemoryQoS config for pod\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"unified\\\", containerConfig.ResourceParameters.Unified)\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\t\\tif err := m.cgroupManager.Create(logger, containerConfig); err != nil {\\n   101\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create container for %v : %v\\\", podContainerName, err)\\n   102\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\t}\\n   105\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   106\u2192}\\n   107\u2192\\n   108\u2192// GetPodContainerName returns the CgroupName identifier, and its literal cgroupfs form on the host.\\n   109\u2192func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) GetPodContainerName(pod *v1.Pod) (CgroupName, string) {\\n   110\u2192\\tpodQOS := v1qos.GetPodQOS(pod)\\n   111\u2192\\t// Get the parent QOS container name\\n   112\u2192\\tvar parentContainer CgroupName\\n   113\u2192\\tswitch podQOS {\\n   114\u2192\\tcase v1.PodQOSGuaranteed:\\n   115\u2192\\t\\tparentContainer = m.qosContainersInfo.Guaranteed\\n   116\u2192\\tcase v1.PodQOSBurstable:\\n   117\u2192\\t\\tparentContainer = m.qosContainersInfo.Burstable\\n   118\u2192\\tcase v1.PodQOSBestEffort:\\n   119\u2192\\t\\tparentContainer = m.qosContainersInfo.BestEffort\\n   120\u2192\\t}\\n   121\u2192\\tpodContainer := GetPodCgroupNameSuffix(pod.UID)\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192\\t// Get the absolute path of the cgroup\\n   124\u2192\\tcgroupName := NewCgroupName(parentContainer, podContainer)\\n   125\u2192\\t// Get the literal cgroupfs name\\n   126\u2192\\tcgroupfsName := m.cgroupManager.Name(cgroupName)\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192\\treturn cgroupName, cgroupfsName\\n   129\u2192}\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) GetPodCgroupMemoryUsage(pod *v1.Pod) (uint64, error) {\\n   132\u2192\\tpodCgroupName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)\\n   133\u2192\\tmemUsage, err := m.cgroupManager.MemoryUsage(podCgroupName)\\n   134\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   135\u2192\\t\\treturn 0, err\\n   136\u2192\\t}\\n   137\u2192\\treturn uint64(memUsage), nil\\n   138\u2192}\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) GetPodCgroupConfig(pod *v1.Pod, resource v1.ResourceName) (*ResourceConfig, error) {\\n   141\u2192\\tpodCgroupName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)\\n   142\u2192\\treturn m.cgroupManager.GetCgroupConfig(podCgroupName, resource)\\n   143\u2192}\\n   144\u2192\\n   145\u2192func (m *podContainerManagerImpl) SetPodCgroupConfig(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, resourceConfig *ResourceConfig) error {\\n   146\u2192\\tpodCgroupName, _ := m.GetPodContainerName(pod)\\n   147\u2192\\treturn m.cgroupManager.SetCgroupConfig(logger, podCgroupName, resourceConfig)\\n   148\u2192}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192// Kill one process ID\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cm\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"bufio\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"math\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"path/filepath\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192\\tlibcontainercgroups \\\"github.com/opencontainers/cgroups\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tresourcehelper \\\"k8s.io/component-helpers/resource\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\tv1helper \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\tv1qos \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper/qos\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\tkubefeatures \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/util\\\"\\n    38\u2192)\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192const (\\n    41\u2192\\t// These limits are defined in the kernel:\\n    42\u2192\\t// https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/0bddd227f3dc55975e2b8dfa7fc6f959b062a2c7/kernel/sched/sched.h#L427-L428\\n    43\u2192\\tMinShares = 2\\n    44\u2192\\tMaxShares = 262144\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192\\tSharesPerCPU  = 1024\\n    47\u2192\\tMilliCPUToCPU = 1000\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192\\t// 100000 microseconds is equivalent to 100ms\\n    50\u2192\\tQuotaPeriod = 100000\\n    51\u2192\\t// 1000 microseconds is equivalent to 1ms\\n    52\u2192\\t// defined here:\\n    53\u2192\\t// https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/cac03ac368fabff0122853de2422d4e17a32de08/kernel/sched/core.c#L10546\\n    54\u2192\\tMinQuotaPeriod = 1000\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192\\t// From the inverse of the conversion in MilliCPUToQuota:\\n    57\u2192\\t// MinQuotaPeriod * MilliCPUToCPU / QuotaPeriod\\n    58\u2192\\tMinMilliCPULimit = 10\\n    59\u2192)\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192// MilliCPUToQuota converts milliCPU to CFS quota and period values.\\n    62\u2192// Input parameters and resulting value is number of microseconds.\\n    63\u2192func MilliCPUToQuota(milliCPU int64, period int64) (quota int64) {\\n    64\u2192\\t// CFS quota is measured in two values:\\n    65\u2192\\t//  - cfs_period_us=100ms (the amount of time to measure usage across given by period)\\n    66\u2192\\t//  - cfs_quota=20ms (the amount of cpu time allowed to be used across a period)\\n    67\u2192\\t// so in the above example, you are limited to 20% of a single CPU\\n    68\u2192\\t// for multi-cpu environments, you just scale equivalent amounts\\n    69\u2192\\t// see https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/scheduler/sched-bwc.txt for details\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192\\tif milliCPU == 0 {\\n    72\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n    73\u2192\\t}\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\tif !utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(kubefeatures.CPUCFSQuotaPeriod) {\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tperiod = QuotaPeriod\\n    77\u2192\\t}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// we then convert your milliCPU to a value normalized over a period\\n    80\u2192\\tquota = (milliCPU * period) / MilliCPUToCPU\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192\\t// quota needs to be a minimum of 1ms.\\n    83\u2192\\tif quota < MinQuotaPeriod {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tquota = MinQuotaPeriod\\n    85\u2192\\t}\\n    86\u2192\\treturn\\n    87\u2192}\\n    88\u2192\\n    89\u2192// MilliCPUToShares converts the milliCPU to CFS shares.\\n    90\u2192func MilliCPUToShares(milliCPU int64) uint64 {\\n    91\u2192\\tif milliCPU == 0 {\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t// Docker converts zero milliCPU to unset, which maps to kernel default\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t// for unset: 1024. Return 2 here to really match kernel default for\\n    94\u2192\\t\\t// zero milliCPU.\\n    95\u2192\\t\\treturn MinShares\\n    96\u2192\\t}\\n    97\u2192\\t// Conceptually (milliCPU / milliCPUToCPU) * sharesPerCPU, but factored to improve rounding.\\n    98\u2192\\tshares := (milliCPU * SharesPerCPU) / MilliCPUToCPU\\n    99\u2192\\tif shares < MinShares {\\n   100\u2192\\t\\treturn MinShares\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"feb7ff54-904a-45eb-9b16-2713f4e08bd0\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:04.406Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"6d73b7a0-900a-40b3-97fa-ffa9e0354c58\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"feb7ff54-904a-45eb-9b16-2713f4e08bd0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaf8145\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01TCthTF77sUECwLLUBAeLLr\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cpumanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"math\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/go-logr/logr\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\truntimeapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/topology\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\tkubecontainer \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/container\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/status\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    41\u2192)\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of pods to reconcile.\\n    44\u2192type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192type runtimeService interface {\\n    47\u2192\\tUpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, id string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) error\\n    48\u2192}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192type policyName string\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192// cpuManagerStateFileName is the file name where cpu manager stores its state\\n    53\u2192const cpuManagerStateFileName = \\\"cpu_manager_state\\\"\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod cpus.\\n    56\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    57\u2192\\t// Start is called during Kubelet initialization.\\n    58\u2192\\t// Start takes a `Context` because it may possibly spin the reconcileState helper, which in turn\\n    59\u2192\\t// needs to update container state, which takes a context.\\n    60\u2192\\tStart(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\t// Called to trigger the allocation of CPUs to a container. This must be\\n    63\u2192\\t// called at some point prior to the AddContainer() call for a container,\\n    64\u2192\\t// e.g. at pod admission time.\\n    65\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds the mapping between container ID to pod UID and the container name\\n    68\u2192\\t// The mapping used to remove the CPU allocation during the container removal\\n    69\u2192\\tAddContainer(logger logr.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer is called after Kubelet decides to kill or delete a\\n    72\u2192\\t// container. After this call, the CPU manager stops trying to reconcile\\n    73\u2192\\t// that container and any CPUs dedicated to the container are freed.\\n    74\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192\\t// State returns a read-only interface to the internal CPU manager state.\\n    77\u2192\\tState() state.Reader\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    80\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    81\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    82\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192\\t// GetExclusiveCPUs implements the podresources.CPUsProvider interface to provide\\n    85\u2192\\t// exclusively allocated cpus for the container\\n    86\u2192\\tGetExclusiveCPUs(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    89\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment per Pod\\n    90\u2192\\t// among this and other resource controllers.\\n    91\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableCPUs returns the total set of CPUs available for allocation.\\n    94\u2192\\tGetAllocatableCPUs() cpuset.CPUSet\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\t// GetCPUAffinity returns cpuset which includes cpus from shared pools\\n    97\u2192\\t// as well as exclusively allocated cpus\\n    98\u2192\\tGetCPUAffinity(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192\\t// GetAllCPUs returns all the CPUs known by cpumanager, as reported by the\\n   101\u2192\\t// hardware discovery. Maps to the CPU capacity.\\n   102\u2192\\tGetAllCPUs() cpuset.CPUSet\\n   103\u2192}\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192type manager struct {\\n   106\u2192\\tsync.Mutex\\n   107\u2192\\tpolicy Policy\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\t// reconcilePeriod is the duration between calls to reconcileState.\\n   110\u2192\\treconcilePeriod time.Duration\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192\\t// state allows pluggable CPU assignment policies while sharing a common\\n   113\u2192\\t// representation of state for the system to inspect and reconcile.\\n   114\u2192\\tstate state.State\\n   115\u2192\\n   116\u2192\\t// lastUpdatedstate holds state for each container from the last time it was updated.\\n   117\u2192\\tlastUpdateState state.State\\n   118\u2192\\n   119\u2192\\t// containerRuntime is the container runtime service interface needed\\n   120\u2192\\t// to make UpdateContainerResources() calls against the containers.\\n   121\u2192\\tcontainerRuntime runtimeService\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192\\t// activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node\\n   124\u2192\\t// so all the containers can be updated in the reconciliation loop.\\n   125\u2192\\tactivePods ActivePodsFunc\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192\\t// podStatusProvider provides a method for obtaining pod statuses\\n   128\u2192\\t// and the containerID of their containers\\n   129\u2192\\tpodStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\t// containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID\\n   132\u2192\\t// for all containers a pod\\n   133\u2192\\tcontainerMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192\\ttopology *topology.CPUTopology\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\tnodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\t// sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.\\n   140\u2192\\t// We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.\\n   141\u2192\\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192\\t// stateFileDirectory holds the directory where the state file for checkpoints is held.\\n   144\u2192\\tstateFileDirectory string\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\t// allCPUs is the set of online CPUs as reported by the system\\n   147\u2192\\tallCPUs cpuset.CPUSet\\n   148\u2192\\n   149\u2192\\t// allocatableCPUs is the set of online CPUs as reported by the system,\\n   150\u2192\\t// and available for allocation, minus the reserved set\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"b6e0e011-e51d-41a3-8d99-55326aa086c9\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:10.737Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"5146d41d-e164-4b9b-9cf4-8784b6a800fa\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b6e0e011-e51d-41a3-8d99-55326aa086c9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaf8145\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01H8piRnTxvHz39aLZSki59Z\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_011HWauoKU6eP1Ym2ZSoZz3B\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/memory_manager.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1180,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":53581,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1180},\"output_tokens\":2,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GKwfQyV36LnfgHQKD\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"042d683c-4caa-4932-af7c-079ac120b0ef\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:10.977Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"042d683c-4caa-4932-af7c-079ac120b0ef\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaf8145\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_011HWauoKU6eP1Ym2ZSoZz3B\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_011HWauoKU6eP1Ym2ZSoZz3B\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:10.980Z\",\"uuid\":\"4b56d606-e288-4e04-95f1-183966da4e21\"}\n{\"parentUuid\" 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package memorymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"runtime\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\truntimeapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tpodutil \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tcorev1helper \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\tkubeletconfig \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/apis/config\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/state\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/status\\\"\\n    40\u2192)\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192// memoryManagerStateFileName is the file name where memory manager stores its state\\n    43\u2192const memoryManagerStateFileName = \\\"memory_manager_state\\\"\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of active pods\\n    46\u2192type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod\\n    47\u2192\\n    48\u2192type runtimeService interface {\\n    49\u2192\\tUpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, id string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) error\\n    50\u2192}\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192type sourcesReadyStub struct{}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AddSource(source string) {}\\n    55\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AllReady() bool          { return true }\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod memory.\\n    58\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    59\u2192\\t// Start is called during Kubelet initialization.\\n    60\u2192\\tStart(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds the mapping between container ID to pod UID and the container name\\n    63\u2192\\t// The mapping used to remove the memory allocation during the container removal\\n    64\u2192\\tAddContainer(logger klog.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192\\t// Allocate is called to pre-allocate memory resources during Pod admission.\\n    67\u2192\\t// This must be called at some point prior to the AddContainer() call for a container, e.g. at pod admission time.\\n    68\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer is called after Kubelet decides to kill or delete a\\n    71\u2192\\t// container. After this call, any memory allocated to the container is freed.\\n    72\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    73\u2192\\n    74\u2192\\t// State returns a read-only interface to the internal memory manager state.\\n    75\u2192\\tState() state.Reader\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    78\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    79\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    80\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(*v1.Pod, *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    83\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    84\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    85\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(*v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\t// GetMemoryNUMANodes provides NUMA nodes that are used to allocate the container memory\\n    88\u2192\\tGetMemoryNUMANodes(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) sets.Set[int]\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableMemory returns the amount of allocatable memory for each NUMA node\\n    91\u2192\\tGetAllocatableMemory() []state.Block\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\t// GetMemory returns the memory allocated by a container from NUMA nodes\\n    94\u2192\\tGetMemory(podUID, containerName string) []state.Block\\n    95\u2192}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192type manager struct {\\n    98\u2192\\tsync.Mutex\\n    99\u2192\\tpolicy Policy\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192\\t// state allows to restore information regarding memory allocation for guaranteed pods\\n   102\u2192\\t// in the case of the kubelet restart\\n   103\u2192\\tstate state.State\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\t// containerRuntime is the container runtime service interface needed\\n   106\u2192\\t// to make UpdateContainerResources() calls against the containers.\\n   107\u2192\\tcontainerRuntime runtimeService\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\t// activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node\\n   110\u2192\\t// so all the containers can be updated during call to the removeStaleState.\\n   111\u2192\\tactivePods ActivePodsFunc\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192\\t// podStatusProvider provides a method for obtaining pod statuses\\n   114\u2192\\t// and the containerID of their containers\\n   115\u2192\\tpodStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192\\t// containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID\\n   118\u2192\\t// for all containers a pod\\n   119\u2192\\tcontainerMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192\\t// sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.\\n   122\u2192\\t// We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.\\n   123\u2192\\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady\\n   124\u2192\\n   125\u2192\\t// stateFileDirectory holds the directory where the state file for checkpoints is held.\\n   126\u2192\\tstateFileDirectory string\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192\\t// allocatableMemory holds the allocatable memory for each NUMA node\\n   129\u2192\\tallocatableMemory []state.Block\\n   130\u2192}\\n   131\u2192\\n   132\u2192var _ Manager = &manager{}\\n   133\u2192\\n   134\u2192// NewManager returns new instance of the memory manager\\n   135\u2192func NewManager(logger klog.Logger, policyName string, machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList, reservedMemory []kubeletconfig.MemoryReservation, stateFileDirectory string, affinity topologymanager.Store) (Manager, error) {\\n   136\u2192\\tvar policy Policy\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192\\tswitch policyType(policyName) {\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192\\tcase policyTypeNone:\\n   141\u2192\\t\\tpolicy = NewPolicyNone(logger)\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192\\tcase PolicyTypeStatic:\\n   144\u2192\\t\\tif runtime.GOOS == \\\"windows\\\" {\\n   145\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"policy %q is not available on Windows\\\", PolicyTypeStatic)\\n   146\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192\\t\\tsystemReserved, err := getSystemReservedMemory(machineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation, reservedMemory)\\n   149\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package topologymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/bitmask\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192const (\\n    33\u2192\\t// defaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes specifies the maximum number of NUMA Nodes that\\n    34\u2192\\t// the TopologyManager supports on the underlying machine.\\n    35\u2192\\t//\\n    36\u2192\\t// At present, having more than this number of NUMA Nodes will result in a\\n    37\u2192\\t// state explosion when trying to enumerate possible NUMAAffinity masks and\\n    38\u2192\\t// generate hints for them. As such, if more NUMA Nodes than this are\\n    39\u2192\\t// present on a machine and the TopologyManager is enabled, an error will\\n    40\u2192\\t// be returned and the TopologyManager will not be loaded.\\n    41\u2192\\tdefaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes = 8\\n    42\u2192\\t// ErrorTopologyAffinity represents the type for a TopologyAffinityError\\n    43\u2192\\tErrorTopologyAffinity = \\\"TopologyAffinityError\\\"\\n    44\u2192)\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192// TopologyAffinityError represents an resource alignment error\\n    47\u2192type TopologyAffinityError struct{}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (e TopologyAffinityError) Error() string {\\n    50\u2192\\treturn \\\"Resources cannot be allocated with Topology locality\\\"\\n    51\u2192}\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192func (e TopologyAffinityError) Type() string {\\n    54\u2192\\treturn ErrorTopologyAffinity\\n    55\u2192}\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod topology hints\\n    58\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    59\u2192\\t// PodAdmitHandler is implemented by Manager\\n    60\u2192\\tlifecycle.PodAdmitHandler\\n    61\u2192\\t// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider\\n    62\u2192\\t// wants to be consulted with when making topology hints\\n    63\u2192\\tAddHintProvider(logger klog.Logger, h HintProvider)\\n    64\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking\\n    65\u2192\\tAddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    66\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking\\n    67\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(containerID string) error\\n    68\u2192\\t// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints\\n    69\u2192\\tStore\\n    70\u2192}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192type manager struct {\\n    73\u2192\\t//Topology Manager Scope\\n    74\u2192\\tscope Scope\\n    75\u2192}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192// HintProvider is an interface for components that want to collaborate to\\n    78\u2192// achieve globally optimal concrete resource alignment with respect to\\n    79\u2192// NUMA locality.\\n    80\u2192type HintProvider interface {\\n    81\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible\\n    82\u2192\\t// concrete resource allocations in terms of NUMA locality hints. Each hint\\n    83\u2192\\t// is optionally marked \\\"preferred\\\" and indicates the set of NUMA nodes\\n    84\u2192\\t// involved in the hypothetical allocation. The topology manager calls\\n    85\u2192\\t// this function for each hint provider, and merges the hints to produce\\n    86\u2192\\t// a consensus \\\"best\\\" hint. The hint providers may subsequently query the\\n    87\u2192\\t// topology manager to influence actual resource assignment.\\n    88\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    89\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible\\n    90\u2192\\t// concrete resource allocations per Pod in terms of NUMA locality hints.\\n    91\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    92\u2192\\t// Allocate triggers resource allocation to occur on the HintProvider after\\n    93\u2192\\t// all hints have been gathered and the aggregated Hint is available via a\\n    94\u2192\\t// call to Store.GetAffinity().\\n    95\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    96\u2192}\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192// Store interface is to allow Hint Providers to retrieve pod affinity\\n    99\u2192type Store interface {\\n   100\u2192\\tGetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint\\n   101\u2192\\tGetPolicy() Policy\\n   102\u2192}\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192// TopologyHint is a struct containing the NUMANodeAffinity for a Container\\n   105\u2192type TopologyHint struct {\\n   106\u2192\\tNUMANodeAffinity bitmask.BitMask\\n   107\u2192\\t// Preferred is set to true when the NUMANodeAffinity encodes a preferred\\n   108\u2192\\t// allocation for the Container. It is set to false otherwise.\\n   109\u2192\\tPreferred bool\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// IsEqual checks if TopologyHint are equal\\n   113\u2192func (th *TopologyHint) IsEqual(topologyHint TopologyHint) bool {\\n   114\u2192\\tif th.Preferred == topologyHint.Preferred {\\n   115\u2192\\t\\tif th.NUMANodeAffinity == nil || topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity == nil {\\n   116\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity == topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity\\n   117\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   118\u2192\\t\\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n   119\u2192\\t}\\n   120\u2192\\treturn false\\n   121\u2192}\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192// LessThan checks if TopologyHint `a` is less than TopologyHint `b`\\n   124\u2192// this means that either `a` is a preferred hint and `b` is not\\n   125\u2192// or `a` NUMANodeAffinity attribute is narrower than `b` NUMANodeAffinity attribute.\\n   126\u2192func (th *TopologyHint) LessThan(other TopologyHint) bool {\\n   127\u2192\\tif th.Preferred != other.Preferred {\\n   128\u2192\\t\\treturn th.Preferred\\n   129\u2192\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsNarrowerThan(other.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n   131\u2192}\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192var _ Manager = &manager{}\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192// NewManager creates a new TopologyManager based on provided policy and scope\\n   136\u2192func NewManager(topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyPolicyName string, topologyScopeName string, topologyPolicyOptions map[string]string) (Manager, error) {\\n   137\u2192\\t// Use klog.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper logger to pass in.\\n   138\u2192\\t// Replace this with an appropriate logger when refactoring this function to accept a logger parameter.\\n   139\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   140\u2192\\n   141\u2192\\t// When policy is none, the scope is not relevant, so we can short circuit here.\\n   142\u2192\\tif topologyPolicyName == PolicyNone {\\n   143\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Creating topology manager with none policy\\\")\\n   144\u2192\\t\\treturn &manager{scope: NewNoneScope()}, nil\\n   145\u2192\\t}\\n   146\u2192\\n   147\u2192\\topts, err := NewPolicyOptions(logger, topologyPolicyOptions)\\n   148\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   149\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   150\u2192\\t}\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"b2ed383f-9e03-4f7c-a98f-86274507ad35\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:11.244Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"5fee25f4-f3e1-42b4-bb51-81ba1d94a42e\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"b2ed383f-9e03-4f7c-a98f-86274507ad35\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaf8145\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01H8piRnTxvHz39aLZSki59Z\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_016ac9qyyDMfA7CXCDMoY7cD\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/manager.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":7,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":1180,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":53581,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":1180},\"output_tokens\":2,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GKwfQyV36LnfgHQKD\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"73117031-e2e2-421b-a39c-f948df52e323\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:11.500Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"73117031-e2e2-421b-a39c-f948df52e323\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaf8145\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_016ac9qyyDMfA7CXCDMoY7cD\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_016ac9qyyDMfA7CXCDMoY7cD\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:11.503Z\",\"uuid\":\"5b037963-d87e-430b-95f5-23c2737ac370\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7311 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package devicemanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\tgoerrors \\\"errors\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"path/filepath\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"runtime\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\terrorsutil \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\tpluginapi \\\"k8s.io/kubelet/pkg/apis/deviceplugin/v1beta1\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\tpodutil \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\\"\\n    41\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    42\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/checkpointmanager\\\"\\n    43\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/checkpointmanager/errors\\\"\\n    44\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    45\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/checkpoint\\\"\\n    46\u2192\\tplugin \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/plugin/v1beta1\\\"\\n    47\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/resourceupdates\\\"\\n    48\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    49\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    50\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    51\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    52\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/pluginmanager/cache\\\"\\n    53\u2192\\tschedulerframework \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n    54\u2192)\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192const nodeWithoutTopology = -1\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of pods to reconcile.\\n    59\u2192type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192// ManagerImpl is the structure in charge of managing Device Plugins.\\n    62\u2192type ManagerImpl struct {\\n    63\u2192\\tcheckpointdir string\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192\\tendpoints map[string]endpointInfo // Key is ResourceName\\n    66\u2192\\tmutex     sync.Mutex\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192\\tserver plugin.Server\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\t// activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node\\n    71\u2192\\t// so the amount of pluginResources requested by existing pods\\n    72\u2192\\t// could be counted when updating allocated devices\\n    73\u2192\\tactivePods ActivePodsFunc\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\t// sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.\\n    76\u2192\\t// We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.\\n    77\u2192\\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// allDevices holds all the devices currently registered to the device manager\\n    80\u2192\\tallDevices ResourceDeviceInstances\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192\\t// healthyDevices contains all the registered healthy resourceNames and their exported device IDs.\\n    83\u2192\\thealthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\t// unhealthyDevices contains all the unhealthy devices and their exported device IDs.\\n    86\u2192\\tunhealthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\t// allocatedDevices contains allocated deviceIds, keyed by resourceName.\\n    89\u2192\\tallocatedDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192\\t// podDevices contains pod to allocated device mapping.\\n    92\u2192\\tpodDevices        *podDevices\\n    93\u2192\\tcheckpointManager checkpointmanager.CheckpointManager\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192\\t// List of NUMA Nodes available on the underlying machine\\n    96\u2192\\tnumaNodes []int\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192\\t// Store of Topology Affinities that the Device Manager can query.\\n    99\u2192\\ttopologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192\\t// devicesToReuse contains devices that can be reused as they have been allocated to\\n   102\u2192\\t// init containers.\\n   103\u2192\\tdevicesToReuse PodReusableDevices\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\t// containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID\\n   106\u2192\\t// for all containers in a pod. Used to detect pods running across a restart\\n   107\u2192\\tcontainerMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\t// containerRunningSet identifies which container among those present in `containerMap`\\n   110\u2192\\t// was reported running by the container runtime when `containerMap` was computed.\\n   111\u2192\\t// Used to detect pods running across a restart\\n   112\u2192\\tcontainerRunningSet sets.Set[string]\\n   113\u2192\\n   114\u2192\\t// update channel for device health updates\\n   115\u2192\\tupdate chan resourceupdates.Update\\n   116\u2192}\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192type endpointInfo struct {\\n   119\u2192\\te    endpoint\\n   120\u2192\\topts *pluginapi.DevicePluginOptions\\n   121\u2192}\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192type sourcesReadyStub struct{}\\n   124\u2192\\n   125\u2192// PodReusableDevices is a map by pod name of devices to reuse.\\n   126\u2192type PodReusableDevices map[string]map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AddSource(source string) {}\\n   129\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AllReady() bool          { return true }\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192// NewManagerImpl creates a new manager.\\n   132\u2192func NewManagerImpl(topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store) (*ManagerImpl, error) {\\n   133\u2192\\t// Use klog.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper logger to pass in.\\n   134\u2192\\t// Replace this with an appropriate context when refactoring this function to accept a logger parameter.\\n   135\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   136\u2192\\tsocketPath := pluginapi.KubeletSocket\\n   137\u2192\\tif runtime.GOOS == \\\"windows\\\" {\\n   138\u2192\\t\\tsocketPath = os.Getenv(\\\"SYSTEMDRIVE\\\") + pluginapi.KubeletSocketWindows\\n   139\u2192\\t}\\n   140\u2192\\treturn newManagerImpl(logger, socketPath, topology, topologyAffinityStore)\\n   141\u2192}\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192func newManagerImpl(logger klog.Logger, socketPath string, topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store) (*ManagerImpl, error) {\\n   144\u2192\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Creating Device Plugin manager\\\", \\\"path\\\", socketPath)\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\tvar numaNodes []int\\n   147\u2192\\tfor _, node := range topology {\\n   148\u2192\\t\\tnumaNodes = append(numaNodes, node.Id)\\n   149\u2192\\t}\\n   150\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cm\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\truntimeapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    26\u2192)\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192type InternalContainerLifecycle interface {\\n    29\u2192\\tPreCreateContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerConfig *runtimeapi.ContainerConfig) error\\n    30\u2192\\tPreStartContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) error\\n    31\u2192\\tPostStopContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// Implements InternalContainerLifecycle interface.\\n    35\u2192type internalContainerLifecycleImpl struct {\\n    36\u2192\\tcpuManager      cpumanager.Manager\\n    37\u2192\\tmemoryManager   memorymanager.Manager\\n    38\u2192\\ttopologyManager topologymanager.Manager\\n    39\u2192}\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192func (i *internalContainerLifecycleImpl) PreStartContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) error {\\n    42\u2192\\tif i.cpuManager != nil {\\n    43\u2192\\t\\ti.cpuManager.AddContainer(logger, pod, container, containerID)\\n    44\u2192\\t}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192\\tif i.memoryManager != nil {\\n    47\u2192\\t\\ti.memoryManager.AddContainer(logger, pod, container, containerID)\\n    48\u2192\\t}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192\\ti.topologyManager.AddContainer(pod, container, containerID)\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    53\u2192}\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192func (i *internalContainerLifecycleImpl) PostStopContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error {\\n    56\u2192\\treturn i.topologyManager.RemoveContainer(containerID)\\n    57\u2192}\\n    58\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. context.\\n/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/cpu_manager.go-\\tStart(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, 16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cm\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"path\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"path/filepath\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192\\tlibcontainercgroups \\\"github.com/opencontainers/cgroups\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/opencontainers/cgroups/fscommon\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\tlibcontainercgroupmanager \\\"github.com/opencontainers/cgroups/manager\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\tcgroupsystemd \\\"github.com/opencontainers/cgroups/systemd\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tv1helper \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192\\tutilruntime \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    38\u2192)\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192const (\\n    41\u2192\\t// systemdSuffix is the cgroup name suffix for systemd\\n    42\u2192\\tsystemdSuffix string = \\\".slice\\\"\\n    43\u2192\\t// Cgroup2MemoryMin is memory.min for cgroup v2\\n    44\u2192\\tCgroup2MemoryMin string = \\\"memory.min\\\"\\n    45\u2192\\t// Cgroup2MemoryHigh is memory.high for cgroup v2\\n    46\u2192\\tCgroup2MemoryHigh      string = \\\"memory.high\\\"\\n    47\u2192\\tCgroup2MaxCpuLimit     string = \\\"max\\\"\\n    48\u2192\\tCgroup2MaxSwapFilename string = \\\"memory.swap.max\\\"\\n    49\u2192)\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192var RootCgroupName = CgroupName([]string{})\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192// NewCgroupName composes a new cgroup name.\\n    54\u2192// Use RootCgroupName as base to start at the root.\\n    55\u2192// This function does some basic check for invalid characters at the name.\\n    56\u2192func NewCgroupName(base CgroupName, components ...string) CgroupName {\\n    57\u2192\\tfor _, component := range components {\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t// Forbit using \\\"_\\\" in internal names. When remapping internal\\n    59\u2192\\t\\t// names to systemd cgroup driver, we want to remap \\\"-\\\" => \\\"_\\\",\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t// so we forbid \\\"_\\\" so that we can always reverse the mapping.\\n    61\u2192\\t\\tif strings.Contains(component, \\\"/\\\") || strings.Contains(component, \\\"_\\\") {\\n    62\u2192\\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid character in component [%q] of CgroupName\\\", component))\\n    63\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    64\u2192\\t}\\n    65\u2192\\treturn CgroupName(append(append([]string{}, base...), components...))\\n    66\u2192}\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192func escapeSystemdCgroupName(part string) string {\\n    69\u2192\\treturn strings.Replace(part, \\\"-\\\", \\\"_\\\", -1)\\n    70\u2192}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192func unescapeSystemdCgroupName(part string) string {\\n    73\u2192\\treturn strings.Replace(part, \\\"_\\\", \\\"-\\\", -1)\\n    74\u2192}\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192// cgroupName.ToSystemd converts the internal cgroup name to a systemd name.\\n    77\u2192// For example, the name {\\\"kubepods\\\", \\\"burstable\\\", \\\"pod1234-abcd-5678-efgh\\\"} becomes\\n    78\u2192// \\\"/kubepods.slice/kubepods-burstable.slice/kubepods-burstable-pod1234_abcd_5678_efgh.slice\\\"\\n    79\u2192// This function always expands the systemd name into the cgroupfs form. If only\\n    80\u2192// the last part is needed, use path.Base(...) on it to discard the rest.\\n    81\u2192func (cgroupName CgroupName) ToSystemd() string {\\n    82\u2192\\tif len(cgroupName) == 0 || (len(cgroupName) == 1 && cgroupName[0] == \\\"\\\") {\\n    83\u2192\\t\\treturn \\\"/\\\"\\n    84\u2192\\t}\\n    85\u2192\\tnewparts := []string{}\\n    86\u2192\\tfor _, part := range cgroupName {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tpart = escapeSystemdCgroupName(part)\\n    88\u2192\\t\\tnewparts = append(newparts, part)\\n    89\u2192\\t}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192\\tresult, err := cgroupsystemd.ExpandSlice(strings.Join(newparts, \\\"-\\\") + systemdSuffix)\\n    92\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t// Should never happen...\\n    94\u2192\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\\\"error converting cgroup name [%v] to systemd format: %v\\\", cgroupName, err))\\n    95\u2192\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\treturn result\\n    97\u2192}\\n    98\u2192\\n    99\u2192func ParseSystemdToCgroupName(name string) CgroupName {\\n   100\u2192\\tdriverName := path.Base(name)\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"867df9ba-6e72-4f13-9a7c-813d072ac9f9\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:16.982Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"add527f9-386e-42b9-bbd1-236d4e3da88d\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"867df9ba-6e72-4f13-9a7c-813d072ac9f9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"aaf8145\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01TtbkXeSxHBRNzwQ7RAhFxK\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect. on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    14\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    15\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    16\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    17\u2192*/\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192package cm\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192import (\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tkubefeatures \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/events\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/stats/pidlimit\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\tkubetypes \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/types\\\"\\n    36\u2192)\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192const (\\n    39\u2192\\tdefaultNodeAllocatableCgroupName = \\\"kubepods\\\"\\n    40\u2192)\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192// createNodeAllocatableCgroups creates Node Allocatable Cgroup when CgroupsPerQOS flag is specified as true\\n    43\u2192func (cm *containerManagerImpl) createNodeAllocatableCgroups(logger klog.Logger) error {\\n    44\u2192\\tnodeAllocatable := cm.internalCapacity\\n    45\u2192\\t// Use Node Allocatable limits instead of capacity if the user requested enforcing node allocatable.\\n    46\u2192\\tnc := cm.NodeConfig.NodeAllocatableConfig\\n    47\u2192\\tif cm.CgroupsPerQOS && nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.NodeAllocatableEnforcementKey) {\\n    48\u2192\\t\\tnodeAllocatable = cm.getNodeAllocatableInternalAbsolute()\\n    49\u2192\\t}\\n    50\u2192\\n    51\u2192\\tcgroupConfig := &CgroupConfig{\\n    52\u2192\\t\\tName: cm.cgroupRoot,\\n    53\u2192\\t\\t// The default limits for cpu shares can be very low which can lead to CPU starvation for pods.\\n    54\u2192\\t\\tResourceParameters: cm.getCgroupConfig(nodeAllocatable, false),\\n    55\u2192\\t}\\n    56\u2192\\tif cm.cgroupManager.Exists(cgroupConfig.Name) {\\n    57\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n    58\u2192\\t}\\n    59\u2192\\tif err := cm.cgroupManager.Create(logger, cgroupConfig); err != nil {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Failed to create cgroup\\\", \\\"cgroupName\\\", cm.cgroupRoot)\\n    61\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n    62\u2192\\t}\\n    63\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    64\u2192}\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192// enforceNodeAllocatableCgroups enforce Node Allocatable Cgroup settings.\\n    67\u2192func (cm *containerManagerImpl) enforceNodeAllocatableCgroups(logger klog.Logger) error {\\n    68\u2192\\tnc := cm.NodeConfig.NodeAllocatableConfig\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\t// We need to update limits on node allocatable cgroup no matter what because\\n    71\u2192\\t// default cpu shares on cgroups are low and can cause cpu starvation.\\n    72\u2192\\tnodeAllocatable := cm.internalCapacity\\n    73\u2192\\t// Use Node Allocatable limits instead of capacity if the user requested enforcing node allocatable.\\n    74\u2192\\tif cm.CgroupsPerQOS && nc.EnforceNodeAllocatable.Has(kubetypes.NodeAllocatableEnforcementKey) {\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tnodeAllocatable = cm.getNodeAllocatableInternalAbsolute()\\n    76\u2192\\t}\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Attempting to enforce Node Allocatable\\\", \\\"config\\\", nc)\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192\\tcgroupConfig := &CgroupConfig{\\n    81\u2192\\t\\tName:               cm.cgroupRoot,\\n    82\u2192\\t\\tResourceParameters: cm.getCgroupConfig(nodeAllocatable, false),\\n    83\u2192\\t}\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\t// Using ObjectReference for events as the node maybe not cached; refer to #42701 for detail.\\n    86\u2192\\tnodeRef := nodeRefFromNode(cm.nodeInfo.Name)\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\t// If Node Allocatable is enforced on a node that has not been drained or is updated on an existing node to a lower value,\\n    89\u2192\\t// existing memory usage across pods might be higher than current Node Allocatable Memory Limits.\\n    90\u2192\\t// Pod Evictions are expected to bring down memory usage to below Node Allocatable limits.\\n    91\u2192\\t// Until evictions happen retry cgroup updates.\\n    92\u2192\\t// Update limits on non root cgroup-root to be safe since the default limits for CPU can be too low.\\n    93\u2192\\t// Check if cgroupRoot is set to a non-empty value (empty would be the root container)\\n    94\u2192\\tif len(cm.cgroupRoot) > 0 {\\n    95\u2192\\t\\tgo func() {\\n    96\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor {\\n    97\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\terr := cm.cgroupManager.Update(logger, cgroupConfig)\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcm.recorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeNormal, events.SuccessfulNodeAllocatableEnforcement, \\\"Updated Node Allocatable limit across pods\\\")\\n   100\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"c42689d0-30d1-4732-8541-92094651296a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:22.809Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"d8236d61-6a83-4efb-a45b-3b176e85b27e\"}\n",
