\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n    54\u2192)\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192const nodeWithoutTopology = -1\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of pods to reconcile.\\n    59\u2192type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192// ManagerImpl is the structure in charge of managing Device Plugins.\\n    62\u2192type ManagerImpl struct {\\n    63\u2192\\tcheckpointdir string\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192\\tendpoints map[string]endpointInfo // Key is ResourceName\\n    66\u2192\\tmutex     sync.Mutex\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192\\tserver plugin.Server\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\t// activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node\\n    71\u2192\\t// so the amount of pluginResources requested by existing pods\\n    72\u2192\\t// could be counted when updating allocated devices\\n    73\u2192\\tactivePods ActivePodsFunc\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\t// sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.\\n    76\u2192\\t// We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.\\n    77\u2192\\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// allDevices holds all the devices currently registered to the device manager\\n    80\u2192\\tallDevices ResourceDeviceInstances\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192\\t// healthyDevices contains all the registered healthy resourceNames and their exported device IDs.\\n    83\u2192\\thealthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\t// unhealthyDevices contains all the unhealthy devices and their exported device IDs.\\n    86\u2192\\tunhealthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\t// allocatedDevices contains allocated deviceIds, keyed by resourceName.\\n    89\u2192\\tallocatedDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192\\t// podDevices contains pod to allocated device mapping.\\n    92\u2192\\tpodDevices        *podDevices\\n    93\u2192\\tcheckpointManager checkpointmanager.CheckpointManager\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192\\t// List of NUMA Nodes available on the underlying machine\\n    96\u2192\\tnumaNodes []int\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192\\t// Store of Topology Affinities that the Device Manager can query.\\n    99\u2192\\ttopologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192\\t// devicesToReuse contains devices that can be reused as they have been allocated to\\n   102\u2192\\t// init containers.\\n   103\u2192\\tdevicesToReuse PodReusableDevices\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\t// containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID\\n   106\u2192\\t// for all containers in a pod. Used to detect pods running across a restart\\n   107\u2192\\tcontainerMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\t// containerRunningSet identifies which container among those present in `containerMap`\\n   110\u2192\\t// was reported running by the container runtime when `containerMap` was computed.\\n   111\u2192\\t// Used to detect pods running across a restart\\n   112\u2192\\tcontainerRunningSet sets.Set[string]\\n   113\u2192\\n   114\u2192\\t// update channel for device health updates\\n   115\u2192\\tupdate chan resourceupdates.Update\\n   116\u2192}\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192type endpointInfo struct {\\n   119\u2192\\te    endpoint\\n   120\u2192\\topts *pluginapi.DevicePluginOptions\\n   121\u2192}\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192type sourcesReadyStub struct{}\\n   124\u2192\\n   125\u2192// PodReusableDevices is a map by pod name of devices to reuse.\\n   126\u2192type PodReusableDevices map[string]map[string]sets.Set[string]\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AddSource(source string) {}\\n   129\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AllReady() bool          { return true }\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192// NewManagerImpl creates a new manager.\\n   132\u2192func NewManagerImpl(topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store) (*ManagerImpl, error) {\\n   133\u2192\\t// Use klog.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper logger to pass in.\\n   134\u2192\\t// Replace this with an appropriate context when refactoring this function to accept a logger parameter.\\n   135\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   136\u2192\\tsocketPath := pluginapi.KubeletSocket\\n   137\u2192\\tif runtime.GOOS == \\\"windows\\\" {\\n   138\u2192\\t\\tsocketPath = os.Getenv(\\\"SYSTEMDRIVE\\\") + pluginapi.KubeletSocketWindows\\n   139\u2192\\t}\\n   140\u2192\\treturn newManagerImpl(logger, socketPath, topology, topologyAffinityStore)\\n   141\u2192}\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192func newManagerImpl(logger klog.Logger, socketPath string, topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store) (*ManagerImpl, error) {\\n   144\u2192\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"Creating Device Plugin manager\\\", \\\"path\\\", socketPath)\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\tvar numaNodes []int\\n   147\u2192\\tfor _, node := range topology {\\n   148\u2192\\t\\tnumaNodes = append(numaNodes, node.Id)\\n   149\u2192\\t}\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192\\tmanager := &ManagerImpl{\\n   152\u2192\\t\\tendpoints: make(map[string]endpointInfo),\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192\\t\\tallDevices:            NewResourceDeviceInstances(),\\n   155\u2192\\t\\thealthyDevices:        make(map[string]sets.Set[string]),\\n   156\u2192\\t\\tunhealthyDevices:      make(map[string]sets.Set[string]),\\n   157\u2192\\t\\tallocatedDevices:      make(map[string]sets.Set[string]),\\n   158\u2192\\t\\tpodDevices:            newPodDevices(),\\n   159\u2192\\t\\tnumaNodes:             numaNodes,\\n   160\u2192\\t\\ttopologyAffinityStore: topologyAffinityStore,\\n   161\u2192\\t\\tdevicesToReuse:        make(PodReusableDevices),\\n   162\u2192\\t\\tupdate:                make(chan resourceupdates.Update, 100),\\n   163\u2192\\t}\\n   164\u2192\\n   165\u2192\\tserver, err := plugin.NewServer(logger, socketPath, manager, manager)\\n   166\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   167\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to create plugin server: %v\\\", err)\\n   168\u2192\\t}\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\tmanager.server = server\\n   171\u2192\\tmanager.checkpointdir, _ = filepath.Split(server.SocketPath())\\n   172\u2192\\n   173\u2192\\t// The following structures are populated with real implementations in manager.Start()\\n   174\u2192\\t// Before that, initializes them to perform no-op operations.\\n   175\u2192\\tmanager.activePods = func() []*v1.Pod { return []*v1.Pod{} }\\n   176\u2192\\tmanager.sourcesReady = &sourcesReadyStub{}\\n   177\u2192\\tcheckpointManager, err := checkpointmanager.NewCheckpointManager(manager.checkpointdir)\\n   178\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   179\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to initialize checkpoint manager: %v\\\", err)\\n   180\u2192\\t}\\n   181\u2192\\tmanager.checkpointManager = checkpointManager\\n   182\u2192\\n   183\u2192\\treturn manager, nil\\n   184\u2192}\\n   185\u2192\\n   186\u2192func (m *ManagerImpl) Updates() <-chan resourceupdates.Update {\\n   187\u2192\\treturn m.update\\n   188\u2192}\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192// CleanupPluginDirectory is to remove all existing unix sockets\\n   191\u2192// from /var/lib/kubelet/device-plugins on Device Plugin Manager start\\n   192\u2192func (m *ManagerImpl) CleanupPluginDirectory(logger klog.Logger, dir string) error {\\n   193\u2192\\td, err := os.Open(dir)\\n   194\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   195\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   196\u2192\\t}\\n   197\u2192\\tdefer d.Close()\\n   198\u2192\\tnames, err := d.Readdirnames(-1)\\n   199\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   200\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"38d0fe79-18a2-471d-befe-e7a57594034f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:17.682Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"5c8ad6d9-6278-426c-b510-754d651fe09b\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"38d0fe79-18a2-471d-befe-e7a57594034f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0132J1p4ooYLLVLkXnzFrv47\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01HvAq17WgpBdpiFMqYciupy\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/types.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":10311,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":65263,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":10311},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GqUg2W3T1RhuCLsLT\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"ba8f0b0d-9a70-43c7-8f68-dd1141ae1a47\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:17.911Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ba8f0b0d-9a70-43c7-8f68-dd1141ae1a47\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01HvAq17WgpBdpiFMqYciupy\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01HvAq17WgpBdpiFMqYciupy\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:17.914Z\",\"uuid\":\"bba7d360-d369-4b30-929a-f079fb994575\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ba8f0b0d-9a70-43c7     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package devicemanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/resourceupdates\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\tkubecontainer \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/container\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/pluginmanager/cache\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\tschedulerframework \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n    35\u2192)\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// Manager manages all the Device Plugins running on a node.\\n    38\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    39\u2192\\t// Start starts device plugin registration service.\\n    40\u2192\\tStart(logger klog.Logger, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap, initialContainerRunningSet sets.Set[string]) error\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192\\t// Allocate configures and assigns devices to a container in a pod. From\\n    43\u2192\\t// the requested device resources, Allocate will communicate with the\\n    44\u2192\\t// owning device plugin to allow setup procedures to take place, and for\\n    45\u2192\\t// the device plugin to provide runtime settings to use the device\\n    46\u2192\\t// (environment variables, mount points and device files).\\n    47\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192\\t// UpdatePluginResources updates node resources based on devices already\\n    50\u2192\\t// allocated to pods. The node object is provided for the device manager to\\n    51\u2192\\t// update the node capacity to reflect the currently available devices.\\n    52\u2192\\tUpdatePluginResources(node *schedulerframework.NodeInfo, attrs *lifecycle.PodAdmitAttributes) error\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192\\t// Stop stops the manager.\\n    55\u2192\\tStop(logger klog.Logger) error\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192\\t// GetDeviceRunContainerOptions checks whether we have cached containerDevices\\n    58\u2192\\t// for the passed-in <pod, container> and returns its DeviceRunContainerOptions\\n    59\u2192\\t// for the found one. An empty struct is returned in case no cached state is found.\\n    60\u2192\\tGetDeviceRunContainerOptions(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) (*DeviceRunContainerOptions, error)\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\t// GetCapacity returns the amount of available device plugin resource capacity, resource allocatable\\n    63\u2192\\t// and inactive device plugin resources previously registered on the node.\\n    64\u2192\\tGetCapacity() (v1.ResourceList, v1.ResourceList, []string)\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192\\t// GetWatcherHandler returns the plugin handler for the device manager.\\n    67\u2192\\tGetWatcherHandler() cache.PluginHandler\\n    68\u2192\\tGetHealthChecker() healthz.HealthChecker\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\t// GetDevices returns information about the devices assigned to pods and containers\\n    71\u2192\\tGetDevices(podUID, containerName string) ResourceDeviceInstances\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192\\t// UpdateAllocatedResourcesStatus updates the status of allocated resources for the pod.\\n    74\u2192\\tUpdateAllocatedResourcesStatus(pod *v1.Pod, status *v1.PodStatus)\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableDevices returns information about all the devices known to the manager\\n    77\u2192\\tGetAllocatableDevices() ResourceDeviceInstances\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// ShouldResetExtendedResourceCapacity returns whether the extended resources should be reset or not,\\n    80\u2192\\t// depending on the checkpoint file availability. Absence of the checkpoint file strongly indicates\\n    81\u2192\\t// the node has been recreated.\\n    82\u2192\\tShouldResetExtendedResourceCapacity() bool\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192\\t// TopologyManager HintProvider provider indicates the Device Manager implements the Topology Manager Interface\\n    85\u2192\\t// and is consulted to make Topology aware resource alignments\\n    86\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\t// TopologyManager HintProvider provider indicates the Device Manager implements the Topology Manager Interface\\n    89\u2192\\t// and is consulted to make Topology aware resource alignments per Pod\\n    90\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192\\t// UpdateAllocatedDevices frees any Devices that are bound to terminated pods.\\n    93\u2192\\tUpdateAllocatedDevices()\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192\\t// Updates returns a channel that receives an Update when the device changed its status.\\n    96\u2192\\tUpdates() <-chan resourceupdates.Update\\n    97\u2192}\\n    98\u2192\\n    99\u2192// DeviceRunContainerOptions contains the combined container runtime settings to consume its allocated devices.\\n   100\u2192type DeviceRunContainerOptions struct {\\n   101\u2192\\t// The environment variables list.\\n   102\u2192\\tEnvs []kubecontainer.EnvVar\\n   103\u2192\\t// The mounts for the container.\\n   104\u2192\\tMounts []kubecontainer.Mount\\n   105\u2192\\t// The host devices mapped into the container.\\n   106\u2192\\tDevices []kubecontainer.DeviceInfo\\n   107\u2192\\t// The Annotations for the container\\n   108\u2192\\tAnnotations []kubecontainer.Annotation\\n   109\u2192\\t// CDI Devices for the container\\n   110\u2192\\tCDIDevices []kubecontainer.CDIDevice\\n   111\u2192}\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192// TODO: evaluate whether we need this error definition.\\n   114\u2192const (\\n   115\u2192\\terrEndpointStopped = \\\"endpoint %v has been stopped\\\"\\n   116\u2192)\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192// endpointStopGracePeriod indicates the grace period after an endpoint is stopped\\n   119\u2192// because its device plugin fails. DeviceManager keeps the stopped endpoint in its\\n   120\u2192// cache during this grace period to cover the time gap for the capacity change to\\n   121\u2192// take effect.\\n   122\u2192const endpointStopGracePeriod = time.Duration(5) * time.Minute\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192// kubeletDeviceManagerCheckpoint is the file name of device plugin checkpoint\\n   125\u2192const kubeletDeviceManagerCheckpoint = \\\"kubelet_internal_checkpoint\\\"\\n   126\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"0a9fa2e5-e6a4-4cdb-894b-4cbb8e391a78\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:17.914Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"ba8f0b0d-9a70-43c7-8f68-dd1141ae1a47\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0a9fa2e5-e6a4-4cdb-894b-4cbb8e391a78\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0132J1p4ooYLLVLkXnzFrv47\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_011Gd8SbFSMQXEcWFpZK2xv1\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/pod_devices.go\",\"limit\":150},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":10311,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":65263,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":10311},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GqUg2W3T1RhuCLsLT\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"74578ea4-d9c9-4b7b-9119-c740e8e99fd0\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:18.241Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"74578ea4-d9c9-4b7b-9119-c740e8e99fd0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_011Gd8SbFSMQXEcWFpZK2xv1\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_011Gd8SbFSMQXEcWFpZK2xv1\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:18.243Z\",\"uuid\":\"6c652606-cb5d-4be3-94eb-0bc48ebd7f1c\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"74578ea4-d9c9-4b7b-9119-c740e8e99fd0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_011Gd8SbFSMQXEcWFpZK2xv1\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package devicemanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"maps\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"google.golang.org/protobuf/proto\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tpluginapi \\\"k8s.io/kubelet/pkg/apis/deviceplugin/v1beta1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/checkpoint\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\tkubecontainer \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/container\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192type deviceAllocateInfo struct {\\n    33\u2192\\t// deviceIds contains device Ids allocated to this container for the given resourceName.\\n    34\u2192\\tdeviceIds checkpoint.DevicesPerNUMA\\n    35\u2192\\t// allocResp contains cached rpc AllocateResponse.\\n    36\u2192\\tallocResp *pluginapi.ContainerAllocateResponse\\n    37\u2192}\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192type resourceAllocateInfo map[string]deviceAllocateInfo // Keyed by resourceName.\\n    40\u2192type containerDevices map[string]resourceAllocateInfo   // Keyed by containerName.\\n    41\u2192type podDevices struct {\\n    42\u2192\\tsync.RWMutex\\n    43\u2192\\tdevs map[string]containerDevices // Keyed by podUID.\\n    44\u2192}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192// NewPodDevices is a function that returns object of podDevices type with its own guard\\n    47\u2192// RWMutex and a map where key is a pod UID and value contains\\n    48\u2192// container devices information of type containerDevices.\\n    49\u2192func newPodDevices() *podDevices {\\n    50\u2192\\treturn &podDevices{devs: make(map[string]containerDevices)}\\n    51\u2192}\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) pods() sets.Set[string] {\\n    54\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n    55\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n    56\u2192\\tret := sets.New[string]()\\n    57\u2192\\tfor k := range pdev.devs {\\n    58\u2192\\t\\tret.Insert(k)\\n    59\u2192\\t}\\n    60\u2192\\treturn ret\\n    61\u2192}\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) size() int {\\n    64\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n    65\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n    66\u2192\\treturn len(pdev.devs)\\n    67\u2192}\\n    68\u2192\\n    69\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) hasPod(podUID string) bool {\\n    70\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n    71\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n    72\u2192\\t_, podExists := pdev.devs[podUID]\\n    73\u2192\\treturn podExists\\n    74\u2192}\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) insert(podUID, contName, resource string, devices checkpoint.DevicesPerNUMA, resp *pluginapi.ContainerAllocateResponse) {\\n    77\u2192\\tpdev.Lock()\\n    78\u2192\\tdefer pdev.Unlock()\\n    79\u2192\\tif _, podExists := pdev.devs[podUID]; !podExists {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\tpdev.devs[podUID] = make(containerDevices)\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\tif _, contExists := pdev.devs[podUID][contName]; !contExists {\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tpdev.devs[podUID][contName] = make(resourceAllocateInfo)\\n    84\u2192\\t}\\n    85\u2192\\tpdev.devs[podUID][contName][resource] = deviceAllocateInfo{\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tdeviceIds: devices,\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tallocResp: resp,\\n    88\u2192\\t}\\n    89\u2192}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) delete(pods []string) {\\n    92\u2192\\tpdev.Lock()\\n    93\u2192\\tdefer pdev.Unlock()\\n    94\u2192\\tfor _, uid := range pods {\\n    95\u2192\\t\\tdelete(pdev.devs, uid)\\n    96\u2192\\t}\\n    97\u2192}\\n    98\u2192\\n    99\u2192// Returns list of device Ids allocated to the given pod for the given resource.\\n   100\u2192// Returns nil if we don't have cached state for the given <podUID, resource>.\\n   101\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) podDevices(podUID, resource string) sets.Set[string] {\\n   102\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n   103\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\tret := sets.New[string]()\\n   106\u2192\\tfor contName := range pdev.devs[podUID] {\\n   107\u2192\\t\\tret = ret.Union(pdev.containerDevices(podUID, contName, resource))\\n   108\u2192\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\treturn ret\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// Returns list of device Ids allocated to the given container for the given resource.\\n   113\u2192// Returns nil if we don't have cached state for the given <podUID, contName, resource>.\\n   114\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) containerDevices(podUID, contName, resource string) sets.Set[string] {\\n   115\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n   116\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n   117\u2192\\tif _, podExists := pdev.devs[podUID]; !podExists {\\n   118\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   119\u2192\\t}\\n   120\u2192\\tif _, contExists := pdev.devs[podUID][contName]; !contExists {\\n   121\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   122\u2192\\t}\\n   123\u2192\\tdevs, resourceExists := pdev.devs[podUID][contName][resource]\\n   124\u2192\\tif !resourceExists {\\n   125\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   126\u2192\\t}\\n   127\u2192\\treturn devs.deviceIds.Devices()\\n   128\u2192}\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192// Populates allocatedResources with the device resources allocated to the specified <podUID, contName>.\\n   131\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) addContainerAllocatedResources(podUID, contName string, allocatedResources map[string]sets.Set[string]) {\\n   132\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n   133\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n   134\u2192\\tcontainers, exists := pdev.devs[podUID]\\n   135\u2192\\tif !exists {\\n   136\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   137\u2192\\t}\\n   138\u2192\\tresources, exists := containers[contName]\\n   139\u2192\\tif !exists {\\n   140\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   141\u2192\\t}\\n   142\u2192\\tfor resource, devices := range resources {\\n   143\u2192\\t\\tallocatedResources[resource] = allocatedResources[resource].Union(devices.deviceIds.Devices())\\n   144\u2192\\t}\\n   145\u2192}\\n   146\u2192\\n   147\u2192// Removes the device resources allocated to the specified <podUID, contName> from allocatedResources.\\n   148\u2192func (pdev *podDevices) removeContainerAllocatedResources(podUID, contName string, allocatedResources map[string]sets.Set[string]) {\\n   149\u2192\\tpdev.RLock()\\n   150\u2192\\tdefer pdev.RUnlock()\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package topologymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/admission\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192const (\\n    31\u2192\\t// containerTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope per container.\\n    32\u2192\\tcontainerTopologyScope = \\\"container\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\t// podTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope per pod.\\n    34\u2192\\tpodTopologyScope = \\\"pod\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t// noneTopologyScope specifies the TopologyManagerScope when topologyPolicyName is none.\\n    36\u2192\\tnoneTopologyScope = \\\"none\\\"\\n    37\u2192)\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192type podTopologyHints map[string]map[string]TopologyHint\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192// Scope interface for Topology Manager\\n    42\u2192type Scope interface {\\n    43\u2192\\tName() string\\n    44\u2192\\tGetPolicy() Policy\\n    45\u2192\\tAdmit(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult\\n    46\u2192\\t// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider\\n    47\u2192\\t// wants to be consoluted with when making topology hints\\n    48\u2192\\tAddHintProvider(h HintProvider)\\n    49\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking\\n    50\u2192\\tAddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    51\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking\\n    52\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(containerID string) error\\n    53\u2192\\t// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints\\n    54\u2192\\tStore\\n    55\u2192}\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192type scope struct {\\n    58\u2192\\tmutex sync.Mutex\\n    59\u2192\\tname  string\\n    60\u2192\\t// Mapping of a Pods mapping of Containers and their TopologyHints\\n    61\u2192\\t// Indexed by PodUID to ContainerName\\n    62\u2192\\tpodTopologyHints podTopologyHints\\n    63\u2192\\t// The list of components registered with the Manager\\n    64\u2192\\thintProviders []HintProvider\\n    65\u2192\\t// Topology Manager Policy\\n    66\u2192\\tpolicy Policy\\n    67\u2192\\t// Mapping of (PodUid, ContainerName) to ContainerID for Adding/Removing Pods from PodTopologyHints mapping\\n    68\u2192\\tpodMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n    69\u2192}\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192func (s *scope) Name() string {\\n    72\u2192\\treturn s.name\\n    73\u2192}\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192func (s *scope) getTopologyHints(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {\\n    76\u2192\\ts.mutex.Lock()\\n    77\u2192\\tdefer s.mutex.Unlock()\\n    78\u2192\\treturn s.podTopologyHints[podUID][containerName]\\n    79\u2192}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192func (s *scope) setTopologyHints(podUID string, containerName string, th TopologyHint) {\\n    82\u2192\\ts.mutex.Lock()\\n    83\u2192\\tdefer s.mutex.Unlock()\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\tif s.podTopologyHints[podUID] == nil {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\ts.podTopologyHints[podUID] = make(map[string]TopologyHint)\\n    87\u2192\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\ts.podTopologyHints[podUID][containerName] = th\\n    89\u2192}\\n    90\u2192\\n    91\u2192func (s *scope) GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {\\n    92\u2192\\treturn s.getTopologyHints(podUID, containerName)\\n    93\u2192}\\n    94\u2192\\n    95\u2192func (s *scope) GetPolicy() Policy {\\n    96\u2192\\treturn s.policy\\n    97\u2192}\\n    98\u2192\\n    99\u2192func (s *scope) AddHintProvider(h HintProvider) {\\n   100\u2192\\ts.hintProviders = append(s.hintProviders, h)\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. d\":\"8d22bc1e-ea37-4b89-ad6e-2323d4bf1139\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01CEibXht72pti1PujzcE9aD\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package topologymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/admission\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    28\u2192)\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192type containerScope struct {\\n    31\u2192\\tscope\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// Ensure containerScope implements Scope interface\\n    35\u2192var _ Scope = &containerScope{}\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// NewContainerScope returns a container scope.\\n    38\u2192func NewContainerScope(policy Policy) Scope {\\n    39\u2192\\treturn &containerScope{\\n    40\u2192\\t\\tscope{\\n    41\u2192\\t\\t\\tname:             containerTopologyScope,\\n    42\u2192\\t\\t\\tpodTopologyHints: podTopologyHints{},\\n    43\u2192\\t\\t\\tpolicy:           policy,\\n    44\u2192\\t\\t\\tpodMap:           containermap.NewContainerMap(),\\n    45\u2192\\t\\t},\\n    46\u2192\\t}\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (s *containerScope) Admit(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult {\\n    50\u2192\\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192\\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {\\n    53\u2192\\t\\tbestHint, admit := s.calculateAffinity(logger, pod, &container)\\n    54\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Best TopologyHint\\\", \\\"bestHint\\\", bestHint, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192\\t\\tif !admit {\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t\\tif IsAlignmentGuaranteed(s.policy) {\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResourcesFailure.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Inc()\\n    59\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t\\tmetrics.TopologyManagerAdmissionErrorsTotal.Inc()\\n    61\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn admission.GetPodAdmitResult(&TopologyAffinityError{})\\n    62\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    63\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Topology Affinity\\\", \\\"bestHint\\\", bestHint, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n    64\u2192\\t\\ts.setTopologyHints(string(pod.UID), container.Name, bestHint)\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192\\t\\terr := s.allocateAlignedResources(pod, &container)\\n    67\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n    68\u2192\\t\\t\\tmetrics.TopologyManagerAdmissionErrorsTotal.Inc()\\n    69\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn admission.GetPodAdmitResult(err)\\n    70\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tif IsAlignmentGuaranteed(s.policy) {\\n    73\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Resource alignment at container scope guaranteed\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod))\\n    74\u2192\\t\\t\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Inc()\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    76\u2192\\t}\\n    77\u2192\\treturn admission.GetPodAdmitResult(nil)\\n    78\u2192}\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192func (s *containerScope) accumulateProvidersHints(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) []map[string][]TopologyHint {\\n    81\u2192\\tvar providersHints []map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\tfor _, provider := range s.hintProviders {\\n    84\u2192\\t\\t// Get the TopologyHints for a Container from a provider.\\n    85\u2192\\t\\thints := provider.GetTopologyHints(pod, container)\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tprovidersHints = append(providersHints, hints)\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"TopologyHints\\\", \\\"hints\\\", hints, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n    88\u2192\\t}\\n    89\u2192\\treturn providersHints\\n    90\u2192}\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192func (s *containerScope) calculateAffinity(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) (TopologyHint, bool) {\\n    93\u2192\\tprovidersHints := s.accumulateProvidersHints(logger, pod, container)\\n    94\u2192\\tbestHint, admit := s.policy.Merge(logger, providersHints)\\n    95\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"ContainerTopologyHint\\\", \\\"bestHint\\\", bestHint, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n    96\u2192\\treturn bestHint, admit\\n    97\u2192}\\n    98\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"9ef06343-8b0a-4c4c-a7ec-6085db9ee7a9\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:21.191Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"8d22bc1e-ea37-4b89-ad6e-2323d4bf1139\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9ef06343-8b0a-4c4c-a7ec-6085db9ee7a9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01Crqk6BiE1KrH93v28fpHqg\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01SS32C99VdfHXdVxhbz1zD7\",\"name\":\"Grep\",\"input\":{\"pattern\":\"type (\\n/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/container_manager.go-51-\\t// Warning message for the users still using cgroup v1\\npkg/kubelet/cm/container_manager.go-52-\\tCgroupV1DeprecatedWarning = \\\"cgroup v1 5.8.\\npkg/kubelet/cm/container_manager.go-56-\\t/ (ref: \\\"k8s.io/kubelet/pkg/apis/podresources/v1\\\"\\n    50\u2192\\tkubefeatures \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    51\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cadvisor\\\"\\n    52\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager\\\"\\n    53\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager\\\"\\n    54\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/dra\\\"\\n    55\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager\\\"\\n    56\u2192\\tmemorymanagerstate \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/state\\\"\\n    57\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/resourceupdates\\\"\\n    58\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    59\u2192\\tcmutil \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/util\\\"\\n    60\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    61\u2192\\tkubecontainer \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/container\\\"\\n    62\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/events\\\"\\n    63\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    64\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    65\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/pluginmanager/cache\\\"\\n    66\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/stats/pidlimit\\\"\\n    67\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/status\\\"\\n    68\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/util/swap\\\"\\n    69\u2192\\tschedulerframework \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/scheduler/framework\\\"\\n    70\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/util/oom\\\"\\n    71\u2192)\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192// A non-user container tracked by the Kubelet.\\n    74\u2192type systemContainer struct {\\n    75\u2192\\t// Absolute name of the container.\\n    76\u2192\\tname string\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192\\t// CPU limit in millicores.\\n    79\u2192\\tcpuMillicores int64\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192\\t// Function that ensures the state of the container.\\n    82\u2192\\t// m is the cgroup manager for the specified container.\\n    83\u2192\\tensureStateFunc func(m cgroups.Manager) error\\n    84\u2192\\n    85\u2192\\t// Manager for the cgroups of the external container.\\n    86\u2192\\tmanager cgroups.Manager\\n    87\u2192}\\n    88\u2192\\n    89\u2192func newSystemCgroups(containerName string) (*systemContainer, error) {\\n    90\u2192\\tmanager, err := createManager(containerName)\\n    91\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n    92\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n    93\u2192\\t}\\n    94\u2192\\treturn &systemContainer{\\n    95\u2192\\t\\tname:    containerName,\\n    96\u2192\\t\\tmanager: manager,\\n    97\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    98\u2192}\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192type containerManagerImpl struct {\\n   101\u2192\\tsync.RWMutex\\n   102\u2192\\tcadvisorInterface cadvisor.Interface\\n   103\u2192\\tmountUtil         mount.Interface\\n   104\u2192\\tNodeConfig\\n   105\u2192\\tstatus Status\\n   106\u2192\\t// External containers being managed.\\n   107\u2192\\tsystemContainers []*systemContainer\\n   108\u2192\\t// Tasks that are run periodically\\n   109\u2192\\tperiodicTasks []func()\\n   110\u2192\\t// Holds all the mounted cgroup subsystems\\n   111\u2192\\tsubsystems *CgroupSubsystems\\n   112\u2192\\tnodeInfo   *v1.Node\\n   113\u2192\\t// Interface for cgroup management\\n   114\u2192\\tcgroupManager CgroupManager\\n   115\u2192\\t// Capacity of this node.\\n   116\u2192\\tcapacity v1.ResourceList\\n   117\u2192\\t// Capacity of this node, including internal resources.\\n   118\u2192\\tinternalCapacity v1.ResourceList\\n   119\u2192\\t// Absolute cgroupfs path to a cgroup that Kubelet needs to place all pods under.\\n   120\u2192\\t// This path include a top level container for enforcing Node Allocatable.\\n   121\u2192\\tcgroupRoot CgroupName\\n   122\u2192\\t// Event recorder interface.\\n   123\u2192\\trecorder record.EventRecorder\\n   124\u2192\\t// Interface for QoS cgroup management\\n   125\u2192\\tqosContainerManager QOSContainerManager\\n   126\u2192\\t// Interface for exporting and allocating devices reported by device plugins.\\n   127\u2192\\tdeviceManager devicemanager.Manager\\n   128\u2192\\t// Interface for CPU affinity management.\\n   129\u2192\\tcpuManager cpumanager.Manager\\n   130\u2192\\t// Interface for memory affinity management.\\n   131\u2192\\tmemoryManager memorymanager.Manager\\n   132\u2192\\t// Interface for Topology resource co-ordination\\n   133\u2192\\ttopologyManager topologymanager.Manager\\n   134\u2192\\t// Implementation of Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA).\\n   135\u2192\\tdraManager *dra.Manager\\n   136\u2192\\t// kubeClient is the interface to the Kubernetes API server. May be nil if the kubelet is running in standalone mode.\\n   137\u2192\\tkubeClient clientset.Interface\\n   138\u2192\\t// resourceUpdates is a channel that provides resource updates.\\n   139\u2192\\tresourceUpdates chan resourceupdates.Update\\n   140\u2192}\\n   141\u2192\\n   142\u2192type features struct {\\n   143\u2192\\tcpuHardcapping bool\\n   144\u2192}\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192var _ ContainerManager = &containerManagerImpl{}\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192// checks if the required cgroups subsystems are mounted.\\n   149\u2192// As of now, only 'cpu' and 'memory' are required.\\n   150\u2192// cpu quota is a soft requirement.\\n   151\u2192func validateSystemRequirements(logger klog.Logger, mountUtil mount.Interface) (features, error) {\\n   152\u2192\\tconst (\\n   153\u2192\\t\\tcgroupMountType = \\\"cgroup\\\"\\n   154\u2192\\t\\tlocalErr        = \\\"system validation failed\\\"\\n   155\u2192\\t)\\n   156\u2192\\tvar (\\n   157\u2192\\t\\tcpuMountPoint string\\n   158\u2192\\t\\tf             features\\n   159\u2192\\t)\\n   160\u2192\\tmountPoints, err := mountUtil.List()\\n   161\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   162\u2192\\t\\treturn f, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s - %v\\\", localErr, err)\\n   163\u2192\\t}\\n   164\u2192\\n   165\u2192\\tif cgroups.IsCgroup2UnifiedMode() {\\n   166\u2192\\t\\tf.cpuHardcapping = true\\n   167\u2192\\t\\treturn f, nil\\n   168\u2192\\t}\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\texpectedCgroups := sets.New(\\\"cpu\\\", \\\"cpuacct\\\", \\\"cpuset\\\", \\\"memory\\\")\\n   171\u2192\\tfor _, mountPoint := range mountPoints {\\n   172\u2192\\t\\tif mountPoint.Type == cgroupMountType {\\n   173\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, opt := range mountPoint.Opts {\\n   174\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif expectedCgroups.Has(opt) {\\n   175\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\texpectedCgroups.Delete(opt)\\n   176\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   177\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif opt == \\\"cpu\\\" {\\n   178\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcpuMountPoint = mountPoint.Path\\n   179\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   180\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   181\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   182\u2192\\t}\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192\\tif expectedCgroups.Len() > 0 {\\n   185\u2192\\t\\treturn f, fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s - Following Cgroup subsystem not mounted: %v\\\", localErr, sets.List(expectedCgroups))\\n   186\u2192\\t}\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192\\t// Check if cpu quota is available.\\n   189\u2192\\t// CPU cgroup is required and so it expected to be mounted at this point.\\n   190\u2192\\tperiodExists, err := utilpath.Exists(utilpath.CheckFollowSymlink, path.Join(cpuMountPoint, \\\"cpu.cfs_period_us\\\"))\\n   191\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   192\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Failed to detect if CPU cgroup cpu.cfs_period_us is available\\\")\\n   193\u2192\\t}\\n   194\u2192\\tquotaExists, err := utilpath.Exists(utilpath.CheckFollowSymlink, path.Join(cpuMountPoint, \\\"cpu.cfs_quota_us\\\"))\\n   195\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   196\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Failed to detect if CPU cgroup cpu.cfs_quota_us is available\\\")\\n   197\u2192\\t}\\n   198\u2192\\tif quotaExists && periodExists {\\n   199\u2192\\t\\tf.cpuHardcapping = true\\n   200\u2192\\t}\\n   201\u2192\\treturn f, nil\\n   202\u2192}\\n   203\u2192\\n   204\u2192// TODO(vmarmol): Add limits to the system containers.\\n   205\u2192// Takes the absolute name of the specified containers.\\n   206\u2192// Empty container name disables use of the specified container.\\n   207\u2192func NewContainerManager(ctx context.Context, mountUtil mount.Interface, cadvisorInterface cadvisor.Interface, nodeConfig NodeConfig, failSwapOn bool, recorder record.EventRecorder, kubeClient clientset.Interface) (ContainerManager, error) {\\n   208\u2192\\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n   209\u2192\\n   210\u2192\\tsubsystems, err := GetCgroupSubsystems()\\n   211\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   212\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to get mounted cgroup subsystems: %v\\\", err)\\n   213\u2192\\t}\\n   214\u2192\\n   215\u2192\\tisSwapOn, err := swap.IsSwapOn()\\n   216\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   217\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to determine if swap is on: %w\\\", err)\\n   218\u2192\\t}\\n   219\u2192\\n   220\u2192\\tif isSwapOn {\\n   221\u2192\\t\\tif failSwapOn {\\n   222\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"running with swap on is not supported, please disable swap or set --fail-swap-on flag to false\\\")\\n   223\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   224\u2192\\n   225\u2192\\t\\tif !swap.IsTmpfsNoswapOptionSupported(mountUtil, nodeConfig.KubeletRootDir) {\\n   226\u2192\\t\\t\\tnodeRef := nodeRefFromNode(string(nodeConfig.NodeName))\\n   227\u2192\\t\\t\\trecorder.Event(nodeRef, v1.EventTypeWarning, events.PossibleMemoryBackedVolumesOnDisk,\\n   228\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The tmpfs noswap option is not supported. Memory-backed volumes (e.g. secrets, emptyDirs, etc.) \\\"+\\n   229\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"might be swapped to disk and should no longer be considered secure.\\\",\\n   230\u2192\\t\\t\\t)\\n   231\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   232\u2192\\t}\\n   233\u2192\\n   234\u2192\\tvar internalCapacity = v1.ResourceList{}\\n   235\u2192\\t// It is safe to invoke `MachineInfo` on cAdvisor before logically initializing cAdvisor here because\\n   236\u2192\\t// machine info is computed and cached once as part of cAdvisor object creation.\\n   237\u2192\\t// But `RootFsInfo` and `ImagesFsInfo` are not available at this moment so they will be called later during manager starts\\n   238\u2192\\tmachineInfo, err := cadvisorInterface.MachineInfo()\\n   239\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   240\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   241\u2192\\t}\\n   242\u2192\\tcapacity := cadvisor.CapacityFromMachineInfo(machineInfo)\\n   243\u2192\\tfor k, v := range capacity {\\n   244\u2192\\t\\tinternalCapacity[k] = v\\n   245\u2192\\t}\\n   246\u2192\\tpidlimits, err := pidlimit.Stats()\\n   247\u2192\\tif err == nil && pidlimits != nil && pidlimits.MaxPID != nil {\\n   248\u2192\\t\\tinternalCapacity[pidlimit.PIDs] = *resource.NewQuantity(\\n   249\u2192\\t\\t\\tint64(*pidlimits.MaxPID),\\n   250\u2192\\t\\t\\tresource.DecimalSI)\\n   251\u2192\\t}\\n   252\u2192\\n   253\u2192\\t// Turn CgroupRoot from a string (in cgroupfs path format) to internal CgroupName\\n   254\u2192\\tcgroupRoot := ParseCgroupfsToCgroupName(nodeConfig.CgroupRoot)\\n   255\u2192\\tcgroupManager := NewCgroupManager(logger, subsystems, nodeConfig.CgroupDriver)\\n   256\u2192\\tnodeConfig.CgroupVersion = cgroupManager.Version()\\n   257\u2192\\t// Check if Cgroup-root actually exists on the node\\n   258\u2192\\tif nodeConfig.CgroupsPerQOS {\\n   259\u2192\\t\\t// this does default to / when enabled, but this tests against regressions.\\n   260\u2192\\t\\tif nodeConfig.CgroupRoot == \\\"\\\" {\\n   261\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid configuration: cgroups-per-qos was specified and cgroup-root was not specified. To enable the QoS cgroup hierarchy you need to specify a valid cgroup-root\\\")\\n   262\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   263\u2192\\n   264\u2192\\t\\t// we need to check that the cgroup root actually exists for each subsystem\\n   265\u2192\\t\\t// of note, we always use the cgroupfs driver when performing this check since\\n   266\u2192\\t\\t// the input is provided in that format.\\n   267\u2192\\t\\t// this is important because we do not want any name conversion to occur.\\n   268\u2192\\t\\tif err := cgroupManager.Validate(cgroupRoot); err != nil {\\n   269\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"invalid configuration: %w\\\", err)\\n   270\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   271\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Container manager verified user specified cgroup-root exists\\\", \\\"cgroupRoot\\\", cgroupRoot)\\n   272\u2192\\t\\t// Include the top level cgroup for enforcing node allocatable into cgroup-root.\\n   273\u2192\\t\\t// This way, all sub modules can avoid having to understand the concept of node allocatable.\\n   274\u2192\\t\\tcgroupRoot = NewCgroupName(cgroupRoot, defaultNodeAllocatableCgroupName)\\n   275\u2192\\t}\\n   276\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Creating Container Manager object based on Node Config\\\", \\\"nodeConfig\\\", nodeConfig)\\n   277\u2192\\n   278\u2192\\tqosContainerManager, err := NewQOSContainerManager(subsystems, cgroupRoot, nodeConfig, cgroupManager)\\n   279\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   280\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   281\u2192\\t}\\n   282\u2192\\n   283\u2192\\tcm := &containerManagerImpl{\\n   284\u2192\\t\\tcadvisorInterface:   cadvisorInterface,\\n   285\u2192\\t\\tmountUtil:           mountUtil,\\n   286\u2192\\t\\tNodeConfig:          nodeConfig,\\n   287\u2192\\t\\tsubsystems:          subsystems,\\n   288\u2192\\t\\tcgroupManager:       cgroupManager,\\n   289\u2192\\t\\tcapacity:            capacity,\\n   290\u2192\\t\\tinternalCapacity:    internalCapacity,\\n   291\u2192\\t\\tcgroupRoot:          cgroupRoot,\\n   292\u2192\\t\\trecorder:            recorder,\\n   293\u2192\\t\\tqosContainerManager: qosContainerManager,\\n   294\u2192\\t}\\n   295\u2192\\n   296\u2192\\tcm.topologyManager, err = topologymanager.NewManager(\\n   297\u2192\\t\\tmachineInfo.Topology,\\n   298\u2192\\t\\tnodeConfig.TopologyManagerPolicy,\\n   299\u2192\\t\\tnodeConfig.TopologyManagerScope,\\n   300\u2192\\t\\tnodeConfig.TopologyManagerPolicyOptions,\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"17e2982e-9513-4b0a-b237-668aca8fffec\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:23.382Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"2fa7bedb-918a-40a5-bfa5-e38bcbf7b592\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"17e2982e-9513-4b0a-b237-668aca8fffec\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01U5NcHMX4EmSXaayAYkSJDb\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the detailed analysis for the user.\\n\\n## Comprehensive Analysis of Kubernetes Kubelet Container Manager Subsystems\\n\\nBased on my detailed exploration of the codebase, here's an in-depth understanding of the CPU Manager, Memory Manager, Topology Manager, and Device Manager subsystems:\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 1. CPU MANAGER\\n\\n**File Paths Examined:**\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/cpu_manager.go` - Main manager\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/policy.go` - Policy interface\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/policy_none.go` - None policy\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/policy_static.go` - Static policy (1,123 lines)\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/cpu_assignment.go` - CPU allocation algorithm\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state/state.go` - State interface\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state/state_checkpoint.go` - Checkpoint state\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state/state_mem.go` - In-memory state\\n\\n**Key Interfaces & Types:**\\n\\n```go\\n// Manager interface\\ntype Manager interface {\\n    Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error\\n    Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    AddContainer(logger logr.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    RemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    State() state.Reader\\n    GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    GetExclusiveCPUs(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    GetCPUAffinity(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n}\\n\\n// Policy interface\\ntype Policy interface {\\n    Name() string\\n    Start(logger logr.Logger, s state.State) error\\n    Allocate(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    RemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, podUID string, containerName string) error\\n    GetTopologyHints(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    GetAllocatableCPUs(m state.State) cpuset.CPUSet\\n}\\n\\n// State interface\\ntype State interface {\\n    Reader\\n    writer\\n    // Reader provides GetCPUSet, GetDefaultCPUSet, GetCPUSetOrDefault, GetCPUAssignments\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Policies:**\\n\\n1. **None Policy**: Allocates CPUs to shared pool, no exclusive allocation\\n2. **Static Policy**: Exclusively pins guaranteed QoS pods with integral CPU requests\\n   - Maintains SHARED pool (default CPUSet) for burstable/best-effort\\n   - Maintains RESERVED pool (system reserved CPUs)\\n   - Maintains ASSIGNABLE pool (SHARED - RESERVED)\\n   - Maintains EXCLUSIVE ALLOCATIONS per container\\n\\n**CPU Affinity Assignment:**\\n\\nThe static policy uses sophisticated topology-aware allocation (`cpu_assignment.go`):\\n- **`cpuAccumulator`**: Core state machine for CPU allocation\\n- **Allocation strategies**:\\n  1. Take full NUMA nodes if available\\n  2. Take full sockets if available\\n  3. Take full uncore caches (if PreferAlignByUncoreCache option enabled)\\n  4. Take full cores\\n  5. Take remaining individual CPUs\\n  \\n- **CPU Sorting Strategies**:\\n  - **Packed**: Group by core before moving to next core (default)\\n  - **Spread**: Spread CPUs across cores\\n  \\n- **NUMA Distribution**:\\n  - **Packed**: Fills NUMA nodes before moving to next\\n  - **Distributed**: Evenly distributes CPUs across NUMA nodes (KEP-2902)\\n\\n**Reconciliation Loop:**\\n\\nThe `reconcileState()` method runs periodically (default every 200ms):\\n- Iterates through active pods\\n- For each container, checks if CPU affinity has changed in state vs. last update\\n- Calls `UpdateContainerResources()` on container runtime if needed\\n- Handles stale state removal when sources become ready\\n\\n**State Management:**\\n\\n- **Checkpoint-based**: Persists to `/var/lib/kubelet/cpu_manager_state`\\n- **In-memory fallback**: For quick access during runtime\\n- **Idempotent operations**: Allocate and RemoveContainer are idempotent\\n\\n**Notable Design Decisions:**\\n\\n1. CPU manager cannot handle runtime topology changes\\n2. Reconciliation period covers temporary CPU sharing during updates\\n3. Reserved CPUs must be > 0 for static policy (prevents shared pool exhaustion)\\n4. Fractional CPUs never exclusively allocated\\n5. Init container CPU reuse support\\n6. Strict SMT alignment option (FullPhysicalCPUsOnly)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2. MEMORY MANAGER\\n\\n**File Paths Examined:**\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/memory_manager.go` - Main manager\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/policy.go` - Policy interface\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/policy_static.go` - Static policy\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/policy_best_effort.go` - Best effort\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/state/state.go` - State interface\\n\\n**Key Interfaces & Types:**\\n\\n```go\\n// Manager interface\\ntype Manager interface {\\n    Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error\\n    AddContainer(logger klog.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    RemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    State() state.Reader\\n    GetTopologyHints(*v1.Pod, *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    GetPodTopologyHints(*v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    GetMemoryNUMANodes(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) sets.Set[int]\\n    GetAllocatableMemory() []state.Block\\n    GetMemory(podUID, containerName string) []state.Block\\n}\\n\\n// Policy interface\\ntype Policy interface {\\n    Name() string\\n    Start(logger klog.Logger, s state.State) error\\n    Allocate(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    RemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, podUID string, containerName string)\\n    GetTopologyHints(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    GetAllocatableMemory(s state.State) []state.Block\\n}\\n\\n// State: stores ContainerMemoryAssignments per pod/container\\n// state.Block: represents a memory block pinned to NUMA nodes with specified size\\n```\\n\\n**Memory Policies:**\\n\\n1. **None**: No memory management\\n2. **Static**: \\n   - Allocates memory to guaranteed QoS pods from specific NUMA nodes\\n   - Checkpoint-based persistence\\n   - NUMA awareness\\n3. **Best Effort** (Windows only):\\n   - Allocates memory with best effort alignment\\n\\n**Memory Allocation:**\\n\\n- Allocates memory blocks pinned to NUMA nodes\\n- Tracks per-NUMA-node allocatable memory\\n- Supports hugepages and regular memory\\n- Handles init container memory cleanup (non-restartable only)\\n\\n**Notable Design Decisions:**\\n\\n1. Platform-specific: Static policy unavailable on Windows\\n2. Memory reuse from init containers to app containers\\n3. Restartable init containers not removed from state\\n4. Checkpoint file stores policy name for validation\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3. TOPOLOGY MANAGER\\n\\n**File Paths Examined:**\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/topology_manager.go` - Main manager\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/policy.go` - Policy interface & merging\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/scope.go` - Scope interface\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/scope_container.go` - Container scope\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/scope_pod.go` - Pod scope\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/numa_info.go` - NUMA topology info\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/bitmask/bitmask.go` - NUMA bitmask\\n\\n**Key Interfaces & Types:**\\n\\n```go\\n// Manager interface\\ntype Manager interface {\\n    lifecycle.PodAdmitHandler\\n    AddHintProvider(logger klog.Logger, h HintProvider)\\n    AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    RemoveContainer(containerID string) error\\n    Store\\n}\\n\\n// HintProvider interface - implemented by CPU/Memory/Device managers\\ntype HintProvider interface {\\n    GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    GetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n}\\n\\n// Store interface\\ntype Store interface {\\n    GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint\\n    GetPolicy() Policy\\n}\\n\\n// Policy interface\\ntype Policy interface {\\n    Name() string\\n    Merge(logger klog.Logger, providersHints []map[string][]TopologyHint) (TopologyHint, bool)\\n}\\n\\n// TopologyHint\\ntype TopologyHint struct {\\n    NUMANodeAffinity bitmask.BitMask  // Which NUMA nodes this allocation fits\\n    Preferred bool                    // Is this a preferred hint?\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Topology Policies:**\\n\\n1. **None**: No topology management (no scope needed)\\n2. **Best Effort**: Best effort alignment to minimize cross-NUMA allocations\\n3. **Restricted**: Stricter alignment requirements\\n4. **SingleNumaNode**: All resources must come from single NUMA node\\n\\n**Scopes:**\\n\\n1. **Container Scope**: Topology hints per container\\n2. **Pod Scope**: Topology hints per pod (all containers must align)\\n\\n**Hint Merging Algorithm:**\\n\\nThe `HintMerger` merges hints from all providers:\\n\\n1. **Collect hints** from all providers for each resource\\n2. **Filter hints** - handle nil/empty hint cases\\n3. **Iterate all permutations** of hints across providers\\n4. **Merge permutations** using bitwise-AND of NUMA affinity masks\\n5. **Compare candidates** based on policy:\\n   - Preferred hints > non-preferred\\n   - Narrower NUMA affinities > wider (fewer NUMA nodes = better)\\n   - Handle non-preferred hints with balancing logic\\n6. **Return best hint** or default all-NUMA hint if none preferred\\n\\n**Topology Manager Workflow:**\\n\\n1. During pod admission:\\n   - Call `GetTopologyHints()` on all registered providers\\n   - Collect hints per resource\\n   - Merge hints per policy\\n   - Call `Allocate()` on providers with final merged hint\\n2. Store topology affinity for future queries\\n3. Support pod-level vs. container-level alignment\\n\\n**Notable Design Decisions:**\\n\\n1. Max 8 NUMA nodes supported (state explosion prevention)\\n2. Hint providers called during pod admission (not allocation)\\n3. Allocation happens AFTER hints are merged and stored\\n4. Providers query Store to get assigned affinity for actual allocation\\n5. Scope implementations handle container vs. pod-level logic\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4. DEVICE MANAGER\\n\\n**File Paths Examined:**\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/manager.go` - Main manager (200+ lines shown)\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/types.go` - Manager interface\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/pod_devices.go` - Pod device tracking\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/topology_hints.go` - Topology integration\\n- `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/plugin/v1beta1/` - Device plugin API\\n\\n**Key Types:**\\n\\n```go\\n// Manager interface\\ntype Manager interface {\\n    Start(logger klog.Logger, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap, initialContainerRunningSet sets.Set[string]) error\\n    Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    UpdatePluginResources(node *schedulerframework.NodeInfo, attrs *lifecycle.PodAdmitAttributes) error\\n    Stop(logger klog.Logger) error\\n    GetDeviceRunContainerOptions(ctx context.Context, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) (*DeviceRunContainerOptions, error)\\n    GetCapacity() (v1.ResourceList, v1.ResourceList, []string)\\n    GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    GetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    UpdateAllocatedDevices()\\n    Updates() <-chan resourceupdates.Update\\n}\\n\\n// ManagerImpl struct\\ntype ManagerImpl struct {\\n    endpoints map[string]endpointInfo               // Key: ResourceName\\n    allDevices ResourceDeviceInstances              // All known devices\\n    healthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]     // Healthy devices by resource\\n    unhealthyDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]   // Unhealthy devices by resource\\n    allocatedDevices map[string]sets.Set[string]   // Allocated devices by resource\\n    podDevices *podDevices                          // Pod -> device mapping\\n    numaNodes []int                                 // NUMA topology\\n    topologyAffinityStore topologymanager.Store     // Topology manager reference\\n    devicesToReuse PodReusableDevices               // Init container device reuse\\n}\\n\\n// Device allocation data\\ntype deviceAllocateInfo struct {\\n    deviceIds checkpoint.DevicesPerNUMA\\n    allocResp *pluginapi.ContainerAllocateResponse\\n}\\n\\ntype podDevices struct {\\n    sync.RWMutex\\n    devs map[string]containerDevices  // podUID -> containerName -> resourceName -> allocation\\n}\\n\\ntype DeviceRunContainerOptions struct {\\n    Envs []kubecontainer.EnvVar\\n    Mounts []kubecontainer.Mount\\n    Devices []kubecontainer.DeviceInfo\\n    Annotations []kubecontainer.Annotation\\n    CDIDevices []kubecontainer.CDIDevice\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Device Plugin API Integration:**\\n\\n- **v1beta1 Plugin API**: Device plugins register via Unix socket\\n- **Server Implementation**: `plugin.Server` manages lifecycle\\n- **Endpoints**: Track device plugin connections\\n- **Resource registration**: Plugins advertise available devices and capabilities\\n\\n**Device Allocation Workflow:**\\n\\n1. Devices register with device manager via plugin API\\n2. Manager maintains device health status\\n3. During pod allocation:\\n   - Query topology hints from plugins (if supported)\\n   - Allocate devices based on topology affinity\\n   - Call plugin's `Allocate()` RPC to get runtime config\\n   - Store result in checkpoint\\n   - Return env vars, mounts, device files to container runtime\\n4. Support device reuse from init containers\\n5. Handle device health updates via channel\\n\\n**Notable Design Decisions:**\\n\\n1. Devices tracked per NUMA node (DevicesPerNUMA)\\n2. Checkpoint persistence for device allocations\\n3. Active pod reconciliation to free stale device allocations\\n4. Init container device reuse support\\n5. Health status tracking for device plugin monitoring\\n6. Support for CDI (Container Device Interface) devices\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5. INTEGRATION WITH TOPOLOGY MANAGER\\n\\n**How Managers Interact Through Topology Manager:**\\n\\n1. **Registration Phase** (in ContainerManager.Start):\\n   - CPU Manager, Memory Manager, Device Manager all register as HintProviders\\n   - Each implements `GetTopologyHints()` and `GetPodTopologyHints()`\\n\\n2. **Pod Admission Phase**:\\n   ```\\n   Pod Admission Request\\n        \u2193\\n   TopologyManager.Admit()\\n        \u2193\\n   For each container/pod:\\n        \u2193\\n   Call GetTopologyHints() on CPU, Memory, Device managers\\n        \u2193\\n   Merge hints per policy (Best Effort/Restricted/SingleNumaNode)\\n        \u2193\\n   Store merged affinity in topology manager store\\n        \u2193\\n   Call Allocate() on each manager with access to stored affinity via Store.GetAffinity()\\n   ```\\n\\n3. **Affinity Query**:\\n   - Managers call `topologyAffinityStore.GetAffinity(podUID, containerName)`\\n   - Returns merged TopologyHint with agreed-upon NUMA nodes\\n   - Managers use this to make allocation decisions\\n\\n4. **Example Flow for Static CPU + Static Memory**:\\n   - CPU Manager generates hints for available CPUs per NUMA\\n   - Memory Manager generates hints for available memory per NUMA\\n   - Topology Manager merges to find intersection of NUMA nodes suitable for both\\n   - If intersection empty \u2192 pod admission fails\\n   - If intersection found \u2192 CPU and Memory allocate from agreed NUMA nodes\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 6. PARENT CONTAINER MANAGER INTEGRATION\\n\\n**File Path**: `/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/container_manager_linux.go` (lines 100-140)\\n\\n**ContainerManager Structure**:\\n```go\\ntype containerManagerImpl struct {\\n    deviceManager devicemanager.Manager      // Device allocation\\n    cpuManager cpumanager.Manager            // CPU affinity\\n    memoryManager memorymanager.Manager      // Memory allocation\\n    topologyManager topologymanager.Manager   // Topology coordination\\n    draManager *dra.Manager                  // Dynamic Resource Allocation\\n    // ... other components\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Integration Points**:\\n\\n1. **Initialization**:\\n   - Create topology manager first (line 296-300)\\n   - Pass topology manager Store to CPU/Memory/Device managers\\n   - Register each manager as HintProvider with topology manager\\n\\n2. **Pod Admission**:\\n   - Topology manager handles admission via PodAdmitHandler\\n   - CPU/Memory/Device managers consulted during this phase\\n\\n3. **Container Resource Updates**:\\n   - CPU manager reconciles periodically\\n   - Device manager updates on health changes\\n   - Memory manager persists allocations\\n\\n4. **Resource Cleanup**:\\n   - When containers removed, each manager cleans up state\\n   - Topology manager tracks container lifecycle\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 7. NOTABLE DESIGN PATTERNS\\n\\n1. **Policy Pattern**: CPU/Memory/Device managers use pluggable policies\\n2. **Store Pattern**: Topology Manager exposes Store interface for hint lookup\\n3. **HintProvider Pattern**: All resource managers implement consistent interface\\n4. **State Machine**: CPU accumulator uses sophisticated state machine for allocation\\n5. **Checkpoint Pattern**: Persistent state stored to survive node restarts\\n6. **Idempotent Operations**: Allocate/RemoveContainer called multiple times safely\\n7. **Stale State Cleanup**: All managers remove state for non-active pods\\n8. **Init Container Handling**: Reuse resources from init containers in app containers\\n9. **Reconciliation Loop**: CPU manager periodically updates container cgroups\\n10. **Health Monitoring**: Device manager tracks plugin health independently\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 8. KEY FILES SUMMARY\\n\\n| Component | Key Files |\\n|-----------|-----------|\\n| **CPU Manager** | `cpu_manager.go`, `policy_static.go`, `cpu_assignment.go`, `state/state.go` |\\n| **Memory Manager** | `memory_manager.go`, `policy_static.go`, `state/state.go` |\\n| **Topology Manager** | `topology_manager.go`, `policy.go`, `scope_container.go`, `scope_pod.go` |\\n| **Device Manager** | `manager.go`, `types.go`, `pod_devices.go`, `plugin/v1beta1/` |\\n| **Container Manager** | `container_manager_linux.go`, `container_manager.go` |\\n\\nThis architecture enables Kubernetes to coordinate CPU, memory, and device allocation while respecting NUMA topology constraints for optimal performance on multi-socket systems.\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":0,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":4759,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":88112,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":4759},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6HRFgS3xtnd3pip8r4\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"ec3c1014-6112-484e-9cfe-37689531b537\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:33:07.851Z\"}\n",

CPU Manager:\\n   - /workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/ directory structure\\n   - The Policy interface and implementations (none, static)\\n   - State management (checkpoint/in-memory)\\n   - How CPU affinity is assigned to containers\\n   - The reconcile loop\\n\\n2. Memory Manager:\\n   - /workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/ directory structure  \\n   - Memory policies and NUMA awareness\\n   - How memory is allocated\\n\\n3. Topology Manager:\\n   - /workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/ directory structure\\n   - How topology hints are gathered and used\\n   - The hint providers and how they interact\\n\\n4.
