16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cpumanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"math\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/go-logr/logr\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\truntimeapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/topology\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\tkubecontainer \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/container\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/status\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    41\u2192)\\n    42\u2192\\n    43\u2192// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of pods to reconcile.\\n    44\u2192type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192type runtimeService interface {\\n    47\u2192\\tUpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, id string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) error\\n    48\u2192}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192type policyName string\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192// cpuManagerStateFileName is the file name where cpu manager stores its state\\n    53\u2192const cpuManagerStateFileName = \\\"cpu_manager_state\\\"\\n    54\u2192\\n    55\u2192// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod cpus.\\n    56\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    57\u2192\\t// Start is called during Kubelet initialization.\\n    58\u2192\\t// Start takes a `Context` because it may possibly spin the reconcileState helper, which in turn\\n    59\u2192\\t// needs to update container state, which takes a context.\\n    60\u2192\\tStart(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\t// Called to trigger the allocation of CPUs to a container. This must be\\n    63\u2192\\t// called at some point prior to the AddContainer() call for a container,\\n    64\u2192\\t// e.g. at pod admission time.\\n    65\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds the mapping between container ID to pod UID and the container name\\n    68\u2192\\t// The mapping used to remove the CPU allocation during the container removal\\n    69\u2192\\tAddContainer(logger logr.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer is called after Kubelet decides to kill or delete a\\n    72\u2192\\t// container. After this call, the CPU manager stops trying to reconcile\\n    73\u2192\\t// that container and any CPUs dedicated to the container are freed.\\n    74\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192\\t// State returns a read-only interface to the internal CPU manager state.\\n    77\u2192\\tState() state.Reader\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    80\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    81\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    82\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192\\t// GetExclusiveCPUs implements the podresources.CPUsProvider interface to provide\\n    85\u2192\\t// exclusively allocated cpus for the container\\n    86\u2192\\tGetExclusiveCPUs(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    87\u2192\\n    88\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    89\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment per Pod\\n    90\u2192\\t// among this and other resource controllers.\\n    91\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableCPUs returns the total set of CPUs available for allocation.\\n    94\u2192\\tGetAllocatableCPUs() cpuset.CPUSet\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\t// GetCPUAffinity returns cpuset which includes cpus from shared pools\\n    97\u2192\\t// as well as exclusively allocated cpus\\n    98\u2192\\tGetCPUAffinity(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192\\t// GetAllCPUs returns all the CPUs known by cpumanager, as reported by the\\n   101\u2192\\t// hardware discovery. Maps to the CPU capacity.\\n   102\u2192\\tGetAllCPUs() cpuset.CPUSet\\n   103\u2192}\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192type manager struct {\\n   106\u2192\\tsync.Mutex\\n   107\u2192\\tpolicy Policy\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\t// reconcilePeriod is the duration between calls to reconcileState.\\n   110\u2192\\treconcilePeriod time.Duration\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192\\t// state allows pluggable CPU assignment policies while sharing a common\\n   113\u2192\\t// representation of state for the system to inspect and reconcile.\\n   114\u2192\\tstate state.State\\n   115\u2192\\n   116\u2192\\t// lastUpdatedstate holds state for each container from the last time it was updated.\\n   117\u2192\\tlastUpdateState state.State\\n   118\u2192\\n   119\u2192\\t// containerRuntime is the container runtime service interface needed\\n   120\u2192\\t// to make UpdateContainerResources() calls against the containers.\\n   121\u2192\\tcontainerRuntime runtimeService\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192\\t// activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node\\n   124\u2192\\t// so all the containers can be updated in the reconciliation loop.\\n   125\u2192\\tactivePods ActivePodsFunc\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192\\t// podStatusProvider provides a method for obtaining pod statuses\\n   128\u2192\\t// and the containerID of their containers\\n   129\u2192\\tpodStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\t// containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID\\n   132\u2192\\t// for all containers a pod\\n   133\u2192\\tcontainerMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192\\ttopology *topology.CPUTopology\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192\\tnodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\t// sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.\\n   140\u2192\\t// We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.\\n   141\u2192\\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192\\t// stateFileDirectory holds the directory where the state file for checkpoints is held.\\n   144\u2192\\tstateFileDirectory string\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\t// allCPUs is the set of online CPUs as reported by the system\\n   147\u2192\\tallCPUs cpuset.CPUSet\\n   148\u2192\\n   149\u2192\\t// allocatableCPUs is the set of online CPUs as reported by the system,\\n   150\u2192\\t// and available for allocation, minus the reserved set\\n   151\u2192\\tallocatableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet\\n   152\u2192}\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192var _ Manager = &manager{}\\n   155\u2192\\n   156\u2192type sourcesReadyStub struct{}\\n   157\u2192\\n   158\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AddSource(source string) {}\\n   159\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AllReady() bool          { return true }\\n   160\u2192\\n   161\u2192// NewManager creates new cpu manager based on provided policy\\n   162\u2192func NewManager(logger logr.Logger, cpuPolicyName string, cpuPolicyOptions map[string]string, reconcilePeriod time.Duration, machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, specificCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, nodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList, stateFileDirectory string, affinity topologymanager.Store) (Manager, error) {\\n   163\u2192\\tvar topo *topology.CPUTopology\\n   164\u2192\\tvar policy Policy\\n   165\u2192\\tvar err error\\n   166\u2192\\n   167\u2192\\ttopo, err = topology.Discover(logger, machineInfo)\\n   168\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   169\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   170\u2192\\t}\\n   171\u2192\\n   172\u2192\\tswitch policyName(cpuPolicyName) {\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\tcase PolicyNone:\\n   175\u2192\\t\\tpolicy, err = NewNonePolicy(cpuPolicyOptions)\\n   176\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   177\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"new none policy error: %w\\\", err)\\n   178\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   179\u2192\\n   180\u2192\\tcase PolicyStatic:\\n   181\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Detected CPU topology\\\", \\\"topology\\\", topo)\\n   182\u2192\\n   183\u2192\\t\\treservedCPUs, ok := nodeAllocatableReservation[v1.ResourceCPU]\\n   184\u2192\\t\\tif !ok {\\n   185\u2192\\t\\t\\t// The static policy cannot initialize without this information.\\n   186\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"[cpumanager] unable to determine reserved CPU resources for static policy\\\")\\n   187\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   188\u2192\\t\\tif reservedCPUs.IsZero() {\\n   189\u2192\\t\\t\\t// The static policy requires this to be nonzero. Zero CPU reservation\\n   190\u2192\\t\\t\\t// would allow the shared pool to be completely exhausted. At that point\\n   191\u2192\\t\\t\\t// either we would violate our guarantee of exclusivity or need to evict\\n   192\u2192\\t\\t\\t// any pod that has at least one container that requires zero CPUs.\\n   193\u2192\\t\\t\\t// See the comments in policy_static.go for more details.\\n   194\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"[cpumanager] the static policy requires systemreserved.cpu + kubereserved.cpu to be greater than zero\\\")\\n   195\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   196\u2192\\n   197\u2192\\t\\t// Take the ceiling of the reservation, since fractional CPUs cannot be\\n   198\u2192\\t\\t// exclusively allocated.\\n   199\u2192\\t\\treservedCPUsFloat := float64(reservedCPUs.MilliValue()) / 1000\\n   200\u2192\\t\\tnumReservedCPUs := int(math.Ceil(reservedCPUsFloat))\\n   201\u2192\\t\\tpolicy, err = NewStaticPolicy(logger, topo, numReservedCPUs, specificCPUs, affinity, cpuPolicyOptions)\\n   202\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   203\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"new static policy error: %w\\\", err)\\n   204\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   205\u2192\\n   206\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   207\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown policy: \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\", cpuPolicyName)\\n   208\u2192\\t}\\n   209\u2192\\n   210\u2192\\tmanager := &manager{\\n   211\u2192\\t\\tpolicy:                     policy,\\n   212\u2192\\t\\treconcilePeriod:            reconcilePeriod,\\n   213\u2192\\t\\tlastUpdateState:            state.NewMemoryState(logger),\\n   214\u2192\\t\\ttopology:                   topo,\\n   215\u2192\\t\\tnodeAllocatableReservation: nodeAllocatableReservation,\\n   216\u2192\\t\\tstateFileDirectory:         stateFileDirectory,\\n   217\u2192\\t\\tallCPUs:                    topo.CPUDetails.CPUs(),\\n   218\u2192\\t}\\n   219\u2192\\tmanager.sourcesReady = &sourcesReadyStub{}\\n   220\u2192\\treturn manager, nil\\n   221\u2192}\\n   222\u2192\\n   223\u2192func (m *manager) Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error {\\n   224\u2192\\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n   225\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Starting\\\", \\\"policy\\\", m.policy.Name())\\n   226\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Reconciling\\\", \\\"reconcilePeriod\\\", m.reconcilePeriod)\\n   227\u2192\\tm.sourcesReady = sourcesReady\\n   228\u2192\\tm.activePods = activePods\\n   229\u2192\\tm.podStatusProvider = podStatusProvider\\n   230\u2192\\tm.containerRuntime = containerRuntime\\n   231\u2192\\tm.containerMap = initialContainers\\n   232\u2192\\n   233\u2192\\tstateImpl, err := state.NewCheckpointState(logger, m.stateFileDirectory, cpuManagerStateFileName, m.policy.Name(), m.containerMap)\\n   234\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   235\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Could not initialize checkpoint manager, please drain node and remove policy state file\\\")\\n   236\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   237\u2192\\t}\\n   238\u2192\\tm.state = stateImpl\\n   239\u2192\\n   240\u2192\\terr = m.policy.Start(logger, m.state)\\n   241\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   242\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Policy start error\\\")\\n   243\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   244\u2192\\t}\\n   245\u2192\\n   246\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"CPU manager started\\\", \\\"policy\\\", m.policy.Name())\\n   247\u2192\\n   248\u2192\\tm.allocatableCPUs = m.policy.GetAllocatableCPUs(m.state)\\n   249\u2192\\n   250\u2192\\tif m.policy.Name() == string(PolicyNone) {\\n   251\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   252\u2192\\t}\\n   253\u2192\\t// Periodically call m.reconcileState() to continue to keep the CPU sets of\\n   254\u2192\\t// all pods in sync with and guaranteed CPUs handed out among them.\\n   255\u2192\\tgo wait.Until(func() { m.reconcileState(ctx) }, m.reconcilePeriod, wait.NeverStop)\\n   256\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   257\u2192}\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192func (m *manager) Allocate(p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container) error {\\n   260\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO() // until we move topology manager to contextual logging\\n   261\u2192\\n   262\u2192\\t// Garbage collect any stranded resources before allocating CPUs.\\n   263\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(logger)\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   266\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   267\u2192\\n   268\u2192\\t// Call down into the policy to assign this container CPUs if required.\\n   269\u2192\\terr := m.policy.Allocate(logger, m.state, p, c)\\n   270\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   271\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"policy error\\\")\\n   272\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   273\u2192\\t}\\n   274\u2192\\n   275\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   276\u2192}\\n   277\u2192\\n   278\u2192func (m *manager) AddContainer(logger logr.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) {\\n   279\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   280\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   281\u2192\\tif cset, exists := m.state.GetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name); exists {\\n   282\u2192\\t\\tm.lastUpdateState.SetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name, cset)\\n   283\u2192\\t}\\n   284\u2192\\tm.containerMap.Add(string(pod.UID), container.Name, containerID)\\n   285\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Added Container\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"podUID\\\", pod.UID, \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name, \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n   286\u2192}\\n   287\u2192\\n   288\u2192func (m *manager) RemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, containerID string) error {\\n   289\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   290\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   291\u2192\\n   292\u2192\\terr := m.policyRemoveContainerByID(logger, containerID)\\n   293\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   294\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"RemoveContainer error\\\")\\n   295\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   296\u2192\\t}\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   299\u2192}\\n   300\u2192\\n   301\u2192func (m *manager) policyRemoveContainerByID(logger logr.Logger, containerID string) error {\\n   302\u2192\\tpodUID, containerName, err := m.containerMap.GetContainerRef(containerID)\\n   303\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   304\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   305\u2192\\t}\\n   306\u2192\\n   307\u2192\\terr = m.policy.RemoveContainer(logger, m.state, podUID, containerName)\\n   308\u2192\\tif err == nil {\\n   309\u2192\\t\\tm.lastUpdateState.Delete(podUID, containerName)\\n   310\u2192\\t\\tm.containerMap.RemoveByContainerID(containerID)\\n   311\u2192\\t}\\n   312\u2192\\n   313\u2192\\treturn err\\n   314\u2192}\\n   315\u2192\\n   316\u2192func (m *manager) policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger logr.Logger, podUID string, containerName string) error {\\n   317\u2192\\terr := m.policy.RemoveContainer(logger, m.state, podUID, containerName)\\n   318\u2192\\tif err == nil {\\n   319\u2192\\t\\tm.lastUpdateState.Delete(podUID, containerName)\\n   320\u2192\\t\\tm.containerMap.RemoveByContainerRef(podUID, containerName)\\n   321\u2192\\t}\\n   322\u2192\\n   323\u2192\\treturn err\\n   324\u2192}\\n   325\u2192\\n   326\u2192func (m *manager) State() state.Reader {\\n   327\u2192\\treturn m.state\\n   328\u2192}\\n   329\u2192\\n   330\u2192func (m *manager) GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   331\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   332\u2192\\t// Garbage collect any stranded resources before providing TopologyHints\\n   333\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(logger)\\n   334\u2192\\t// Delegate to active policy\\n   335\u2192\\treturn m.policy.GetTopologyHints(logger, m.state, pod, container)\\n   336\u2192}\\n   337\u2192\\n   338\u2192func (m *manager) GetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   339\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   340\u2192\\t// Garbage collect any stranded resources before providing TopologyHints\\n   341\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(logger)\\n   342\u2192\\t// Delegate to active policy\\n   343\u2192\\treturn m.policy.GetPodTopologyHints(logger, m.state, pod)\\n   344\u2192}\\n   345\u2192\\n   346\u2192func (m *manager) GetAllocatableCPUs() cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   347\u2192\\treturn m.allocatableCPUs.Clone()\\n   348\u2192}\\n   349\u2192\\n   350\u2192func (m *manager) GetAllCPUs() cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   351\u2192\\treturn m.allCPUs.Clone()\\n   352\u2192}\\n   353\u2192\\n   354\u2192type reconciledContainer struct {\\n   355\u2192\\tpodName       string\\n   356\u2192\\tcontainerName string\\n   357\u2192\\tcontainerID   string\\n   358\u2192}\\n   359\u2192\\n   360\u2192func (m *manager) removeStaleState(rootLogger logr.Logger) {\\n   361\u2192\\t// Only once all sources are ready do we attempt to remove any stale state.\\n   362\u2192\\t// This ensures that the call to `m.activePods()` below will succeed with\\n   363\u2192\\t// the actual active pods list.\\n   364\u2192\\tif !m.sourcesReady.AllReady() {\\n   365\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   366\u2192\\t}\\n   367\u2192\\n   368\u2192\\t// We grab the lock to ensure that no new containers will grab CPUs while\\n   369\u2192\\t// executing the code below. Without this lock, its possible that we end up\\n   370\u2192\\t// removing state that is newly added by an asynchronous call to\\n   371\u2192\\t// AddContainer() during the execution of this code.\\n   372\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   373\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   374\u2192\\n   375\u2192\\t// Get the list of active pods.\\n   376\u2192\\tactivePods := m.activePods()\\n   377\u2192\\n   378\u2192\\t// Build a list of (podUID, containerName) pairs for all containers in all active Pods.\\n   379\u2192\\tactiveContainers := make(map[string]map[string]struct{})\\n   380\u2192\\tfor _, pod := range activePods {\\n   381\u2192\\t\\tactiveContainers[string(pod.UID)] = make(map[string]struct{})\\n   382\u2192\\t\\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {\\n   383\u2192\\t\\t\\tactiveContainers[string(pod.UID)][container.Name] = struct{}{}\\n   384\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   385\u2192\\t}\\n   386\u2192\\n   387\u2192\\t// Loop through the CPUManager state. Remove any state for containers not\\n   388\u2192\\t// in the `activeContainers` list built above.\\n   389\u2192\\tassignments := m.state.GetCPUAssignments()\\n   390\u2192\\tfor podUID := range assignments {\\n   391\u2192\\t\\tfor containerName := range assignments[podUID] {\\n   392\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithValues(rootLogger, \\\"podUID\\\", podUID, \\\"containerName\\\", containerName)\\n   393\u2192\\n   394\u2192\\t\\t\\tif _, ok := activeContainers[podUID][containerName]; ok {\\n   395\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"container still active\\\")\\n   396\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   397\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   398\u2192\\n   399\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"removing container\\\")\\n   400\u2192\\t\\t\\terr := m.policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger, podUID, containerName)\\n   401\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   402\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"failed to remove container\\\")\\n   403\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   404\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   405\u2192\\t}\\n   406\u2192\\n   407\u2192\\tm.containerMap.Visit(func(podUID, containerName, containerID string) {\\n   408\u2192\\t\\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithValues(rootLogger, \\\"podUID\\\", podUID, \\\"containerName\\\", containerName)\\n   409\u2192\\t\\tif _, ok := activeContainers[podUID][containerName]; ok {\\n   410\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"containerMap: container still active\\\")\\n   411\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   412\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   413\u2192\\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"containerMap: removing container\\\")\\n   414\u2192\\t\\terr := m.policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger, podUID, containerName)\\n   415\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   416\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"containerMap: failed to remove container\\\")\\n   417\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   418\u2192\\t})\\n   419\u2192}\\n   420\u2192\\n   421\u2192func (m *manager) reconcileState(ctx context.Context) (success []reconciledContainer, failure []reconciledContainer) {\\n   422\u2192\\tsuccess = []reconciledContainer{}\\n   423\u2192\\tfailure = []reconciledContainer{}\\n   424\u2192\\n   425\u2192\\trootLogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n   426\u2192\\n   427\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(rootLogger)\\n   428\u2192\\tfor _, pod := range m.activePods() {\\n   429\u2192\\t\\tpodLogger := klog.LoggerWithValues(rootLogger, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod))\\n   430\u2192\\n   431\u2192\\t\\tpstatus, ok := m.podStatusProvider.GetPodStatus(pod.UID)\\n   432\u2192\\t\\tif !ok {\\n   433\u2192\\t\\t\\tpodLogger.V(5).Info(\\\"skipping pod; status not found\\\")\\n   434\u2192\\t\\t\\tfailure = append(failure, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\"})\\n   435\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   436\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   437\u2192\\n   438\u2192\\t\\tallContainers := pod.Spec.InitContainers\\n   439\u2192\\t\\tallContainers = append(allContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...)\\n   440\u2192\\t\\tfor _, container := range allContainers {\\n   441\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger := klog.LoggerWithValues(podLogger, \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n   442\u2192\\n   443\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontainerID, err := findContainerIDByName(&pstatus, container.Name)\\n   444\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   445\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"skipping container; ID not found in pod status\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n   446\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfailure = append(failure, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, container.Name, \\\"\\\"})\\n   447\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   448\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   449\u2192\\n   450\u2192\\t\\t\\tcstatus, err := findContainerStatusByName(&pstatus, container.Name)\\n   451\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   452\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"skipping container; container status not found in pod status\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n   453\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfailure = append(failure, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, container.Name, \\\"\\\"})\\n   454\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   455\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   456\u2192\\n   457\u2192\\t\\t\\tif cstatus.State.Waiting != nil ||\\n   458\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t(cstatus.State.Waiting == nil && cstatus.State.Running == nil && cstatus.State.Terminated == nil) {\\n   459\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"skipping container; container still in the waiting state\\\", \\\"err\\\", err)\\n   460\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfailure = append(failure, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, container.Name, \\\"\\\"})\\n   461\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   462\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   463\u2192\\n   464\u2192\\t\\t\\tm.Lock()\\n   465\u2192\\t\\t\\tif cstatus.State.Terminated != nil {\\n   466\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// The container is terminated but we can't call m.RemoveContainer()\\n   467\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// here because it could remove the allocated cpuset for the container\\n   468\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// which may be in the process of being restarted.  That would result\\n   469\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// in the container losing any exclusively-allocated CPUs that it\\n   470\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// was allocated.\\n   471\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t_, _, err := m.containerMap.GetContainerRef(containerID)\\n   472\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif err == nil {\\n   473\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"ignoring terminated container\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n   474\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   475\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tm.Unlock()\\n   476\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   477\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   478\u2192\\n   479\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Once we make it here we know we have a running container.\\n   480\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Idempotently add it to the containerMap incase it is missing.\\n   481\u2192\\t\\t\\t// This can happen after a kubelet restart, for example.\\n   482\u2192\\t\\t\\tm.containerMap.Add(string(pod.UID), container.Name, containerID)\\n   483\u2192\\t\\t\\tm.Unlock()\\n   484\u2192\\n   485\u2192\\t\\t\\tcset := m.state.GetCPUSetOrDefault(string(pod.UID), container.Name)\\n   486\u2192\\t\\t\\tif cset.IsEmpty() {\\n   487\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// NOTE: This should not happen outside of tests.\\n   488\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"ReconcileState: skipping container; empty cpuset assigned\\\")\\n   489\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tfailure = append(failure, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, container.Name, containerID})\\n   490\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   491\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   492\u2192\\n   493\u2192\\t\\t\\tlcset := m.lastUpdateState.GetCPUSetOrDefault(string(pod.UID), container.Name)\\n   494\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !cset.Equals(lcset) {\\n   495\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"updating container\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"cpuSet\\\", cset)\\n   496\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\terr = m.updateContainerCPUSet(ctx, containerID, cset)\\n   497\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   498\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"failed to update container\\\", \\\"containerID\\\", containerID, \\\"cpuSet\\\", cset)\\n   499\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfailure = append(failure, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, container.Name, containerID})\\n   500\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   501\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   502\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tm.lastUpdateState.SetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name, cset)\\n   503\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   504\u2192\\t\\t\\tsuccess = append(success, reconciledContainer{pod.Name, container.Name, containerID})\\n   505\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   506\u2192\\t}\\n   507\u2192\\treturn success, failure\\n   508\u2192}\\n   509\u2192\\n   510\u2192func findContainerIDByName(status *v1.PodStatus, name string) (string, error) {\\n   511\u2192\\tallStatuses := status.InitContainerStatuses\\n   512\u2192\\tallStatuses = append(allStatuses, status.ContainerStatuses...)\\n   513\u2192\\tfor _, container := range allStatuses {\\n   514\u2192\\t\\tif container.Name == name && container.ContainerID != \\\"\\\" {\\n   515\u2192\\t\\t\\tcid := &kubecontainer.ContainerID{}\\n   516\u2192\\t\\t\\terr := cid.ParseString(container.ContainerID)\\n   517\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   518\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\", err\\n   519\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   520\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn cid.ID, nil\\n   521\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   522\u2192\\t}\\n   523\u2192\\treturn \\\"\\\", fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to find ID for container with name %v in pod status (it may not be running)\\\", name)\\n   524\u2192}\\n   525\u2192\\n   526\u2192func findContainerStatusByName(status *v1.PodStatus, name string) (*v1.ContainerStatus, error) {\\n   527\u2192\\tfor _, containerStatus := range append(status.InitContainerStatuses, status.ContainerStatuses...) {\\n   528\u2192\\t\\tif containerStatus.Name == name {\\n   529\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn &containerStatus, nil\\n   530\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   531\u2192\\t}\\n   532\u2192\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to find status for container with name %v in pod status (it may not be running)\\\", name)\\n   533\u2192}\\n   534\u2192\\n   535\u2192func (m *manager) GetExclusiveCPUs(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   536\u2192\\tif result, ok := m.state.GetCPUSet(podUID, containerName); ok {\\n   537\u2192\\t\\treturn result\\n   538\u2192\\t}\\n   539\u2192\\n   540\u2192\\treturn cpuset.CPUSet{}\\n   541\u2192}\\n   542\u2192\\n   543\u2192func (m *manager) GetCPUAffinity(podUID, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   544\u2192\\treturn m.state.GetCPUSetOrDefault(podUID, containerName)\\n   545\u2192}\\n   546\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"685629c7-3248-43d5-835e-7ef141c9c49e\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:08.790Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"ac1ec86d-d767-4320-9ab0-6dc97de43f21\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"685629c7-3248-43d5-835e-7ef141c9c49e\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01S9DAzTzLo54VjaLU8MiUnQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_014HFagoJNU3VwQPTenx4ZVY\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/policy.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":1,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":445,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13068,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":445},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GDZvzzkNEz56GYRvn\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"efe6922f-6e13-4de6-991e-ef39eaa56f58\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:08.997Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"efe6922f-6e13-4de6-991e-ef39eaa56f58\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_014HFagoJNU3VwQPTenx4ZVY\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_014HFagoJNU3VwQPTenx4ZVY\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.001Z\",\"uuid\":\"44cca5a2-cbd2-49bf-8b76-dbb916990bcd\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"efe6922f-6e13-4de6-991e-     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cpumanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/go-logr/logr\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    26\u2192)\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192// Policy implements logic for pod container to CPU assignment.\\n    29\u2192type Policy interface {\\n    30\u2192\\tName() string\\n    31\u2192\\tStart(logger logr.Logger, s state.State) error\\n    32\u2192\\t// Allocate call is idempotent\\n    33\u2192\\tAllocate(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    34\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer call is idempotent\\n    35\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, podUID string, containerName string) error\\n    36\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    37\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    38\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    39\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    40\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    41\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment per Pod\\n    42\u2192\\t// among this and other resource controllers.\\n    43\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    44\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableCPUs returns the total set of CPUs available for allocation.\\n    45\u2192\\tGetAllocatableCPUs(m state.State) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    46\u2192}\\n    47\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"24248245-a3d0-4b7a-8324-9c9830c9842a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.001Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"efe6922f-6e13-4de6-991e-ef39eaa56f58\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"24248245-a3d0-4b7a-8324-9c9830c9842a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01S9DAzTzLo54VjaLU8MiUnQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_0118cZ5xBiQNEg8FYR8zU4v4\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/policy_none.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":1,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":445,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13068,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":445},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GDZvzzkNEz56GYRvn\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"22012fc7-7e24-47bb-85c4-3c2a47141c54\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.204Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"22012fc7-7e24-47bb-85c4-3c2a47141c54\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_0118cZ5xBiQNEg8FYR8zU4v4\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_0118cZ5xBiQNEg8FYR8zU4v4\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.206Z\",\"uuid\":\"cd835d39-fbde-400a-85e1-df3f81d1c700\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"22012fc7-7e24-47bb-85c4-3c2a47141c54\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_0118cZ5xBiQNEg8FYR8zU4v4\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cpumanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/go-logr/logr\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    27\u2192)\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192type nonePolicy struct {\\n    30\u2192}\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192var _ Policy = &nonePolicy{}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// PolicyNone name of none policy\\n    35\u2192const PolicyNone policyName = \\\"none\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// NewNonePolicy returns a cpuset manager policy that does nothing\\n    38\u2192func NewNonePolicy(cpuPolicyOptions map[string]string) (Policy, error) {\\n    39\u2192\\tif len(cpuPolicyOptions) > 0 {\\n    40\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"None policy: received unsupported options=%v\\\", cpuPolicyOptions)\\n    41\u2192\\t}\\n    42\u2192\\treturn &nonePolicy{}, nil\\n    43\u2192}\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) Name() string {\\n    46\u2192\\treturn string(PolicyNone)\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) Start(logger logr.Logger, s state.State) error {\\n    50\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Start\\\")\\n    51\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    52\u2192}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) Allocate(_ logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error {\\n    55\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) RemoveContainer(_ logr.Logger, s state.State, podUID string, containerName string) error {\\n    59\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    60\u2192}\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) GetTopologyHints(_ logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n    63\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    64\u2192}\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) GetPodTopologyHints(_ logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n    67\u2192\\treturn nil\\n    68\u2192}\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192// Assignable CPUs are the ones that can be exclusively allocated to pods that meet the exclusivity requirement\\n    71\u2192// (ie guaranteed QoS class and integral CPU request).\\n    72\u2192// Assignability of CPUs as a concept is only applicable in case of static policy i.e. scenarios where workloads\\n    73\u2192// CAN get exclusive access to core(s).\\n    74\u2192// Hence, we return empty set here: no cpus are assignable according to above definition with this policy.\\n    75\u2192func (p *nonePolicy) GetAllocatableCPUs(m state.State) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n    76\u2192\\treturn cpuset.New()\\n    77\u2192}\\n    78\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"6acc60a6-3e44-4a3f-be22-ee5e61583a71\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.206Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"22012fc7-7e24-47bb-85c4-3c2a47141c54\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"6acc60a6-3e44-4a3f-be22-ee5e61583a71\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01S9DAzTzLo54VjaLU8MiUnQ\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01KDPYJrEf8zjbYccRd9aZoP\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/policy_static.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":1,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":445,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13068,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":445},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GDZvzzkNEz56GYRvn\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"dd62f649-0b97-499d-a15f-fe4d85911f30\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.420Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"dd62f649-0b97-499d-a15f-fe4d85911f30\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01KDPYJrEf8zjbYccRd9aZoP\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01KDPYJrEf8zjbYccRd9aZoP\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.662Z\",\"uuid\":\"74086f6e-8a3c-4a93-a619-6f1592b732ac\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"dd62f649-0b97-499d-a15f-fe4d85911f30\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01KDPYJrEf8zjbYccRd9aZoP\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cpumanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/go-logr/logr\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tresourcehelper \\\"k8s.io/component-helpers/resource\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\tpodutil \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\tv1qos \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper/qos\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/topology\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/bitmask\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    38\u2192)\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192const (\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192\\t// PolicyStatic is the name of the static policy.\\n    43\u2192\\t// Should options be given, these will be ignored and backward (up to 1.21 included)\\n    44\u2192\\t// compatible behaviour will be enforced\\n    45\u2192\\tPolicyStatic policyName = \\\"static\\\"\\n    46\u2192\\t// ErrorSMTAlignment represents the type of an SMTAlignmentError\\n    47\u2192\\tErrorSMTAlignment = \\\"SMTAlignmentError\\\"\\n    48\u2192)\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// SMTAlignmentError represents an error due to SMT alignment\\n    51\u2192type SMTAlignmentError struct {\\n    52\u2192\\tRequestedCPUs         int\\n    53\u2192\\tCpusPerCore           int\\n    54\u2192\\tAvailablePhysicalCPUs int\\n    55\u2192\\tCausedByPhysicalCPUs  bool\\n    56\u2192}\\n    57\u2192\\n    58\u2192func (e SMTAlignmentError) Error() string {\\n    59\u2192\\tif e.CausedByPhysicalCPUs {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"SMT Alignment Error: not enough free physical CPUs: available physical CPUs = %d, requested CPUs = %d, CPUs per core = %d\\\", e.AvailablePhysicalCPUs, e.RequestedCPUs, e.CpusPerCore)\\n    61\u2192\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\\\"SMT Alignment Error: requested %d cpus not multiple cpus per core = %d\\\", e.RequestedCPUs, e.CpusPerCore)\\n    63\u2192}\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192// Type returns human-readable type of this error. Used in the admission control to populate Admission Failure reason.\\n    66\u2192func (e SMTAlignmentError) Type() string {\\n    67\u2192\\treturn ErrorSMTAlignment\\n    68\u2192}\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192// staticPolicy is a CPU manager policy that does not change CPU\\n    71\u2192// assignments for exclusively pinned guaranteed containers after the main\\n    72\u2192// container process starts.\\n    73\u2192//\\n    74\u2192// This policy allocates CPUs exclusively for a container if all the following\\n    75\u2192// conditions are met:\\n    76\u2192//\\n    77\u2192// - The pod QoS class is Guaranteed.\\n    78\u2192// - The CPU request is a positive integer.\\n    79\u2192//\\n    80\u2192// The static policy maintains the following sets of logical CPUs:\\n    81\u2192//\\n    82\u2192//   - SHARED: Burstable, BestEffort, and non-integral Guaranteed containers\\n    83\u2192//     run here. Initially this contains all CPU IDs on the system. As\\n    84\u2192//     exclusive allocations are created and destroyed, this CPU set shrinks\\n    85\u2192//     and grows, accordingly. This is stored in the state as the default\\n    86\u2192//     CPU set.\\n    87\u2192//\\n    88\u2192//   - RESERVED: A subset of the shared pool which is not exclusively\\n    89\u2192//     allocatable. The membership of this pool is static for the lifetime of\\n    90\u2192//     the Kubelet. The size of the reserved pool is\\n    91\u2192//     ceil(systemreserved.cpu + kubereserved.cpu).\\n    92\u2192//     Reserved CPUs are taken topologically starting with lowest-indexed\\n    93\u2192//     physical core, as reported by cAdvisor.\\n    94\u2192//\\n    95\u2192//   - ASSIGNABLE: Equal to SHARED - RESERVED. Exclusive CPUs are allocated\\n    96\u2192//     from this pool.\\n    97\u2192//\\n    98\u2192//   - EXCLUSIVE ALLOCATIONS: CPU sets assigned exclusively to one container.\\n    99\u2192//     These are stored as explicit assignments in the state.\\n   100\u2192//\\n   101\u2192// When an exclusive allocation is made, the static policy also updates the\\n   102\u2192// default cpuset in the state abstraction. The CPU manager's periodic\\n   103\u2192// reconcile loop takes care of rewriting the cpuset in cgroupfs for any\\n   104\u2192// containers that may be running in the shared pool. For this reason,\\n   105\u2192// applications running within exclusively-allocated containers must tolerate\\n   106\u2192// potentially sharing their allocated CPUs for up to the CPU manager\\n   107\u2192// reconcile period.\\n   108\u2192type staticPolicy struct {\\n   109\u2192\\t// cpu socket topology\\n   110\u2192\\ttopology *topology.CPUTopology\\n   111\u2192\\t// set of CPUs that is not available for exclusive assignment\\n   112\u2192\\treservedCPUs cpuset.CPUSet\\n   113\u2192\\t// Superset of reservedCPUs. It includes not just the reservedCPUs themselves,\\n   114\u2192\\t// but also any siblings of those reservedCPUs on the same physical die.\\n   115\u2192\\t// NOTE: If the reserved set includes full physical CPUs from the beginning\\n   116\u2192\\t// (e.g. only reserved pairs of core siblings) this set is expected to be\\n   117\u2192\\t// identical to the reserved set.\\n   118\u2192\\treservedPhysicalCPUs cpuset.CPUSet\\n   119\u2192\\t// topology manager reference to get container Topology affinity\\n   120\u2192\\taffinity topologymanager.Store\\n   121\u2192\\t// set of CPUs to reuse across allocations in a pod\\n   122\u2192\\tcpusToReuse map[string]cpuset.CPUSet\\n   123\u2192\\t// options allow to fine-tune the behaviour of the policy\\n   124\u2192\\toptions StaticPolicyOptions\\n   125\u2192\\t// we compute this value multiple time, and it's not supposed to change\\n   126\u2192\\t// at runtime - the cpumanager can't deal with runtime topology changes anyway.\\n   127\u2192\\tcpuGroupSize int\\n   128\u2192}\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192// Ensure staticPolicy implements Policy interface\\n   131\u2192var _ Policy = &staticPolicy{}\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192// NewStaticPolicy returns a CPU manager policy that does not change CPU\\n   134\u2192// assignments for exclusively pinned guaranteed containers after the main\\n   135\u2192// container process starts.\\n   136\u2192func NewStaticPolicy(logger logr.Logger, topology *topology.CPUTopology, numReservedCPUs int, reservedCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, affinity topologymanager.Store, cpuPolicyOptions map[string]string) (Policy, error) {\\n   137\u2192\\topts, err := NewStaticPolicyOptions(cpuPolicyOptions)\\n   138\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   139\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   140\u2192\\t}\\n   141\u2192\\terr = ValidateStaticPolicyOptions(opts, topology, affinity)\\n   142\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   143\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   144\u2192\\t}\\n   145\u2192\\n   146\u2192\\tcpuGroupSize := topology.CPUsPerCore()\\n   147\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"created with configuration\\\", \\\"options\\\", opts, \\\"cpuGroupSize\\\", cpuGroupSize)\\n   148\u2192\\n   149\u2192\\tpolicy := &staticPolicy{\\n   150\u2192\\t\\ttopology:     topology,\\n   151\u2192\\t\\taffinity:     affinity,\\n   152\u2192\\t\\tcpusToReuse:  make(map[string]cpuset.CPUSet),\\n   153\u2192\\t\\toptions:      opts,\\n   154\u2192\\t\\tcpuGroupSize: cpuGroupSize,\\n   155\u2192\\t}\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192\\tallCPUs := topology.CPUDetails.CPUs()\\n   158\u2192\\tvar reserved cpuset.CPUSet\\n   159\u2192\\tif reservedCPUs.Size() > 0 {\\n   160\u2192\\t\\treserved = reservedCPUs\\n   161\u2192\\t} else {\\n   162\u2192\\t\\t// takeByTopology allocates CPUs associated with low-numbered cores from\\n   163\u2192\\t\\t// allCPUs.\\n   164\u2192\\t\\t//\\n   165\u2192\\t\\t// For example: Given a system with 8 CPUs available and HT enabled,\\n   166\u2192\\t\\t// if numReservedCPUs=2, then reserved={0,4}\\n   167\u2192\\t\\treserved, _ = policy.takeByTopology(logger, allCPUs, numReservedCPUs)\\n   168\u2192\\t}\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\tif reserved.Size() != numReservedCPUs {\\n   171\u2192\\t\\terr := fmt.Errorf(\\\"[cpumanager] unable to reserve the required amount of CPUs (size of %s did not equal %d)\\\", reserved, numReservedCPUs)\\n   172\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   173\u2192\\t}\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192\\tvar reservedPhysicalCPUs cpuset.CPUSet\\n   176\u2192\\tfor _, cpu := range reserved.UnsortedList() {\\n   177\u2192\\t\\tcore, err := topology.CPUCoreID(cpu)\\n   178\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   179\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"[cpumanager] unable to build the reserved physical CPUs from the reserved set: %w\\\", err)\\n   180\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   181\u2192\\t\\treservedPhysicalCPUs = reservedPhysicalCPUs.Union(topology.CPUDetails.CPUsInCores(core))\\n   182\u2192\\t}\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Reserved CPUs not available for exclusive assignment\\\", \\\"reservedSize\\\", reserved.Size(), \\\"reserved\\\", reserved, \\\"reservedPhysicalCPUs\\\", reservedPhysicalCPUs)\\n   185\u2192\\tpolicy.reservedCPUs = reserved\\n   186\u2192\\tpolicy.reservedPhysicalCPUs = reservedPhysicalCPUs\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192\\treturn policy, nil\\n   189\u2192}\\n   190\u2192\\n   191\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) Name() string {\\n   192\u2192\\treturn string(PolicyStatic)\\n   193\u2192}\\n   194\u2192\\n   195\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) Start(logger logr.Logger, s state.State) error {\\n   196\u2192\\tif err := p.validateState(logger, s); err != nil {\\n   197\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"invalid state, please drain node and remove policy state file\\\")\\n   198\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   199\u2192\\t}\\n   200\u2192\\tp.initializeMetrics(logger, s)\\n   201\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   202\u2192}\\n   203\u2192\\n   204\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) validateState(logger logr.Logger, s state.State) error {\\n   205\u2192\\ttmpAssignments := s.GetCPUAssignments()\\n   206\u2192\\ttmpDefaultCPUset := s.GetDefaultCPUSet()\\n   207\u2192\\n   208\u2192\\tallCPUs := p.topology.CPUDetails.CPUs()\\n   209\u2192\\tif p.options.StrictCPUReservation {\\n   210\u2192\\t\\tallCPUs = allCPUs.Difference(p.reservedCPUs)\\n   211\u2192\\t}\\n   212\u2192\\n   213\u2192\\t// Default cpuset cannot be empty when assignments exist\\n   214\u2192\\tif tmpDefaultCPUset.IsEmpty() {\\n   215\u2192\\t\\tif len(tmpAssignments) != 0 {\\n   216\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"default cpuset cannot be empty\\\")\\n   217\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   218\u2192\\t\\t// state is empty initialize\\n   219\u2192\\t\\ts.SetDefaultCPUSet(allCPUs)\\n   220\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Static policy initialized\\\", \\\"defaultCPUSet\\\", allCPUs)\\n   221\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   222\u2192\\t}\\n   223\u2192\\n   224\u2192\\t// State has already been initialized from file (is not empty)\\n   225\u2192\\t// 1. Check if the reserved cpuset is not part of default cpuset because:\\n   226\u2192\\t// - kube/system reserved have changed (increased) - may lead to some containers not being able to start\\n   227\u2192\\t// - user tampered with file\\n   228\u2192\\tif p.options.StrictCPUReservation {\\n   229\u2192\\t\\tif !p.reservedCPUs.Intersection(tmpDefaultCPUset).IsEmpty() {\\n   230\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"some of strictly reserved cpus: %q are present in defaultCpuSet: %q\\\",\\n   231\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tp.reservedCPUs.Intersection(tmpDefaultCPUset).String(), tmpDefaultCPUset.String())\\n   232\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   233\u2192\\t} else {\\n   234\u2192\\t\\tif !p.reservedCPUs.Intersection(tmpDefaultCPUset).Equals(p.reservedCPUs) {\\n   235\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"not all reserved cpus: %q are present in defaultCpuSet: %q\\\",\\n   236\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tp.reservedCPUs.String(), tmpDefaultCPUset.String())\\n   237\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   238\u2192\\t}\\n   239\u2192\\n   240\u2192\\t// 2. Check if state for static policy is consistent\\n   241\u2192\\tfor pod := range tmpAssignments {\\n   242\u2192\\t\\tfor container, cset := range tmpAssignments[pod] {\\n   243\u2192\\t\\t\\t// None of the cpu in DEFAULT cset should be in s.assignments\\n   244\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !tmpDefaultCPUset.Intersection(cset).IsEmpty() {\\n   245\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"pod: %s, container: %s cpuset: %q overlaps with default cpuset %q\\\",\\n   246\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tpod, container, cset.String(), tmpDefaultCPUset.String())\\n   247\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   248\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   249\u2192\\t}\\n   250\u2192\\n   251\u2192\\t// 3. It's possible that the set of available CPUs has changed since\\n   252\u2192\\t// the state was written. This can be due to for example\\n   253\u2192\\t// offlining a CPU when kubelet is not running. If this happens,\\n   254\u2192\\t// CPU manager will run into trouble when later it tries to\\n   255\u2192\\t// assign non-existent CPUs to containers. Validate that the\\n   256\u2192\\t// topology that was received during CPU manager startup matches with\\n   257\u2192\\t// the set of CPUs stored in the state.\\n   258\u2192\\ttotalKnownCPUs := tmpDefaultCPUset.Clone()\\n   259\u2192\\ttmpCPUSets := []cpuset.CPUSet{}\\n   260\u2192\\tfor pod := range tmpAssignments {\\n   261\u2192\\t\\tfor _, cset := range tmpAssignments[pod] {\\n   262\u2192\\t\\t\\ttmpCPUSets = append(tmpCPUSets, cset)\\n   263\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   264\u2192\\t}\\n   265\u2192\\ttotalKnownCPUs = totalKnownCPUs.Union(tmpCPUSets...)\\n   266\u2192\\tif !totalKnownCPUs.Equals(allCPUs) {\\n   267\u2192\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"current set of available CPUs %q doesn't match with CPUs in state %q\\\",\\n   268\u2192\\t\\t\\tallCPUs.String(), totalKnownCPUs.String())\\n   269\u2192\\n   270\u2192\\t}\\n   271\u2192\\n   272\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   273\u2192}\\n   274\u2192\\n   275\u2192// GetAllocatableCPUs returns the total set of CPUs available for allocation.\\n   276\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) GetAllocatableCPUs(s state.State) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   277\u2192\\treturn p.topology.CPUDetails.CPUs().Difference(p.reservedCPUs)\\n   278\u2192}\\n   279\u2192\\n   280\u2192// GetAvailableCPUs returns the set of unassigned CPUs minus the reserved set.\\n   281\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) GetAvailableCPUs(s state.State) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   282\u2192\\treturn s.GetDefaultCPUSet().Difference(p.reservedCPUs)\\n   283\u2192}\\n   284\u2192\\n   285\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) GetAvailablePhysicalCPUs(s state.State) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   286\u2192\\treturn s.GetDefaultCPUSet().Difference(p.reservedPhysicalCPUs)\\n   287\u2192}\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) updateCPUsToReuse(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, cset cpuset.CPUSet) {\\n   290\u2192\\t// If pod entries to m.cpusToReuse other than the current pod exist, delete them.\\n   291\u2192\\tfor podUID := range p.cpusToReuse {\\n   292\u2192\\t\\tif podUID != string(pod.UID) {\\n   293\u2192\\t\\t\\tdelete(p.cpusToReuse, podUID)\\n   294\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   295\u2192\\t}\\n   296\u2192\\t// If no cpuset exists for cpusToReuse by this pod yet, create one.\\n   297\u2192\\tif _, ok := p.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)]; !ok {\\n   298\u2192\\t\\tp.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)] = cpuset.New()\\n   299\u2192\\t}\\n   300\u2192\\t// Check if the container is an init container.\\n   301\u2192\\t// If so, add its cpuset to the cpuset of reusable CPUs for any new allocations.\\n   302\u2192\\tfor _, initContainer := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\\n   303\u2192\\t\\tif container.Name == initContainer.Name {\\n   304\u2192\\t\\t\\tif podutil.IsRestartableInitContainer(&initContainer) {\\n   305\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// If the container is a restartable init container, we should not\\n   306\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// reuse its cpuset, as a restartable init container can run with\\n   307\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// regular containers.\\n   308\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n   309\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   310\u2192\\t\\t\\tp.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)] = p.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)].Union(cset)\\n   311\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   312\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   313\u2192\\t}\\n   314\u2192\\t// Otherwise it is an app container.\\n   315\u2192\\t// Remove its cpuset from the cpuset of reusable CPUs for any new allocations.\\n   316\u2192\\tp.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)] = p.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)].Difference(cset)\\n   317\u2192}\\n   318\u2192\\n   319\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) Allocate(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) (rerr error) {\\n   320\u2192\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"podUID\\\", pod.UID, \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n   321\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Allocate start\\\") // V=0 for backward compatibility\\n   322\u2192\\tdefer logger.V(2).Info(\\\"Allocate end\\\")\\n   323\u2192\\n   324\u2192\\tnumCPUs := p.guaranteedCPUs(logger, pod, container)\\n   325\u2192\\tif numCPUs == 0 {\\n   326\u2192\\t\\t// container belongs in the shared pool (nothing to do; use default cpuset)\\n   327\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   328\u2192\\t}\\n   329\u2192\\n   330\u2192\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.PodLevelResources) && resourcehelper.IsPodLevelResourcesSet(pod) {\\n   331\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"CPU Manager allocation skipped, pod is using pod-level resources which are not supported by the static CPU manager policy\\\")\\n   332\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   333\u2192\\t}\\n   334\u2192\\n   335\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Static policy: Allocate\\\")\\n   336\u2192\\n   337\u2192\\t// container belongs in an exclusively allocated pool\\n   338\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerPinningRequestsTotal.Inc()\\n   339\u2192\\tdefer func() {\\n   340\u2192\\t\\tif rerr != nil {\\n   341\u2192\\t\\t\\tmetrics.CPUManagerPinningErrorsTotal.Inc()\\n   342\u2192\\t\\t\\tif p.options.FullPhysicalCPUsOnly {\\n   343\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResourcesFailure.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedPhysicalCPU).Inc()\\n   344\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   345\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   346\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   347\u2192\\t\\t// TODO: move in updateMetricsOnAllocate\\n   348\u2192\\t\\tif p.options.FullPhysicalCPUsOnly {\\n   349\u2192\\t\\t\\t// increment only if we know we allocate aligned resources\\n   350\u2192\\t\\t\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedPhysicalCPU).Inc()\\n   351\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   352\u2192\\t}()\\n   353\u2192\\n   354\u2192\\tif p.options.FullPhysicalCPUsOnly {\\n   355\u2192\\t\\tif (numCPUs % p.cpuGroupSize) != 0 {\\n   356\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Since CPU Manager has been enabled requesting strict SMT alignment, it means a guaranteed pod can only be admitted\\n   357\u2192\\t\\t\\t// if the CPU requested is a multiple of the number of virtual cpus per physical cores.\\n   358\u2192\\t\\t\\t// In case CPU request is not a multiple of the number of virtual cpus per physical cores the Pod will be put\\n   359\u2192\\t\\t\\t// in Failed state, with SMTAlignmentError as reason. Since the allocation happens in terms of physical cores\\n   360\u2192\\t\\t\\t// and the scheduler is responsible for ensuring that the workload goes to a node that has enough CPUs,\\n   361\u2192\\t\\t\\t// the pod would be placed on a node where there are enough physical cores available to be allocated.\\n   362\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Just like the behaviour in case of static policy, takeByTopology will try to first allocate CPUs from the same socket\\n   363\u2192\\t\\t\\t// and only in case the request cannot be sattisfied on a single socket, CPU allocation is done for a workload to occupy all\\n   364\u2192\\t\\t\\t// CPUs on a physical core. Allocation of individual threads would never have to occur.\\n   365\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn SMTAlignmentError{\\n   366\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tRequestedCPUs:        numCPUs,\\n   367\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tCpusPerCore:          p.cpuGroupSize,\\n   368\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tCausedByPhysicalCPUs: false,\\n   369\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   370\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   371\u2192\\n   372\u2192\\t\\tavailablePhysicalCPUs := p.GetAvailablePhysicalCPUs(s).Size()\\n   373\u2192\\n   374\u2192\\t\\t// It's legal to reserve CPUs which are not core siblings. In this case the CPU allocator can descend to single cores\\n   375\u2192\\t\\t// when picking CPUs. This will void the guarantee of FullPhysicalCPUsOnly. To prevent this, we need to additionally consider\\n   376\u2192\\t\\t// all the core siblings of the reserved CPUs as unavailable when computing the free CPUs, before to start the actual allocation.\\n   377\u2192\\t\\t// This way, by construction all possible CPUs allocation whose number is multiple of the SMT level are now correct again.\\n   378\u2192\\t\\tif numCPUs > availablePhysicalCPUs {\\n   379\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn SMTAlignmentError{\\n   380\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tRequestedCPUs:         numCPUs,\\n   381\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tCpusPerCore:           p.cpuGroupSize,\\n   382\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tAvailablePhysicalCPUs: availablePhysicalCPUs,\\n   383\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tCausedByPhysicalCPUs:  true,\\n   384\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   385\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   386\u2192\\t}\\n   387\u2192\\tif cset, ok := s.GetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name); ok {\\n   388\u2192\\t\\tp.updateCPUsToReuse(pod, container, cset)\\n   389\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Static policy: container already present in state, skipping\\\")\\n   390\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   391\u2192\\t}\\n   392\u2192\\n   393\u2192\\t// Call Topology Manager to get the aligned socket affinity across all hint providers.\\n   394\u2192\\thint := p.affinity.GetAffinity(string(pod.UID), container.Name)\\n   395\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Topology Affinity\\\", \\\"affinity\\\", hint)\\n   396\u2192\\n   397\u2192\\t// Allocate CPUs according to the NUMA affinity contained in the hint.\\n   398\u2192\\tcpuAllocation, err := p.allocateCPUs(logger, s, numCPUs, hint.NUMANodeAffinity, p.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)])\\n   399\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   400\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Unable to allocate CPUs\\\", \\\"numCPUs\\\", numCPUs)\\n   401\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   402\u2192\\t}\\n   403\u2192\\n   404\u2192\\ts.SetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name, cpuAllocation.CPUs)\\n   405\u2192\\tp.updateCPUsToReuse(pod, container, cpuAllocation.CPUs)\\n   406\u2192\\tp.updateMetricsOnAllocate(logger, s, cpuAllocation)\\n   407\u2192\\n   408\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Allocated exclusive CPUs\\\", \\\"cpuset\\\", cpuAllocation.CPUs.String())\\n   409\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   410\u2192}\\n   411\u2192\\n   412\u2192// getAssignedCPUsOfSiblings returns assigned cpus of given container's siblings(all containers other than the given container) in the given pod `podUID`.\\n   413\u2192func getAssignedCPUsOfSiblings(s state.State, podUID string, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   414\u2192\\tassignments := s.GetCPUAssignments()\\n   415\u2192\\tcset := cpuset.New()\\n   416\u2192\\tfor name, cpus := range assignments[podUID] {\\n   417\u2192\\t\\tif containerName == name {\\n   418\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   419\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   420\u2192\\t\\tcset = cset.Union(cpus)\\n   421\u2192\\t}\\n   422\u2192\\treturn cset\\n   423\u2192}\\n   424\u2192\\n   425\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) RemoveContainer(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, podUID string, containerName string) error {\\n   426\u2192\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"podUID\\\", podUID, \\\"containerName\\\", containerName)\\n   427\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"RemoveContainer start\\\") // backward compatibility\\n   428\u2192\\tdefer logger.V(4).Info(\\\"RemoveContainer start\\\")\\n   429\u2192\\tcpusInUse := getAssignedCPUsOfSiblings(s, podUID, containerName)\\n   430\u2192\\ttoRelease, ok := s.GetCPUSet(podUID, containerName)\\n   431\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n   432\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   433\u2192\\t}\\n   434\u2192\\ts.Delete(podUID, containerName)\\n   435\u2192\\t// Mutate the shared pool, adding released cpus.\\n   436\u2192\\ttoRelease = toRelease.Difference(cpusInUse)\\n   437\u2192\\tupdatedCPUs := s.GetDefaultCPUSet().Union(toRelease)\\n   438\u2192\\ts.SetDefaultCPUSet(updatedCPUs)\\n   439\u2192\\tp.updateMetricsOnRelease(logger, s, toRelease)\\n   440\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\" RemoveContainer end\\\", \\\"defaultCPUSet\\\", updatedCPUs)\\n   441\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   442\u2192}\\n   443\u2192\\n   444\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) allocateCPUs(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, numCPUs int, numaAffinity bitmask.BitMask, reusableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet) (topology.Allocation, error) {\\n   445\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"AllocateCPUs\\\", \\\"numCPUs\\\", numCPUs, \\\"socket\\\", numaAffinity)\\n   446\u2192\\n   447\u2192\\tallocatableCPUs := p.GetAvailableCPUs(s).Union(reusableCPUs)\\n   448\u2192\\n   449\u2192\\t// If there are aligned CPUs in numaAffinity, attempt to take those first.\\n   450\u2192\\tresult := topology.EmptyAllocation()\\n   451\u2192\\tif numaAffinity != nil {\\n   452\u2192\\t\\talignedCPUs := p.getAlignedCPUs(numaAffinity, allocatableCPUs)\\n   453\u2192\\n   454\u2192\\t\\tnumAlignedToAlloc := alignedCPUs.Size()\\n   455\u2192\\t\\tif numCPUs < numAlignedToAlloc {\\n   456\u2192\\t\\t\\tnumAlignedToAlloc = numCPUs\\n   457\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   458\u2192\\n   459\u2192\\t\\tallocatedCPUs, err := p.takeByTopology(logger, alignedCPUs, numAlignedToAlloc)\\n   460\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   461\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn topology.EmptyAllocation(), err\\n   462\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   463\u2192\\n   464\u2192\\t\\tresult.CPUs = result.CPUs.Union(allocatedCPUs)\\n   465\u2192\\t}\\n   466\u2192\\n   467\u2192\\t// Get any remaining CPUs from what's leftover after attempting to grab aligned ones.\\n   468\u2192\\tremainingCPUs, err := p.takeByTopology(logger, allocatableCPUs.Difference(result.CPUs), numCPUs-result.CPUs.Size())\\n   469\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   470\u2192\\t\\treturn topology.EmptyAllocation(), err\\n   471\u2192\\t}\\n   472\u2192\\tresult.CPUs = result.CPUs.Union(remainingCPUs)\\n   473\u2192\\tresult.Aligned = p.topology.CheckAlignment(result.CPUs)\\n   474\u2192\\n   475\u2192\\t// Remove allocated CPUs from the shared CPUSet.\\n   476\u2192\\ts.SetDefaultCPUSet(s.GetDefaultCPUSet().Difference(result.CPUs))\\n   477\u2192\\n   478\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"AllocateCPUs\\\", \\\"result\\\", result.String())\\n   479\u2192\\treturn result, nil\\n   480\u2192}\\n   481\u2192\\n   482\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) guaranteedCPUs(logger logr.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) int {\\n   483\u2192\\tqos := v1qos.GetPodQOS(pod)\\n   484\u2192\\tif qos != v1.PodQOSGuaranteed {\\n   485\u2192\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"Exclusive CPU allocation skipped, pod QoS is not guaranteed\\\", \\\"qos\\\", qos)\\n   486\u2192\\t\\treturn 0\\n   487\u2192\\t}\\n   488\u2192\\tcpuQuantity := container.Resources.Requests[v1.ResourceCPU]\\n   489\u2192\\tcpuValue := cpuQuantity.Value()\\n   490\u2192\\tif cpuValue*1000 != cpuQuantity.MilliValue() {\\n   491\u2192\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"Exclusive CPU allocation skipped, pod requested non-integral CPUs\\\", \\\"cpu\\\", cpuValue)\\n   492\u2192\\t\\treturn 0\\n   493\u2192\\t}\\n   494\u2192\\t// Safe downcast to do for all systems with < 2.1 billion CPUs.\\n   495\u2192\\t// Per the language spec, `int` is guaranteed to be at least 32 bits wide.\\n   496\u2192\\t// https://golang.org/ref/spec#Numeric_types\\n   497\u2192\\treturn int(cpuQuantity.Value())\\n   498\u2192}\\n   499\u2192\\n   500\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) podGuaranteedCPUs(logger logr.Logger, pod *v1.Pod) int {\\n   501\u2192\\t// The maximum of requested CPUs by init containers.\\n   502\u2192\\trequestedByInitContainers := 0\\n   503\u2192\\trequestedByRestartableInitContainers := 0\\n   504\u2192\\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\\n   505\u2192\\t\\tif _, ok := container.Resources.Requests[v1.ResourceCPU]; !ok {\\n   506\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   507\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   508\u2192\\t\\trequestedCPU := p.guaranteedCPUs(logger, pod, &container)\\n   509\u2192\\t\\t// See https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-node/753-sidecar-containers#resources-calculation-for-scheduling-and-pod-admission\\n   510\u2192\\t\\t// for the detail.\\n   511\u2192\\t\\tif podutil.IsRestartableInitContainer(&container) {\\n   512\u2192\\t\\t\\trequestedByRestartableInitContainers += requestedCPU\\n   513\u2192\\t\\t} else if requestedByRestartableInitContainers+requestedCPU > requestedByInitContainers {\\n   514\u2192\\t\\t\\trequestedByInitContainers = requestedByRestartableInitContainers + requestedCPU\\n   515\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   516\u2192\\t}\\n   517\u2192\\n   518\u2192\\t// The sum of requested CPUs by app containers.\\n   519\u2192\\trequestedByAppContainers := 0\\n   520\u2192\\tfor _, container := range pod.Spec.Containers {\\n   521\u2192\\t\\tif _, ok := container.Resources.Requests[v1.ResourceCPU]; !ok {\\n   522\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   523\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   524\u2192\\t\\trequestedByAppContainers += p.guaranteedCPUs(logger, pod, &container)\\n   525\u2192\\t}\\n   526\u2192\\n   527\u2192\\trequestedByLongRunningContainers := requestedByAppContainers + requestedByRestartableInitContainers\\n   528\u2192\\tif requestedByInitContainers > requestedByLongRunningContainers {\\n   529\u2192\\t\\treturn requestedByInitContainers\\n   530\u2192\\t}\\n   531\u2192\\treturn requestedByLongRunningContainers\\n   532\u2192}\\n   533\u2192\\n   534\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) takeByTopology(logger logr.Logger, availableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, numCPUs int) (cpuset.CPUSet, error) {\\n   535\u2192\\tcpuSortingStrategy := CPUSortingStrategyPacked\\n   536\u2192\\tif p.options.DistributeCPUsAcrossCores {\\n   537\u2192\\t\\tcpuSortingStrategy = CPUSortingStrategySpread\\n   538\u2192\\t}\\n   539\u2192\\n   540\u2192\\tif p.options.DistributeCPUsAcrossNUMA {\\n   541\u2192\\t\\tcpuGroupSize := 1\\n   542\u2192\\t\\tif p.options.FullPhysicalCPUsOnly {\\n   543\u2192\\t\\t\\tcpuGroupSize = p.cpuGroupSize\\n   544\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   545\u2192\\t\\treturn takeByTopologyNUMADistributed(logger, p.topology, availableCPUs, numCPUs, cpuGroupSize, cpuSortingStrategy)\\n   546\u2192\\t}\\n   547\u2192\\n   548\u2192\\treturn takeByTopologyNUMAPacked(logger, p.topology, availableCPUs, numCPUs, cpuSortingStrategy, p.options.PreferAlignByUncoreCacheOption)\\n   549\u2192}\\n   550\u2192\\n   551\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) GetTopologyHints(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   552\u2192\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"podUID\\\", pod.UID, \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n   553\u2192\\n   554\u2192\\t// Get a count of how many guaranteed CPUs have been requested.\\n   555\u2192\\trequested := p.guaranteedCPUs(logger, pod, container)\\n   556\u2192\\n   557\u2192\\t// Number of required CPUs is not an integer or a container is not part of the Guaranteed QoS class.\\n   558\u2192\\t// It will be treated by the TopologyManager as having no preference and cause it to ignore this\\n   559\u2192\\t// resource when considering pod alignment.\\n   560\u2192\\t// In terms of hints, this is equal to: TopologyHints[NUMANodeAffinity: nil, Preferred: true].\\n   561\u2192\\tif requested == 0 {\\n   562\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   563\u2192\\t}\\n   564\u2192\\n   565\u2192\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.PodLevelResources) && resourcehelper.IsPodLevelResourcesSet(pod) {\\n   566\u2192\\t\\tlogger.V(3).Info(\\\"CPU Manager hint generation skipped, pod is using pod-level resources which are not supported by the static CPU manager policy\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"podUID\\\", pod.UID)\\n   567\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   568\u2192\\t}\\n   569\u2192\\n   570\u2192\\t// Short circuit to regenerate the same hints if there are already\\n   571\u2192\\t// guaranteed CPUs allocated to the Container. This might happen after a\\n   572\u2192\\t// kubelet restart, for example.\\n   573\u2192\\tif allocated, exists := s.GetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name); exists {\\n   574\u2192\\t\\tif allocated.Size() != requested {\\n   575\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"CPUs already allocated to container with different number than request\\\", \\\"requestedSize\\\", requested, \\\"allocatedSize\\\", allocated.Size())\\n   576\u2192\\t\\t\\t// An empty list of hints will be treated as a preference that cannot be satisfied.\\n   577\u2192\\t\\t\\t// In definition of hints this is equal to: TopologyHint[NUMANodeAffinity: nil, Preferred: false].\\n   578\u2192\\t\\t\\t// For all but the best-effort policy, the Topology Manager will throw a pod-admission error.\\n   579\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   580\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tstring(v1.ResourceCPU): {},\\n   581\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   582\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   583\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Regenerating TopologyHints for CPUs already allocated\\\")\\n   584\u2192\\t\\treturn map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   585\u2192\\t\\t\\tstring(v1.ResourceCPU): p.generateCPUTopologyHints(allocated, cpuset.CPUSet{}, requested),\\n   586\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   587\u2192\\t}\\n   588\u2192\\n   589\u2192\\t// Get a list of available CPUs.\\n   590\u2192\\tavailable := p.GetAvailableCPUs(s)\\n   591\u2192\\n   592\u2192\\t// Get a list of reusable CPUs (e.g. CPUs reused from initContainers).\\n   593\u2192\\t// It should be an empty CPUSet for a newly created pod.\\n   594\u2192\\treusable := p.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)]\\n   595\u2192\\n   596\u2192\\t// Generate hints.\\n   597\u2192\\tcpuHints := p.generateCPUTopologyHints(available, reusable, requested)\\n   598\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"TopologyHints generated\\\", \\\"cpuHints\\\", cpuHints)\\n   599\u2192\\n   600\u2192\\treturn map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   601\u2192\\t\\tstring(v1.ResourceCPU): cpuHints,\\n   602\u2192\\t}\\n   603\u2192}\\n   604\u2192\\n   605\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) GetPodTopologyHints(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   606\u2192\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"podUID\\\", pod.UID)\\n   607\u2192\\n   608\u2192\\t// Get a count of how many guaranteed CPUs have been requested by Pod.\\n   609\u2192\\trequested := p.podGuaranteedCPUs(logger, pod)\\n   610\u2192\\n   611\u2192\\t// Number of required CPUs is not an integer or a pod is not part of the Guaranteed QoS class.\\n   612\u2192\\t// It will be treated by the TopologyManager as having no preference and cause it to ignore this\\n   613\u2192\\t// resource when considering pod alignment.\\n   614\u2192\\t// In terms of hints, this is equal to: TopologyHints[NUMANodeAffinity: nil, Preferred: true].\\n   615\u2192\\tif requested == 0 {\\n   616\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   617\u2192\\t}\\n   618\u2192\\n   619\u2192\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(features.PodLevelResources) && resourcehelper.IsPodLevelResourcesSet(pod) {\\n   620\u2192\\t\\tlogger.V(3).Info(\\\"CPU Manager pod hint generation skipped, pod is using pod-level resources which are not supported by the static CPU manager policy\\\")\\n   621\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   622\u2192\\t}\\n   623\u2192\\n   624\u2192\\tassignedCPUs := cpuset.New()\\n   625\u2192\\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {\\n   626\u2192\\t\\tlogger_ := klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n   627\u2192\\n   628\u2192\\t\\trequestedByContainer := p.guaranteedCPUs(logger, pod, &container)\\n   629\u2192\\t\\t// Short circuit to regenerate the same hints if there are already\\n   630\u2192\\t\\t// guaranteed CPUs allocated to the Container. This might happen after a\\n   631\u2192\\t\\t// kubelet restart, for example.\\n   632\u2192\\t\\tif allocated, exists := s.GetCPUSet(string(pod.UID), container.Name); exists {\\n   633\u2192\\t\\t\\tif allocated.Size() != requestedByContainer {\\n   634\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger_.Info(\\\"CPUs already allocated to container with different number than request\\\",
