\\\"allocatedSize\\\", requested, \\\"requestedByContainer\\\", requestedByContainer, \\\"allocatedSize\\\", allocated.Size())\\n   635\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// An empty list of hints will be treated as a preference that cannot be satisfied.\\n   636\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// In definition of hints this is equal to: TopologyHint[NUMANodeAffinity: nil, Preferred: false].\\n   637\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t// For all but the best-effort policy, the Topology Manager will throw a pod-admission error.\\n   638\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   639\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tstring(v1.ResourceCPU): {},\\n   640\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   641\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   642\u2192\\t\\t\\t// A set of CPUs already assigned to containers in this pod\\n   643\u2192\\t\\t\\tassignedCPUs = assignedCPUs.Union(allocated)\\n   644\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   645\u2192\\t}\\n   646\u2192\\tif assignedCPUs.Size() == requested {\\n   647\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Regenerating TopologyHints for CPUs already allocated\\\")\\n   648\u2192\\t\\treturn map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   649\u2192\\t\\t\\tstring(v1.ResourceCPU): p.generateCPUTopologyHints(assignedCPUs, cpuset.CPUSet{}, requested),\\n   650\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   651\u2192\\t}\\n   652\u2192\\n   653\u2192\\t// Get a list of available CPUs.\\n   654\u2192\\tavailable := p.GetAvailableCPUs(s)\\n   655\u2192\\n   656\u2192\\t// Get a list of reusable CPUs (e.g. CPUs reused from initContainers).\\n   657\u2192\\t// It should be an empty CPUSet for a newly created pod.\\n   658\u2192\\treusable := p.cpusToReuse[string(pod.UID)]\\n   659\u2192\\n   660\u2192\\t// Ensure any CPUs already assigned to containers in this pod are included as part of the hint generation.\\n   661\u2192\\treusable = reusable.Union(assignedCPUs)\\n   662\u2192\\n   663\u2192\\t// Generate hints.\\n   664\u2192\\tcpuHints := p.generateCPUTopologyHints(available, reusable, requested)\\n   665\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"TopologyHints generated\\\", \\\"cpuHints\\\", cpuHints)\\n   666\u2192\\n   667\u2192\\treturn map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   668\u2192\\t\\tstring(v1.ResourceCPU): cpuHints,\\n   669\u2192\\t}\\n   670\u2192}\\n   671\u2192\\n   672\u2192// generateCPUTopologyHints generates a set of TopologyHints given the set of\\n   673\u2192// available CPUs and the number of CPUs being requested.\\n   674\u2192//\\n   675\u2192// It follows the convention of marking all hints that have the same number of\\n   676\u2192// bits set as the narrowest matching NUMANodeAffinity with 'Preferred: true', and\\n   677\u2192// marking all others with 'Preferred: false'.\\n   678\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) generateCPUTopologyHints(availableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, reusableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, request int) []topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   679\u2192\\t// Initialize minAffinitySize to include all NUMA Nodes.\\n   680\u2192\\tminAffinitySize := p.topology.CPUDetails.NUMANodes().Size()\\n   681\u2192\\n   682\u2192\\t// Iterate through all combinations of numa nodes bitmask and build hints from them.\\n   683\u2192\\thints := []topologymanager.TopologyHint{}\\n   684\u2192\\tbitmask.IterateBitMasks(p.topology.CPUDetails.NUMANodes().List(), func(mask bitmask.BitMask) {\\n   685\u2192\\t\\t// First, update minAffinitySize for the current request size.\\n   686\u2192\\t\\tcpusInMask := p.topology.CPUDetails.CPUsInNUMANodes(mask.GetBits()...).Size()\\n   687\u2192\\t\\tif cpusInMask >= request && mask.Count() < minAffinitySize {\\n   688\u2192\\t\\t\\tminAffinitySize = mask.Count()\\n   689\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   690\u2192\\n   691\u2192\\t\\t// Then check to see if we have enough CPUs available on the current\\n   692\u2192\\t\\t// numa node bitmask to satisfy the CPU request.\\n   693\u2192\\t\\tnumMatching := 0\\n   694\u2192\\t\\tfor _, c := range reusableCPUs.List() {\\n   695\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Disregard this mask if its NUMANode isn't part of it.\\n   696\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !mask.IsSet(p.topology.CPUDetails[c].NUMANodeID) {\\n   697\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   698\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   699\u2192\\t\\t\\tnumMatching++\\n   700\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   701\u2192\\n   702\u2192\\t\\t// Finally, check to see if enough available CPUs remain on the current\\n   703\u2192\\t\\t// NUMA node combination to satisfy the CPU request.\\n   704\u2192\\t\\tfor _, c := range availableCPUs.List() {\\n   705\u2192\\t\\t\\tif mask.IsSet(p.topology.CPUDetails[c].NUMANodeID) {\\n   706\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tnumMatching++\\n   707\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   708\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   709\u2192\\n   710\u2192\\t\\t// If they don't, then move onto the next combination.\\n   711\u2192\\t\\tif numMatching < request {\\n   712\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   713\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   714\u2192\\n   715\u2192\\t\\t// Otherwise, create a new hint from the numa node bitmask and add it to the\\n   716\u2192\\t\\t// list of hints.  We set all hint preferences to 'false' on the first\\n   717\u2192\\t\\t// pass through.\\n   718\u2192\\t\\thints = append(hints, topologymanager.TopologyHint{\\n   719\u2192\\t\\t\\tNUMANodeAffinity: mask,\\n   720\u2192\\t\\t\\tPreferred:        false,\\n   721\u2192\\t\\t})\\n   722\u2192\\t})\\n   723\u2192\\n   724\u2192\\t// Loop back through all hints and update the 'Preferred' field based on\\n   725\u2192\\t// counting the number of bits sets in the affinity mask and comparing it\\n   726\u2192\\t// to the minAffinitySize. Only those with an equal number of bits set (and\\n   727\u2192\\t// with a minimal set of numa nodes) will be considered preferred.\\n   728\u2192\\tfor i := range hints {\\n   729\u2192\\t\\tif p.options.AlignBySocket && p.isHintSocketAligned(hints[i], minAffinitySize) {\\n   730\u2192\\t\\t\\thints[i].Preferred = true\\n   731\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   732\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   733\u2192\\t\\tif hints[i].NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == minAffinitySize {\\n   734\u2192\\t\\t\\thints[i].Preferred = true\\n   735\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   736\u2192\\t}\\n   737\u2192\\n   738\u2192\\treturn hints\\n   739\u2192}\\n   740\u2192\\n   741\u2192// isHintSocketAligned function return true if numa nodes in hint are socket aligned.\\n   742\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) isHintSocketAligned(hint topologymanager.TopologyHint, minAffinitySize int) bool {\\n   743\u2192\\tnumaNodesBitMask := hint.NUMANodeAffinity.GetBits()\\n   744\u2192\\tnumaNodesPerSocket := p.topology.NumNUMANodes / p.topology.NumSockets\\n   745\u2192\\tif numaNodesPerSocket == 0 {\\n   746\u2192\\t\\treturn false\\n   747\u2192\\t}\\n   748\u2192\\t// minSockets refers to minimum number of socket required to satify allocation.\\n   749\u2192\\t// A hint is considered socket aligned if sockets across which numa nodes span is equal to minSockets\\n   750\u2192\\tminSockets := (minAffinitySize + numaNodesPerSocket - 1) / numaNodesPerSocket\\n   751\u2192\\treturn p.topology.CPUDetails.SocketsInNUMANodes(numaNodesBitMask...).Size() == minSockets\\n   752\u2192}\\n   753\u2192\\n   754\u2192// getAlignedCPUs return set of aligned CPUs based on numa affinity mask and configured policy options.\\n   755\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) getAlignedCPUs(numaAffinity bitmask.BitMask, allocatableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   756\u2192\\talignedCPUs := cpuset.New()\\n   757\u2192\\tnumaBits := numaAffinity.GetBits()\\n   758\u2192\\n   759\u2192\\t// If align-by-socket policy option is enabled, NUMA based hint is expanded to\\n   760\u2192\\t// socket aligned hint. It will ensure that first socket aligned available CPUs are\\n   761\u2192\\t// allocated before we try to find CPUs across socket to satisfy allocation request.\\n   762\u2192\\tif p.options.AlignBySocket {\\n   763\u2192\\t\\tsocketBits := p.topology.CPUDetails.SocketsInNUMANodes(numaBits...).UnsortedList()\\n   764\u2192\\t\\tfor _, socketID := range socketBits {\\n   765\u2192\\t\\t\\talignedCPUs = alignedCPUs.Union(allocatableCPUs.Intersection(p.topology.CPUDetails.CPUsInSockets(socketID)))\\n   766\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   767\u2192\\t\\treturn alignedCPUs\\n   768\u2192\\t}\\n   769\u2192\\n   770\u2192\\tfor _, numaNodeID := range numaBits {\\n   771\u2192\\t\\talignedCPUs = alignedCPUs.Union(allocatableCPUs.Intersection(p.topology.CPUDetails.CPUsInNUMANodes(numaNodeID)))\\n   772\u2192\\t}\\n   773\u2192\\n   774\u2192\\treturn alignedCPUs\\n   775\u2192}\\n   776\u2192\\n   777\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) initializeMetrics(logger logr.Logger, s state.State) {\\n   778\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerSharedPoolSizeMilliCores.Set(float64(p.GetAvailableCPUs(s).Size() * 1000))\\n   779\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResourcesFailure.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedPhysicalCPU).Add(0) // ensure the value exists\\n   780\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedPhysicalCPU).Add(0)        // ensure the value exists\\n   781\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedUncoreCache).Add(0)        // ensure the value exists\\n   782\u2192\\ttotalAssignedCPUs := getTotalAssignedExclusiveCPUs(s)\\n   783\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerExclusiveCPUsAllocationCount.Set(float64(totalAssignedCPUs.Size()))\\n   784\u2192\\tupdateAllocationPerNUMAMetric(logger, p.topology, totalAssignedCPUs)\\n   785\u2192}\\n   786\u2192\\n   787\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) updateMetricsOnAllocate(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, cpuAlloc topology.Allocation) {\\n   788\u2192\\tncpus := cpuAlloc.CPUs.Size()\\n   789\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerExclusiveCPUsAllocationCount.Add(float64(ncpus))\\n   790\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerSharedPoolSizeMilliCores.Add(float64(-ncpus * 1000))\\n   791\u2192\\tif cpuAlloc.Aligned.UncoreCache {\\n   792\u2192\\t\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedUncoreCache).Inc()\\n   793\u2192\\t}\\n   794\u2192\\ttotalAssignedCPUs := getTotalAssignedExclusiveCPUs(s)\\n   795\u2192\\tupdateAllocationPerNUMAMetric(logger, p.topology, totalAssignedCPUs)\\n   796\u2192}\\n   797\u2192\\n   798\u2192func (p *staticPolicy) updateMetricsOnRelease(logger logr.Logger, s state.State, cset cpuset.CPUSet) {\\n   799\u2192\\tncpus := cset.Size()\\n   800\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerExclusiveCPUsAllocationCount.Add(float64(-ncpus))\\n   801\u2192\\tmetrics.CPUManagerSharedPoolSizeMilliCores.Add(float64(ncpus * 1000))\\n   802\u2192\\ttotalAssignedCPUs := getTotalAssignedExclusiveCPUs(s)\\n   803\u2192\\tupdateAllocationPerNUMAMetric(logger, p.topology, totalAssignedCPUs.Difference(cset))\\n   804\u2192}\\n   805\u2192\\n   806\u2192func getTotalAssignedExclusiveCPUs(s state.State) cpuset.CPUSet {\\n   807\u2192\\ttotalAssignedCPUs := cpuset.New()\\n   808\u2192\\tfor _, assignment := range s.GetCPUAssignments() {\\n   809\u2192\\t\\tfor _, cset := range assignment {\\n   810\u2192\\t\\t\\ttotalAssignedCPUs = totalAssignedCPUs.Union(cset)\\n   811\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   812\u2192\\t}\\n   813\u2192\\treturn totalAssignedCPUs\\n   814\u2192}\\n   815\u2192\\n   816\u2192func updateAllocationPerNUMAMetric(logger logr.Logger, topo *topology.CPUTopology, allocatedCPUs cpuset.CPUSet) {\\n   817\u2192\\tnumaCount := make(map[int]int)\\n   818\u2192\\n   819\u2192\\t// Count CPUs allocated per NUMA node\\n   820\u2192\\tfor _, cpuID := range allocatedCPUs.UnsortedList() {\\n   821\u2192\\t\\tnumaNode, err := topo.CPUNUMANodeID(cpuID)\\n   822\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   823\u2192\\t\\t\\t//NOTE: We are logging the error but it is highly unlikely to happen as the CPUset\\n   824\u2192\\t\\t\\t//      is already computed, evaluated and there is no room for user tampering.\\n   825\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Unable to determine NUMA node\\\", \\\"cpuID\\\", cpuID)\\n   826\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   827\u2192\\t\\tnumaCount[numaNode]++\\n   828\u2192\\t}\\n   829\u2192\\n   830\u2192\\t// Update metric\\n   831\u2192\\tfor numaNode, count := range numaCount {\\n   832\u2192\\t\\tmetrics.CPUManagerAllocationPerNUMA.WithLabelValues(strconv.Itoa(numaNode)).Set(float64(count))\\n   833\u2192\\t}\\n   834\u2192}\\n   835\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"1b6a6f58-fb99-4b77-b371-e52ed3b9d57a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:09.662Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"dd62f649-0b97-499d-a15f-fe4d85911f30\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1b6a6f58-fb99-4b77-b371-e52ed3b9d57a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01XNnkn5owh1kJ7oC9psvAB9\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now let me read the CPU state management files:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":24951,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13513,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":24951},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GQRJ2dyCoZh7cH3Jk\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"75e69106-1e05-4134-9d78-ee3a5893bf00\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:11.781Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"75e69106-1e05-4134-9d78-ee3a5893bf00\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01XNnkn5owh1kJ7oC9psvAB9\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Rnbh3iWKSMvXGzuHrMSqvE\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/state/state.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":24951,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13513,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":24951},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GQRJ2dyCoZh7cH3Jk\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"9d900e03-f832-4a29-8786-67bf88033bc1\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.115Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9d900e03-f832-4a29-8786-67bf88033bc1\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Rnbh3iWKSMvXGzuHrMSqvE\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Rnbh3iWKSMvXGzuHrMSqvE\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.118Z\",\"uuid\":\"deaadc31-61f7-484d-81ec-24e8bfdd62cb\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9d900e03-f832-4a29-8786-67bf88033bc1\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Rnbh3iWKSMvXGzuHrMSqvE\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package state\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    21\u2192)\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192// ContainerCPUAssignments type used in cpu manager state\\n    24\u2192type ContainerCPUAssignments map[string]map[string]cpuset.CPUSet\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192// Clone returns a copy of ContainerCPUAssignments\\n    27\u2192func (as ContainerCPUAssignments) Clone() ContainerCPUAssignments {\\n    28\u2192\\tret := make(ContainerCPUAssignments, len(as))\\n    29\u2192\\tfor pod := range as {\\n    30\u2192\\t\\tret[pod] = make(map[string]cpuset.CPUSet, len(as[pod]))\\n    31\u2192\\t\\tfor container, cset := range as[pod] {\\n    32\u2192\\t\\t\\tret[pod][container] = cset\\n    33\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    34\u2192\\t}\\n    35\u2192\\treturn ret\\n    36\u2192}\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192// Reader interface used to read current cpu/pod assignment state\\n    39\u2192type Reader interface {\\n    40\u2192\\tGetCPUSet(podUID string, containerName string) (cpuset.CPUSet, bool)\\n    41\u2192\\tGetDefaultCPUSet() cpuset.CPUSet\\n    42\u2192\\tGetCPUSetOrDefault(podUID string, containerName string) cpuset.CPUSet\\n    43\u2192\\tGetCPUAssignments() ContainerCPUAssignments\\n    44\u2192}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192type writer interface {\\n    47\u2192\\tSetCPUSet(podUID string, containerName string, cpuset cpuset.CPUSet)\\n    48\u2192\\tSetDefaultCPUSet(cpuset cpuset.CPUSet)\\n    49\u2192\\tSetCPUAssignments(ContainerCPUAssignments)\\n    50\u2192\\tDelete(podUID string, containerName string)\\n    51\u2192\\tClearState()\\n    52\u2192}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192// State interface provides methods for tracking and setting cpu/pod assignment\\n    55\u2192type State interface {\\n    56\u2192\\tReader\\n    57\u2192\\twriter\\n    58\u2192}\\n    59\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"2ad183b7-5ee5-41d5-a4b5-f8db21087f72\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.118Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"9d900e03-f832-4a29-8786-67bf88033bc1\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"2ad183b7-5ee5-41d5-a4b5-f8db21087f72\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01XNnkn5owh1kJ7oC9psvAB9\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01EFEP2WrBe4uSrpDn1KTGxw\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/cpu_assignment.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":24951,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13513,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":24951},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GQRJ2dyCoZh7cH3Jk\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"7298f48c-81a5-467f-973b-1cb8a557bb4c\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.351Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7298f48c-81a5-467f-973b-1cb8a557bb4c\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01EFEP2WrBe4uSrpDn1KTGxw\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01EFEP2WrBe4uSrpDn1KTGxw\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.547Z\",\"uuid\":\"1ef916d1-5dec-4505-a148-300413496648\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"7298f48c-81a5-467f-973b-1cb8a557bb4c\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01EFEP2WrBe4uSrpDn1KTGxw\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package cpumanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"maps\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"math\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/go-logr/logr\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/cpumanager/topology\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/cpuset\\\"\\n    29\u2192)\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192// LoopControl controls the behavior of the cpu accumulator loop logic\\n    32\u2192type LoopControl int\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192// Possible loop control outcomes\\n    35\u2192const (\\n    36\u2192\\tContinue LoopControl = iota\\n    37\u2192\\tBreak\\n    38\u2192)\\n    39\u2192\\n    40\u2192type mapIntInt map[int]int\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192func (m mapIntInt) Clone() mapIntInt {\\n    43\u2192\\treturn maps.Clone(m)\\n    44\u2192}\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192func (m mapIntInt) Keys() []int {\\n    47\u2192\\tvar keys []int\\n    48\u2192\\tfor k := range m {\\n    49\u2192\\t\\tkeys = append(keys, k)\\n    50\u2192\\t}\\n    51\u2192\\treturn keys\\n    52\u2192}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192func (m mapIntInt) Values(keys ...int) []int {\\n    55\u2192\\tif keys == nil {\\n    56\u2192\\t\\tkeys = m.Keys()\\n    57\u2192\\t}\\n    58\u2192\\tvar values []int\\n    59\u2192\\tfor _, k := range keys {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\tvalues = append(values, m[k])\\n    61\u2192\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\treturn values\\n    63\u2192}\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192func sum(xs []int) int {\\n    66\u2192\\tvar s int\\n    67\u2192\\tfor _, x := range xs {\\n    68\u2192\\t\\ts += x\\n    69\u2192\\t}\\n    70\u2192\\treturn s\\n    71\u2192}\\n    72\u2192\\n    73\u2192func mean(xs []int) float64 {\\n    74\u2192\\tvar sum float64\\n    75\u2192\\tfor _, x := range xs {\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tsum += float64(x)\\n    77\u2192\\t}\\n    78\u2192\\tm := sum / float64(len(xs))\\n    79\u2192\\treturn math.Round(m*1000) / 1000\\n    80\u2192}\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192func standardDeviation(xs []int) float64 {\\n    83\u2192\\tm := mean(xs)\\n    84\u2192\\tvar sum float64\\n    85\u2192\\tfor _, x := range xs {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tsum += (float64(x) - m) * (float64(x) - m)\\n    87\u2192\\t}\\n    88\u2192\\ts := math.Sqrt(sum / float64(len(xs)))\\n    89\u2192\\treturn math.Round(s*1000) / 1000\\n    90\u2192}\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192type numaOrSocketsFirstFuncs interface {\\n    93\u2192\\ttakeFullFirstLevel()\\n    94\u2192\\ttakeFullSecondLevel()\\n    95\u2192\\tsortAvailableNUMANodes() []int\\n    96\u2192\\tsortAvailableSockets() []int\\n    97\u2192\\tsortAvailableCores() []int\\n    98\u2192}\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192type numaFirst struct{ acc *cpuAccumulator }\\n   101\u2192type socketsFirst struct{ acc *cpuAccumulator }\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192var _ numaOrSocketsFirstFuncs = (*numaFirst)(nil)\\n   104\u2192var _ numaOrSocketsFirstFuncs = (*socketsFirst)(nil)\\n   105\u2192\\n   106\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, then we take\\n   107\u2192// from the set of NUMA Nodes as the first level.\\n   108\u2192func (n *numaFirst) takeFullFirstLevel() {\\n   109\u2192\\tn.acc.takeFullNUMANodes()\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, then we take\\n   113\u2192// from the set of sockets as the second level.\\n   114\u2192func (n *numaFirst) takeFullSecondLevel() {\\n   115\u2192\\tn.acc.takeFullSockets()\\n   116\u2192}\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192// Sort the UncoreCaches within the NUMA nodes.\\n   119\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sortAvailableUncoreCaches() []int {\\n   120\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   121\u2192\\tfor _, numa := range a.sortAvailableNUMANodes() {\\n   122\u2192\\t\\tuncore := a.details.UncoreInNUMANodes(numa).UnsortedList()\\n   123\u2192\\t\\ta.sort(uncore, a.details.CPUsInUncoreCaches)\\n   124\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, uncore...)\\n   125\u2192\\t}\\n   126\u2192\\treturn result\\n   127\u2192}\\n   128\u2192\\n   129\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, then just\\n   130\u2192// sort the NUMA nodes directly, and return them.\\n   131\u2192func (n *numaFirst) sortAvailableNUMANodes() []int {\\n   132\u2192\\tnumas := n.acc.details.NUMANodes().UnsortedList()\\n   133\u2192\\tn.acc.sort(numas, n.acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes)\\n   134\u2192\\treturn numas\\n   135\u2192}\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, then we need\\n   138\u2192// to pull the set of sockets out of each sorted NUMA node, and accumulate the\\n   139\u2192// partial order across them.\\n   140\u2192func (n *numaFirst) sortAvailableSockets() []int {\\n   141\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   142\u2192\\tfor _, numa := range n.sortAvailableNUMANodes() {\\n   143\u2192\\t\\tsockets := n.acc.details.SocketsInNUMANodes(numa).UnsortedList()\\n   144\u2192\\t\\tn.acc.sort(sockets, n.acc.details.CPUsInSockets)\\n   145\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, sockets...)\\n   146\u2192\\t}\\n   147\u2192\\treturn result\\n   148\u2192}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, then\\n   151\u2192// cores sit directly below sockets in the memory hierarchy.\\n   152\u2192func (n *numaFirst) sortAvailableCores() []int {\\n   153\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   154\u2192\\tfor _, socket := range n.acc.sortAvailableSockets() {\\n   155\u2192\\t\\tcores := n.acc.details.CoresInSockets(socket).UnsortedList()\\n   156\u2192\\t\\tn.acc.sort(cores, n.acc.details.CPUsInCores)\\n   157\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, cores...)\\n   158\u2192\\t}\\n   159\u2192\\treturn result\\n   160\u2192}\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, then we take\\n   163\u2192// from the set of sockets as the first level.\\n   164\u2192func (s *socketsFirst) takeFullFirstLevel() {\\n   165\u2192\\ts.acc.takeFullSockets()\\n   166\u2192}\\n   167\u2192\\n   168\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, then we take\\n   169\u2192// from the set of NUMA Nodes as the second level.\\n   170\u2192func (s *socketsFirst) takeFullSecondLevel() {\\n   171\u2192\\ts.acc.takeFullNUMANodes()\\n   172\u2192}\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, then we need\\n   175\u2192// to pull the set of NUMA nodes out of each sorted Socket, and accumulate the\\n   176\u2192// partial order across them.\\n   177\u2192func (s *socketsFirst) sortAvailableNUMANodes() []int {\\n   178\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   179\u2192\\tfor _, socket := range s.sortAvailableSockets() {\\n   180\u2192\\t\\tnumas := s.acc.details.NUMANodesInSockets(socket).UnsortedList()\\n   181\u2192\\t\\ts.acc.sort(numas, s.acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes)\\n   182\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, numas...)\\n   183\u2192\\t}\\n   184\u2192\\treturn result\\n   185\u2192}\\n   186\u2192\\n   187\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, then just\\n   188\u2192// sort the sockets directly, and return them.\\n   189\u2192func (s *socketsFirst) sortAvailableSockets() []int {\\n   190\u2192\\tsockets := s.acc.details.Sockets().UnsortedList()\\n   191\u2192\\ts.acc.sort(sockets, s.acc.details.CPUsInSockets)\\n   192\u2192\\treturn sockets\\n   193\u2192}\\n   194\u2192\\n   195\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, then cores\\n   196\u2192// sit directly below NUMA Nodes in the memory hierarchy.\\n   197\u2192func (s *socketsFirst) sortAvailableCores() []int {\\n   198\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   199\u2192\\tfor _, numa := range s.acc.sortAvailableNUMANodes() {\\n   200\u2192\\t\\tcores := s.acc.details.CoresInNUMANodes(numa).UnsortedList()\\n   201\u2192\\t\\ts.acc.sort(cores, s.acc.details.CPUsInCores)\\n   202\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, cores...)\\n   203\u2192\\t}\\n   204\u2192\\treturn result\\n   205\u2192}\\n   206\u2192\\n   207\u2192type availableCPUSorter interface {\\n   208\u2192\\tsort() []int\\n   209\u2192}\\n   210\u2192\\n   211\u2192type sortCPUsPacked struct{ acc *cpuAccumulator }\\n   212\u2192type sortCPUsSpread struct{ acc *cpuAccumulator }\\n   213\u2192\\n   214\u2192var _ availableCPUSorter = (*sortCPUsPacked)(nil)\\n   215\u2192var _ availableCPUSorter = (*sortCPUsSpread)(nil)\\n   216\u2192\\n   217\u2192func (s sortCPUsPacked) sort() []int {\\n   218\u2192\\treturn s.acc.sortAvailableCPUsPacked()\\n   219\u2192}\\n   220\u2192\\n   221\u2192func (s sortCPUsSpread) sort() []int {\\n   222\u2192\\treturn s.acc.sortAvailableCPUsSpread()\\n   223\u2192}\\n   224\u2192\\n   225\u2192// CPUSortingStrategy describes the CPU sorting solution within the socket scope.\\n   226\u2192// Using topoDualSocketHT (12 CPUs, 2 sockets, 6 cores) as an example:\\n   227\u2192//\\n   228\u2192//\\tCPUDetails: map[int]topology.CPUInfo{\\n   229\u2192//\\t\\t0:  {CoreID: 0, SocketID: 0, NUMANodeID: 0},\\n   230\u2192//\\t\\t1:  {CoreID: 1, SocketID: 1, NUMANodeID: 1},\\n   231\u2192//\\t\\t2:  {CoreID: 2, SocketID: 0, NUMANodeID: 0},\\n   232\u2192//\\t\\t3:  {CoreID: 3, SocketID: 1, NUMANodeID: 1},\\n   233\u2192//\\t\\t4:  {CoreID: 4, SocketID: 0, NUMANodeID: 0},\\n   234\u2192//\\t\\t5:  {CoreID: 5, SocketID: 1, NUMANodeID: 1},\\n   235\u2192//\\t\\t6:  {CoreID: 0, SocketID: 0, NUMANodeID: 0},\\n   236\u2192//\\t\\t7:  {CoreID: 1, SocketID: 1, NUMANodeID: 1},\\n   237\u2192//\\t\\t8:  {CoreID: 2, SocketID: 0, NUMANodeID: 0},\\n   238\u2192//\\t\\t9:  {CoreID: 3, SocketID: 1, NUMANodeID: 1},\\n   239\u2192//\\t\\t10: {CoreID: 4, SocketID: 0, NUMANodeID: 0},\\n   240\u2192//\\t\\t11: {CoreID: 5, SocketID: 1, NUMANodeID: 1},\\n   241\u2192//\\t},\\n   242\u2192//\\n   243\u2192// - CPUSortingOptionPacked sorts CPUs in a packed manner, where CPUs are grouped by core\\n   244\u2192// before moving to the next core, resulting in packed cores, like:\\n   245\u2192// 0, 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11\\n   246\u2192// - CPUSortingOptionSpread sorts CPUs in a spread manner, where CPUs are spread across cores\\n   247\u2192// before moving to the next CPU, resulting in spread-out cores, like:\\n   248\u2192// 0, 6, 2, 8, 4, 10, 1, 7, 3, 9, 5, 11\\n   249\u2192//\\n   250\u2192// By default, CPUSortingOptionPacked will be used, and CPUSortingOptionSpread will only be activated\\n   251\u2192// when the user specifies the `DistributeCPUsAcrossCoresOption` static policy option.\\n   252\u2192type CPUSortingStrategy string\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192const (\\n   255\u2192\\tCPUSortingStrategyPacked CPUSortingStrategy = \\\"packed\\\"\\n   256\u2192\\tCPUSortingStrategySpread CPUSortingStrategy = \\\"spread\\\"\\n   257\u2192)\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192type cpuAccumulator struct {\\n   260\u2192\\t// `logger` is the logger instance the cpuAccumulator is going to be use. We store internally\\n   261\u2192\\t// because the expected lifetime of a cpuAccumulator object is fully contained in a\\n   262\u2192\\t// `takeByTopology` call. Hence there's no functional difference between storing it or\\n   263\u2192\\t// getting repeatedly from the calls. Furthermore, this way we can keep the API surface smaller\\n   264\u2192\\t// (less parameters).\\n   265\u2192\\tlogger logr.Logger\\n   266\u2192\\n   267\u2192\\t// `topo` describes the layout of CPUs (i.e. hyper-threads if hyperthreading is on) between\\n   268\u2192\\t// cores (i.e. physical CPUs if hyper-threading is on), NUMA nodes, and sockets on the K8s\\n   269\u2192\\t// cluster node. `topo` is never mutated, meaning that as the cpuAccumulator claims CPUs topo is\\n   270\u2192\\t// not modified. Its primary purpose is being a reference of the original (i.e. at the time the\\n   271\u2192\\t// cpuAccumulator was created) topology to learn things such as how many CPUs are on each\\n   272\u2192\\t// socket, NUMA node, etc... .\\n   273\u2192\\ttopo *topology.CPUTopology\\n   274\u2192\\n   275\u2192\\t// `details` is the set of free CPUs that the cpuAccumulator can claim to accumulate the desired\\n   276\u2192\\t// number of CPUS. When a CPU is claimed, it's removed from `details`.\\n   277\u2192\\tdetails topology.CPUDetails\\n   278\u2192\\n   279\u2192\\t// `numCPUsNeeded` is the number of CPUs that the accumulator still needs to accumulate to reach\\n   280\u2192\\t// the desired number of CPUs. When the cpuAccumulator is created, `numCPUsNeeded` is set to the\\n   281\u2192\\t// total number of CPUs to accumulate. Every time a CPU is claimed, `numCPUsNeeded` is decreased\\n   282\u2192\\t// by 1 until it has value 0, meaning that all the needed CPUs have been accumulated\\n   283\u2192\\t// (success), or a situation where it's bigger than 0 but no more CPUs are available is reached\\n   284\u2192\\t// (failure).\\n   285\u2192\\tnumCPUsNeeded int\\n   286\u2192\\n   287\u2192\\t// `result` is the set of CPUs that have been accumulated so far. When a CPU is claimed, it's\\n   288\u2192\\t// added to `result`. The cpuAccumulator completed its duty successfully when `result` has\\n   289\u2192\\t// cardinality equal to the total number of CPUs to accumulate.\\n   290\u2192\\tresult cpuset.CPUSet\\n   291\u2192\\n   292\u2192\\tnumaOrSocketsFirst numaOrSocketsFirstFuncs\\n   293\u2192\\n   294\u2192\\t// availableCPUSorter is used to control the cpu sorting result.\\n   295\u2192\\t// The sequence of returned CPU IDs depends on the policy.\\n   296\u2192\\t// By default, cpus is sorted by sortAvailableCPUsPacked()\\n   297\u2192\\t// If packed is false, cpu is sorted by sortAvailableCPUsSpread()\\n   298\u2192\\tavailableCPUSorter availableCPUSorter\\n   299\u2192}\\n   300\u2192\\n   301\u2192func newCPUAccumulator(logger logr.Logger, topo *topology.CPUTopology, availableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, numCPUs int, cpuSortingStrategy CPUSortingStrategy) *cpuAccumulator {\\n   302\u2192\\tacc := &cpuAccumulator{\\n   303\u2192\\t\\tlogger:        logger,\\n   304\u2192\\t\\ttopo:          topo,\\n   305\u2192\\t\\tdetails:       topo.CPUDetails.KeepOnly(availableCPUs),\\n   306\u2192\\t\\tnumCPUsNeeded: numCPUs,\\n   307\u2192\\t\\tresult:        cpuset.New(),\\n   308\u2192\\t}\\n   309\u2192\\n   310\u2192\\tif topo.NumSockets >= topo.NumNUMANodes {\\n   311\u2192\\t\\tacc.numaOrSocketsFirst = &numaFirst{acc}\\n   312\u2192\\t} else {\\n   313\u2192\\t\\tacc.numaOrSocketsFirst = &socketsFirst{acc}\\n   314\u2192\\t}\\n   315\u2192\\n   316\u2192\\tif cpuSortingStrategy == CPUSortingStrategyPacked {\\n   317\u2192\\t\\tacc.availableCPUSorter = &sortCPUsPacked{acc}\\n   318\u2192\\t} else {\\n   319\u2192\\t\\tacc.availableCPUSorter = &sortCPUsSpread{acc}\\n   320\u2192\\t}\\n   321\u2192\\n   322\u2192\\treturn acc\\n   323\u2192}\\n   324\u2192\\n   325\u2192// Returns true if the supplied NUMANode is fully available in `a.details`.\\n   326\u2192// \\\"fully available\\\" means that all the CPUs in it are free.\\n   327\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) isNUMANodeFree(numaID int) bool {\\n   328\u2192\\treturn a.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numaID).Size() == a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUsInNUMANodes(numaID).Size()\\n   329\u2192}\\n   330\u2192\\n   331\u2192// Returns true if the supplied socket is fully available in `a.details`.\\n   332\u2192// \\\"fully available\\\" means that all the CPUs in it are free.\\n   333\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) isSocketFree(socketID int) bool {\\n   334\u2192\\treturn a.details.CPUsInSockets(socketID).Size() == a.topo.CPUsPerSocket()\\n   335\u2192}\\n   336\u2192\\n   337\u2192// Returns true if the supplied UnCoreCache is fully available,\\n   338\u2192// \\\"fully available\\\" means that all the CPUs in it are free.\\n   339\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) isUncoreCacheFree(uncoreID int) bool {\\n   340\u2192\\treturn a.details.CPUsInUncoreCaches(uncoreID).Size() == a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUsInUncoreCaches(uncoreID).Size()\\n   341\u2192}\\n   342\u2192\\n   343\u2192// Returns true if the supplied core is fully available in `a.details`.\\n   344\u2192// \\\"fully available\\\" means that all the CPUs in it are free.\\n   345\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) isCoreFree(coreID int) bool {\\n   346\u2192\\treturn a.details.CPUsInCores(coreID).Size() == a.topo.CPUsPerCore()\\n   347\u2192}\\n   348\u2192\\n   349\u2192// Returns free NUMA Node IDs as a slice sorted by sortAvailableNUMANodes().\\n   350\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) freeNUMANodes() []int {\\n   351\u2192\\tfree := []int{}\\n   352\u2192\\tfor _, numa := range a.sortAvailableNUMANodes() {\\n   353\u2192\\t\\tif a.isNUMANodeFree(numa) {\\n   354\u2192\\t\\t\\tfree = append(free, numa)\\n   355\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   356\u2192\\t}\\n   357\u2192\\treturn free\\n   358\u2192}\\n   359\u2192\\n   360\u2192// Returns free socket IDs as a slice sorted by sortAvailableSockets().\\n   361\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) freeSockets() []int {\\n   362\u2192\\tfree := []int{}\\n   363\u2192\\tfor _, socket := range a.sortAvailableSockets() {\\n   364\u2192\\t\\tif a.isSocketFree(socket) {\\n   365\u2192\\t\\t\\tfree = append(free, socket)\\n   366\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   367\u2192\\t}\\n   368\u2192\\treturn free\\n   369\u2192}\\n   370\u2192\\n   371\u2192// Returns free UncoreCache IDs as a slice sorted by sortAvailableUnCoreCache().\\n   372\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) freeUncoreCache() []int {\\n   373\u2192\\tfree := []int{}\\n   374\u2192\\tfor _, uncore := range a.sortAvailableUncoreCaches() {\\n   375\u2192\\t\\tif a.isUncoreCacheFree(uncore) {\\n   376\u2192\\t\\t\\tfree = append(free, uncore)\\n   377\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   378\u2192\\t}\\n   379\u2192\\treturn free\\n   380\u2192}\\n   381\u2192\\n   382\u2192// Returns free core IDs as a slice sorted by sortAvailableCores().\\n   383\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) freeCores() []int {\\n   384\u2192\\tfree := []int{}\\n   385\u2192\\tfor _, core := range a.sortAvailableCores() {\\n   386\u2192\\t\\tif a.isCoreFree(core) {\\n   387\u2192\\t\\t\\tfree = append(free, core)\\n   388\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   389\u2192\\t}\\n   390\u2192\\treturn free\\n   391\u2192}\\n   392\u2192\\n   393\u2192// Returns free CPU IDs as a slice sorted by sortAvailableCPUsPacked().\\n   394\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) freeCPUs() []int {\\n   395\u2192\\treturn a.availableCPUSorter.sort()\\n   396\u2192}\\n   397\u2192\\n   398\u2192// Sorts the provided list of NUMA nodes/sockets/cores/cpus referenced in 'ids'\\n   399\u2192// by the number of available CPUs contained within them (smallest to largest).\\n   400\u2192// The 'getCPU()' parameter defines the function that should be called to\\n   401\u2192// retrieve the list of available CPUs for the type being referenced. If two\\n   402\u2192// NUMA nodes/sockets/cores/cpus have the same number of available CPUs, they\\n   403\u2192// are sorted in ascending order by their id.\\n   404\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sort(ids []int, getCPUs func(ids ...int) cpuset.CPUSet) {\\n   405\u2192\\tsort.Slice(ids,\\n   406\u2192\\t\\tfunc(i, j int) bool {\\n   407\u2192\\t\\t\\tiCPUs := getCPUs(ids[i])\\n   408\u2192\\t\\t\\tjCPUs := getCPUs(ids[j])\\n   409\u2192\\t\\t\\tif iCPUs.Size() < jCPUs.Size() {\\n   410\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn true\\n   411\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   412\u2192\\t\\t\\tif iCPUs.Size() > jCPUs.Size() {\\n   413\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n   414\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   415\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn ids[i] < ids[j]\\n   416\u2192\\t\\t})\\n   417\u2192}\\n   418\u2192\\n   419\u2192// Sort all NUMA nodes with at least one free CPU.\\n   420\u2192//\\n   421\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher than sockets in the memory hierarchy, they are sorted by ascending number\\n   422\u2192// of free CPUs that they contain. \\\"higher than sockets in the memory hierarchy\\\" means that NUMA nodes\\n   423\u2192// contain a bigger number of CPUs (free and busy) than sockets, or equivalently that each NUMA node\\n   424\u2192// contains more than one socket.\\n   425\u2192//\\n   426\u2192// If instead NUMA nodes are lower in the memory hierarchy than sockets, they are sorted as follows.\\n   427\u2192// First, they are sorted by number of free CPUs in the sockets that contain them. Then, for each\\n   428\u2192// socket they are sorted by number of free CPUs that they contain. The order is always ascending.\\n   429\u2192// In other words, the relative order of two NUMA nodes is determined as follows:\\n   430\u2192//  1. If the two NUMA nodes belong to different sockets, the NUMA node in the socket with the\\n   431\u2192//     smaller amount of free CPUs appears first.\\n   432\u2192//  2. If the two NUMA nodes belong to the same socket, the NUMA node with the smaller amount of free\\n   433\u2192//     CPUs appears first.\\n   434\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sortAvailableNUMANodes() []int {\\n   435\u2192\\treturn a.numaOrSocketsFirst.sortAvailableNUMANodes()\\n   436\u2192}\\n   437\u2192\\n   438\u2192// Sort all sockets with at least one free CPU.\\n   439\u2192//\\n   440\u2192// If sockets are higher than NUMA nodes in the memory hierarchy, they are sorted by ascending number\\n   441\u2192// of free CPUs that they contain. \\\"higher than NUMA nodes in the memory hierarchy\\\" means that\\n   442\u2192// sockets contain a bigger number of CPUs (free and busy) than NUMA nodes, or equivalently that each\\n   443\u2192// socket contains more than one NUMA node.\\n   444\u2192//\\n   445\u2192// If instead sockets are lower in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, they are sorted as follows.\\n   446\u2192// First, they are sorted by number of free CPUs in the NUMA nodes that contain them. Then, for each\\n   447\u2192// NUMA node they are sorted by number of free CPUs that they contain. The order is always ascending.\\n   448\u2192// In other words, the relative order of two sockets is determined as follows:\\n   449\u2192//  1. If the two sockets belong to different NUMA nodes, the socket in the NUMA node with the\\n   450\u2192//     smaller amount of free CPUs appears first.\\n   451\u2192//  2. If the two sockets belong to the same NUMA node, the socket with the smaller amount of free\\n   452\u2192//     CPUs appears first.\\n   453\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sortAvailableSockets() []int {\\n   454\u2192\\treturn a.numaOrSocketsFirst.sortAvailableSockets()\\n   455\u2192}\\n   456\u2192\\n   457\u2192// Sort all cores with at least one free CPU.\\n   458\u2192//\\n   459\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, meaning that sockets contain a\\n   460\u2192// bigger number of CPUs (free and busy) than NUMA nodes, or equivalently that each socket contains\\n   461\u2192// more than one NUMA node, the cores are sorted as follows. First, they are sorted by number of\\n   462\u2192// free CPUs that their sockets contain. Then, for each socket, the cores in it are sorted by number\\n   463\u2192// of free CPUs that their NUMA nodes contain. Then, for each NUMA node, the cores in it are sorted\\n   464\u2192// by number of free CPUs that they contain. The order is always ascending. In other words, the\\n   465\u2192// relative order of two cores is determined as follows:\\n   466\u2192//  1. If the two cores belong to different sockets, the core in the socket with the smaller amount of\\n   467\u2192//     free CPUs appears first.\\n   468\u2192//  2. If the two cores belong to the same socket but different NUMA nodes, the core in the NUMA node\\n   469\u2192//     with the smaller amount of free CPUs appears first.\\n   470\u2192//  3. If the two cores belong to the same NUMA node and socket, the core with the smaller amount of\\n   471\u2192//     free CPUs appears first.\\n   472\u2192//\\n   473\u2192// If instead NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, the sorting happens in the\\n   474\u2192// same way as described in the previous paragraph, except that the priority of NUMA nodes and\\n   475\u2192// sockets is inverted (e.g. first sort the cores by number of free CPUs in their NUMA nodes, then,\\n   476\u2192// for each NUMA node, sort the cores by number of free CPUs in their sockets, etc...).\\n   477\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sortAvailableCores() []int {\\n   478\u2192\\treturn a.numaOrSocketsFirst.sortAvailableCores()\\n   479\u2192}\\n   480\u2192\\n   481\u2192// Sort all free CPUs.\\n   482\u2192//\\n   483\u2192// If sockets are higher in the memory hierarchy than NUMA nodes, meaning that sockets contain a\\n   484\u2192// bigger number of CPUs (free and busy) than NUMA nodes, or equivalently that each socket contains\\n   485\u2192// more than one NUMA node, the CPUs are sorted as follows. First, they are sorted by number of\\n   486\u2192// free CPUs that their sockets contain. Then, for each socket, the CPUs in it are sorted by number\\n   487\u2192// of free CPUs that their NUMA nodes contain. Then, for each NUMA node, the CPUs in it are sorted\\n   488\u2192// by number of free CPUs that their cores contain. Finally, for each core, the CPUs in it are\\n   489\u2192// sorted by numerical ID. The order is always ascending. In other words, the relative order of two\\n   490\u2192// CPUs is determined as follows:\\n   491\u2192//  1. If the two CPUs belong to different sockets, the CPU in the socket with the smaller amount of\\n   492\u2192//     free CPUs appears first.\\n   493\u2192//  2. If the two CPUs belong to the same socket but different NUMA nodes, the CPU in the NUMA node\\n   494\u2192//     with the smaller amount of free CPUs appears first.\\n   495\u2192//  3. If the two CPUs belong to the same socket and NUMA node but different cores, the CPU in the\\n   496\u2192//     core with the smaller amount of free CPUs appears first.\\n   497\u2192//  4. If the two CPUs belong to the same NUMA node, socket, and core, the CPU with the smaller ID\\n   498\u2192//     appears first.\\n   499\u2192//\\n   500\u2192// If instead NUMA nodes are higher in the memory hierarchy than sockets, the sorting happens in the\\n   501\u2192// same way as described in the previous paragraph, except that the priority of NUMA nodes and\\n   502\u2192// sockets is inverted (e.g. first sort the CPUs by number of free CPUs in their NUMA nodes, then,\\n   503\u2192// for each NUMA node, sort the CPUs by number of free CPUs in their sockets, etc...).\\n   504\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sortAvailableCPUsPacked() []int {\\n   505\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   506\u2192\\tfor _, core := range a.sortAvailableCores() {\\n   507\u2192\\t\\tcpus := a.details.CPUsInCores(core).UnsortedList()\\n   508\u2192\\t\\tsort.Ints(cpus)\\n   509\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, cpus...)\\n   510\u2192\\t}\\n   511\u2192\\treturn result\\n   512\u2192}\\n   513\u2192\\n   514\u2192// Sort all available CPUs:\\n   515\u2192// - First by core using sortAvailableSockets().\\n   516\u2192// - Then within each socket, sort cpus directly using the sort() algorithm defined above.\\n   517\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) sortAvailableCPUsSpread() []int {\\n   518\u2192\\tvar result []int\\n   519\u2192\\tfor _, socket := range a.sortAvailableSockets() {\\n   520\u2192\\t\\tcpus := a.details.CPUsInSockets(socket).UnsortedList()\\n   521\u2192\\t\\tsort.Ints(cpus)\\n   522\u2192\\t\\tresult = append(result, cpus...)\\n   523\u2192\\t}\\n   524\u2192\\treturn result\\n   525\u2192}\\n   526\u2192\\n   527\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) take(cpus cpuset.CPUSet) {\\n   528\u2192\\ta.result = a.result.Union(cpus)\\n   529\u2192\\ta.details = a.details.KeepOnly(a.details.CPUs().Difference(a.result))\\n   530\u2192\\ta.numCPUsNeeded -= cpus.Size()\\n   531\u2192}\\n   532\u2192\\n   533\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takeFullNUMANodes() {\\n   534\u2192\\tfor _, numa := range a.freeNUMANodes() {\\n   535\u2192\\t\\tcpusInNUMANode := a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa)\\n   536\u2192\\t\\tif !a.needsAtLeast(cpusInNUMANode.Size()) {\\n   537\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   538\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   539\u2192\\t\\ta.logger.V(4).Info(\\\"takeFullNUMANodes: claiming NUMA node\\\", \\\"numa\\\", numa)\\n   540\u2192\\t\\ta.take(cpusInNUMANode)\\n   541\u2192\\t}\\n   542\u2192}\\n   543\u2192\\n   544\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takeFullSockets() {\\n   545\u2192\\tfor _, socket := range a.freeSockets() {\\n   546\u2192\\t\\tcpusInSocket := a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUsInSockets(socket)\\n   547\u2192\\t\\tif !a.needsAtLeast(cpusInSocket.Size()) {\\n   548\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   549\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   550\u2192\\t\\ta.logger.V(4).Info(\\\"takeFullSockets: claiming socket\\\", \\\"socket\\\", socket)\\n   551\u2192\\t\\ta.take(cpusInSocket)\\n   552\u2192\\t}\\n   553\u2192}\\n   554\u2192\\n   555\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takeFullUncore() {\\n   556\u2192\\tfor _, uncore := range a.freeUncoreCache() {\\n   557\u2192\\t\\tcpusInUncore := a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUsInUncoreCaches(uncore)\\n   558\u2192\\t\\tif !a.needsAtLeast(cpusInUncore.Size()) {\\n   559\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   560\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   561\u2192\\t\\ta.logger.V(4).Info(\\\"takeFullUncore: claiming uncore\\\", \\\"uncore\\\", uncore)\\n   562\u2192\\t\\ta.take(cpusInUncore)\\n   563\u2192\\t}\\n   564\u2192}\\n   565\u2192\\n   566\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takePartialUncore(uncoreID int) {\\n   567\u2192\\t// determine the number of cores needed whether SMT/hyperthread is enabled or disabled\\n   568\u2192\\tnumCoresNeeded := (a.numCPUsNeeded + a.topo.CPUsPerCore() - 1) / a.topo.CPUsPerCore()\\n   569\u2192\\n   570\u2192\\t// determine the N number of free cores (physical cpus) within the UncoreCache, then\\n   571\u2192\\t// determine the M number of free cpus (virtual cpus) that correspond with the free cores\\n   572\u2192\\tfreeCores := a.details.CoresNeededInUncoreCache(numCoresNeeded, uncoreID)\\n   573\u2192\\tfreeCPUs := a.details.CPUsInCores(freeCores.UnsortedList()...)\\n   574\u2192\\n   575\u2192\\t// when SMT/hyperthread is enabled and remaining cpu requirement is an odd integer value:\\n   576\u2192\\t// sort the free CPUs that were determined based on the cores that have available cpus.\\n   577\u2192\\t// if the amount of free cpus is greather than the cpus needed, we can drop the last cpu\\n   578\u2192\\t// since the odd integer request will only require one out of the two free cpus that\\n   579\u2192\\t// correspond to the last core\\n   580\u2192\\tif a.numCPUsNeeded%2 != 0 && a.topo.CPUsPerCore() > 1 {\\n   581\u2192\\t\\t// we sort freeCPUs to ensure we pack virtual cpu allocations, meaning we allocate\\n   582\u2192\\t\\t// whole core's worth of cpus as much as possible to reduce smt-misalignment\\n   583\u2192\\t\\tsortFreeCPUs := freeCPUs.List()\\n   584\u2192\\t\\tif len(sortFreeCPUs) > a.numCPUsNeeded {\\n   585\u2192\\t\\t\\t// if we are in takePartialUncore, the accumulator is not satisfied after\\n   586\u2192\\t\\t\\t// takeFullUncore, so freeCPUs.Size() can't be < 1\\n   587\u2192\\t\\t\\tsortFreeCPUs = sortFreeCPUs[:freeCPUs.Size()-1]\\n   588\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   589\u2192\\t\\tfreeCPUs = cpuset.New(sortFreeCPUs...)\\n   590\u2192\\t}\\n   591\u2192\\n   592\u2192\\t// claim the cpus if the free cpus within the UncoreCache can satisfy the needed cpus\\n   593\u2192\\tclaimed := (a.numCPUsNeeded == freeCPUs.Size())\\n   594\u2192\\ta.logger.V(4).Info(\\\"takePartialUncore: trying to claim partial uncore\\\",\\n   595\u2192\\t\\t\\\"uncore\\\", uncoreID,\\n   596\u2192\\t\\t\\\"claimed\\\", claimed,\\n   597\u2192\\t\\t\\\"needed\\\", a.numCPUsNeeded,\\n   598\u2192\\t\\t\\\"cores\\\", freeCores.String(),\\n   599\u2192\\t\\t\\\"cpus\\\", freeCPUs.String())\\n   600\u2192\\tif !claimed {\\n   601\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   602\u2192\\n   603\u2192\\t}\\n   604\u2192\\ta.take(freeCPUs)\\n   605\u2192}\\n   606\u2192\\n   607\u2192// First try to take full UncoreCache, if available and need is at least the size of the UncoreCache group.\\n   608\u2192// Second try to take the partial UncoreCache if available and the request size can fit w/in the UncoreCache.\\n   609\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takeUncoreCache() {\\n   610\u2192\\tnumCPUsInUncore := a.topo.CPUsPerUncore()\\n   611\u2192\\tfor _, uncore := range a.sortAvailableUncoreCaches() {\\n   612\u2192\\t\\t// take full UncoreCache if the CPUs needed is greater than free UncoreCache size\\n   613\u2192\\t\\tif a.needsAtLeast(numCPUsInUncore) {\\n   614\u2192\\t\\t\\ta.takeFullUncore()\\n   615\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   616\u2192\\n   617\u2192\\t\\tif a.isSatisfied() {\\n   618\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   619\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   620\u2192\\n   621\u2192\\t\\t// take partial UncoreCache if the CPUs needed is less than free UncoreCache size\\n   622\u2192\\t\\ta.takePartialUncore(uncore)\\n   623\u2192\\t\\tif a.isSatisfied() {\\n   624\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   625\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   626\u2192\\t}\\n   627\u2192}\\n   628\u2192\\n   629\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takeFullCores() {\\n   630\u2192\\tfor _, core := range a.freeCores() {\\n   631\u2192\\t\\tcpusInCore := a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUsInCores(core)\\n   632\u2192\\t\\tif !a.needsAtLeast(cpusInCore.Size()) {\\n   633\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   634\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   635\u2192\\t\\ta.logger.V(4).Info(\\\"takeFullCores: claiming core\\\", \\\"core\\\", core)\\n   636\u2192\\t\\ta.take(cpusInCore)\\n   637\u2192\\t}\\n   638\u2192}\\n   639\u2192\\n   640\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) takeRemainingCPUs() {\\n   641\u2192\\tfor _, cpu := range a.availableCPUSorter.sort() {\\n   642\u2192\\t\\ta.logger.V(4).Info(\\\"takeRemainingCPUs: claiming CPU\\\", \\\"cpu\\\", cpu)\\n   643\u2192\\t\\ta.take(cpuset.New(cpu))\\n   644\u2192\\t\\tif a.isSatisfied() {\\n   645\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   646\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   647\u2192\\t}\\n   648\u2192}\\n   649\u2192\\n   650\u2192// rangeNUMANodesNeededToSatisfy returns minimum and maximum (in this order) number of NUMA nodes\\n   651\u2192// needed to satisfy the cpuAccumulator's goal of accumulating `a.numCPUsNeeded` CPUs, assuming that\\n   652\u2192// CPU groups have size given by the `cpuGroupSize` argument.\\n   653\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) rangeNUMANodesNeededToSatisfy(cpuGroupSize int) (minNumNUMAs, maxNumNUMAs int) {\\n   654\u2192\\t// Get the total number of NUMA nodes in the system.\\n   655\u2192\\tnumNUMANodes := a.topo.CPUDetails.NUMANodes().Size()\\n   656\u2192\\n   657\u2192\\t// Get the total number of NUMA nodes that have CPUs available on them.\\n   658\u2192\\tnumNUMANodesAvailable := a.details.NUMANodes().Size()\\n   659\u2192\\n   660\u2192\\t// Get the total number of CPUs in the system.\\n   661\u2192\\tnumCPUs := a.topo.CPUDetails.CPUs().Size()\\n   662\u2192\\n   663\u2192\\t// Get the total number of 'cpuGroups' in the system.\\n   664\u2192\\tnumCPUGroups := (numCPUs-1)/cpuGroupSize + 1\\n   665\u2192\\n   666\u2192\\t// Calculate the number of 'cpuGroups' per NUMA Node in the system (rounding up).\\n   667\u2192\\tnumCPUGroupsPerNUMANode := (numCPUGroups-1)/numNUMANodes + 1\\n   668\u2192\\n   669\u2192\\t// Calculate the number of available 'cpuGroups' across all NUMA nodes as\\n   670\u2192\\t// well as the number of 'cpuGroups' that need to be allocated (rounding up).\\n   671\u2192\\tnumCPUGroupsNeeded := (a.numCPUsNeeded-1)/cpuGroupSize + 1\\n   672\u2192\\n   673\u2192\\t// Calculate the minimum number of numa nodes required to satisfy the\\n   674\u2192\\t// allocation (rounding up).\\n   675\u2192\\tminNumNUMAs = (numCPUGroupsNeeded-1)/numCPUGroupsPerNUMANode + 1\\n   676\u2192\\n   677\u2192\\t// Calculate the maximum number of numa nodes required to satisfy the allocation.\\n   678\u2192\\tmaxNumNUMAs = min(numCPUGroupsNeeded, numNUMANodesAvailable)\\n   679\u2192\\n   680\u2192\\treturn\\n   681\u2192}\\n   682\u2192\\n   683\u2192// needsAtLeast returns true if and only if the accumulator needs at least `n` CPUs.\\n   684\u2192// This means that needsAtLeast returns true even if more than `n` CPUs are needed.\\n   685\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) needsAtLeast(n int) bool {\\n   686\u2192\\treturn a.numCPUsNeeded >= n\\n   687\u2192}\\n   688\u2192\\n   689\u2192// isSatisfied returns true if and only if the accumulator has all the CPUs it needs.\\n   690\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) isSatisfied() bool {\\n   691\u2192\\treturn a.numCPUsNeeded < 1\\n   692\u2192}\\n   693\u2192\\n   694\u2192// isFailed returns true if and only if there aren't enough available CPUs in the system.\\n   695\u2192// (e.g. the accumulator needs 4 CPUs but only 3 are available).\\n   696\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) isFailed() bool {\\n   697\u2192\\treturn a.numCPUsNeeded > a.details.CPUs().Size()\\n   698\u2192}\\n   699\u2192\\n   700\u2192// iterateCombinations walks through all n-choose-k subsets of size k in n and\\n   701\u2192// calls function 'f()' on each subset. For example, if n={0,1,2}, and k=2,\\n   702\u2192// then f() will be called on the subsets {0,1}, {0,2}. and {1,2}. If f() ever\\n   703\u2192// returns 'Break', we break early and exit the loop.\\n   704\u2192func (a *cpuAccumulator) iterateCombinations(n []int, k int, f func([]int) LoopControl) {\\n   705\u2192\\tif k < 1 {\\n   706\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   707\u2192\\t}\\n   708\u2192\\n   709\u2192\\tvar helper func(n []int, k int, start int, accum []int, f func([]int) LoopControl) LoopControl\\n   710\u2192\\thelper = func(n []int, k int, start int, accum []int, f func([]int) LoopControl) LoopControl {\\n   711\u2192\\t\\tif k == 0 {\\n   712\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn f(accum)\\n   713\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   714\u2192\\t\\tfor i := start; i <= len(n)-k; i++ {\\n   715\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontrol := helper(n, k-1, i+1, append(accum, n[i]), f)\\n   716\u2192\\t\\t\\tif control == Break {\\n   717\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Break\\n   718\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   719\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   720\u2192\\t\\treturn Continue\\n   721\u2192\\t}\\n   722\u2192\\n   723\u2192\\thelper(n, k, 0, []int{}, f)\\n   724\u2192}\\n   725\u2192\\n   726\u2192// takeByTopologyNUMAPacked returns a CPUSet containing `numCPUs` CPUs taken from the CPUs in the\\n   727\u2192// set `availableCPUs`. `topo` describes how the CPUs are arranged between sockets, NUMA nodes\\n   728\u2192// and physical cores (if hyperthreading is on a \\\"CPU\\\" is a thread rather than a full physical\\n   729\u2192// core).\\n   730\u2192//\\n   731\u2192// If sockets are higher than NUMA nodes in the memory hierarchy (i.e. a socket contains more than\\n   732\u2192// one NUMA node), the CPUs are selected as follows.\\n   733\u2192//\\n   734\u2192// If `numCPUs` is bigger than the total number of CPUs in a socket, and there are free (i.e. all\\n   735\u2192// CPUs in them are free) sockets, the function takes as many entire free sockets as possible.\\n   736\u2192// If there are no free sockets, or `numCPUs` is less than a whole socket, or the remaining number\\n   737\u2192// of CPUs to take after having taken some whole sockets is less than a whole socket, the function\\n   738\u2192// tries to take whole NUMA nodes.\\n   739\u2192//\\n   740\u2192// If the remaining number of CPUs to take is bigger than the total number of CPUs in a NUMA node,\\n   741\u2192// and there are free (i.e. all CPUs in them are free) NUMA nodes, the function takes as many entire\\n   742\u2192// free NUMA nodes as possible. The free NUMA nodes are taken from one socket at a time, and the\\n   743\u2192// sockets are considered by ascending order of free CPUs in them. If there are no free NUMA nodes,\\n   744\u2192// or the remaining number of CPUs to take after having taken full sockets and NUMA nodes is less\\n   745\u2192// than a whole NUMA node, the function tries to take whole physical cores (cores).\\n   746\u2192//\\n   747\u2192// If `PreferAlignByUncoreCache` is enabled, the function will try to optimally assign Uncorecaches.\\n   748\u2192// If `numCPUs` is larger than or equal to the total number of CPUs in a Uncorecache, and there are\\n   749\u2192// free (i.e. all CPUs within the Uncorecache are free) Uncorecaches, the function takes as many entire\\n   750\u2192// cores from free Uncorecaches as possible. If/Once `numCPUs` is smaller than the total number of\\n   751\u2192// CPUs in a free Uncorecache, the function scans each Uncorecache index in numerical order to assign\\n   752\u2192// cores that will fit within the Uncorecache. If `numCPUs` cannot fit within any Uncorecache, the\\n   753\u2192// function tries to take whole physical cores.\\n   754\u2192//\\n   755\u2192// If `numCPUs` is bigger than the total number of CPUs in a core, and there are\\n   756\u2192// free (i.e. all CPUs in them are free) cores, the function takes as many entire free cores as possible.\\n   757\u2192// The cores are taken from one socket at a time, and the sockets are considered by\\n   758\u2192// ascending order of free CPUs in them. For a given socket, the cores are taken one NUMA node at a time,\\n   759\u2192// and the NUMA nodes are considered by ascending order of free CPUs in them. If there are no free\\n   760\u2192// cores, or the remaining number of CPUs to take after having taken full sockets, NUMA nodes and\\n   761\u2192// cores is less than a whole core, the function tries to take individual CPUs.\\n   762\u2192//\\n   763\u2192// The individual CPUs are taken from one socket at a time, and the sockets are considered by\\n   764\u2192// ascending order of free CPUs in them. For a given socket, the CPUs are taken one NUMA node at a time,\\n   765\u2192// and the NUMA nodes are considered by ascending order of free CPUs in them. For a given NUMA node, the\\n   766\u2192// CPUs are taken one core at a time, and the core are considered by ascending order of free CPUs in them.\\n   767\u2192//\\n   768\u2192// If NUMA nodes are higher than Sockets in the memory hierarchy (i.e. a NUMA node contains more\\n   769\u2192// than one socket), the CPUs are selected as written above, with the only differences being that\\n   770\u2192// (1) the order with which full sockets and full NUMA nodes are acquired is swapped, and (2) the\\n   771\u2192// order with which lower-level topology elements are selected is also swapped accordingly. E.g.\\n   772\u2192// when selecting full cores, the cores are selected starting from the ones in the NUMA node with\\n   773\u2192// the least amount of free CPUs to the one with the highest amount of free CPUs (i.e. in ascending\\n   774\u2192// order of free CPUs). For any NUMA node, the cores are selected from the ones in the socket with\\n   775\u2192// the least amount of free CPUs to the one with the highest amount of free CPUs.\\n   776\u2192func takeByTopologyNUMAPacked(logger logr.Logger, topo *topology.CPUTopology, availableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, numCPUs int, cpuSortingStrategy CPUSortingStrategy, preferAlignByUncoreCache bool) (cpuset.CPUSet, error) {\\n   777\u2192\\tacc := newCPUAccumulator(logger, topo, availableCPUs, numCPUs, cpuSortingStrategy)\\n   778\u2192\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   779\u2192\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   780\u2192\\t}\\n   781\u2192\\tif acc.isFailed() {\\n   782\u2192\\t\\treturn cpuset.New(), fmt.Errorf(\\\"not enough cpus available to satisfy request: requested=%d, available=%d\\\", numCPUs, availableCPUs.Size())\\n   783\u2192\\t}\\n   784\u2192\\n   785\u2192\\t// Algorithm: topology-aware best-fit\\n   786\u2192\\t// 1. Acquire whole NUMA nodes and sockets, if available and the container\\n   787\u2192\\t//    requires at least a NUMA node or socket's-worth of CPUs. If NUMA\\n   788\u2192\\t//    Nodes map to 1 or more sockets, pull from NUMA nodes first.\\n   789\u2192\\t//    Otherwise pull from sockets first.\\n   790\u2192\\tacc.numaOrSocketsFirst.takeFullFirstLevel()\\n   791\u2192\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   792\u2192\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   793\u2192\\t}\\n   794\u2192\\tacc.numaOrSocketsFirst.takeFullSecondLevel()\\n   795\u2192\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   796\u2192\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   797\u2192\\t}\\n   798\u2192\\n   799\u2192\\t// 2. If PreferAlignByUncoreCache is enabled, acquire whole UncoreCaches\\n   800\u2192\\t//    if available and the container requires at least a UncoreCache's-worth\\n   801\u2192\\t//    of CPUs. Otherwise, acquire CPUs from the least amount of UncoreCaches.\\n   802\u2192\\tif preferAlignByUncoreCache {\\n   803\u2192\\t\\tacc.takeUncoreCache()\\n   804\u2192\\t\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   805\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   806\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   807\u2192\\t}\\n   808\u2192\\n   809\u2192\\t// 3. Acquire whole cores, if available and the container requires at least\\n   810\u2192\\t//    a core's-worth of CPUs.\\n   811\u2192\\t//    If `CPUSortingStrategySpread` is specified, skip taking the whole core.\\n   812\u2192\\tif cpuSortingStrategy != CPUSortingStrategySpread {\\n   813\u2192\\t\\tacc.takeFullCores()\\n   814\u2192\\t\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   815\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   816\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   817\u2192\\t}\\n   818\u2192\\n   819\u2192\\t// 4. Acquire single threads, preferring to fill partially-allocated cores\\n   820\u2192\\t//    on the same sockets as the whole cores we have already taken in this\\n   821\u2192\\t//    allocation.\\n   822\u2192\\tacc.takeRemainingCPUs()\\n   823\u2192\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   824\u2192\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   825\u2192\\t}\\n   826\u2192\\n   827\u2192\\treturn cpuset.New(), fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to allocate cpus\\\")\\n   828\u2192}\\n   829\u2192\\n   830\u2192// takeByTopologyNUMADistributed returns a CPUSet of size 'numCPUs'.\\n   831\u2192//\\n   832\u2192// It generates this CPUset by allocating CPUs from 'availableCPUs' according\\n   833\u2192// to the algorithm outlined in KEP-2902:\\n   834\u2192//\\n   835\u2192// https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/e7f51ffbe2ee398ffd1fba4a6d854f276bfad9fb/keps/sig-node/2902-cpumanager-distribute-cpus-policy-option\\n   836\u2192//\\n   837\u2192// This algorithm evenly distribute CPUs across NUMA nodes in cases where more\\n   838\u2192// than one NUMA node is required to satisfy the allocation. This is in\\n   839\u2192// contrast to the takeByTopologyNUMAPacked algorithm, which attempts to 'pack'\\n   840\u2192// CPUs onto NUMA nodes and fill them up before moving on to the next one.\\n   841\u2192//\\n   842\u2192// At a high-level this algorithm can be summarized as:\\n   843\u2192//\\n   844\u2192// For each NUMA single node:\\n   845\u2192//   - If all requested CPUs can be allocated from this NUMA node;\\n   846\u2192//     --> Do the allocation by running takeByTopologyNUMAPacked() over the\\n   847\u2192//     available CPUs in that NUMA node and return\\n   848\u2192//\\n   849\u2192// Otherwise, for each pair of NUMA nodes:\\n   850\u2192//   - If the set of requested CPUs (modulo 2) can be evenly split across\\n   851\u2192//     the 2 NUMA nodes; AND\\n   852\u2192//   - Any remaining CPUs (after the modulo operation) can be striped across\\n   853\u2192//     some subset of the NUMA nodes;\\n   854\u2192//     --> Do the allocation by running takeByTopologyNUMAPacked() over the\\n   855\u2192//     available CPUs in both NUMA nodes and return\\n   856\u2192//\\n   857\u2192// Otherwise, for each 3-tuple of NUMA nodes:\\n   858\u2192//   - If the set of requested CPUs (modulo 3) can be evenly distributed\\n   859\u2192//     across the 3 NUMA nodes; AND\\n   860\u2192//   - Any remaining CPUs (after the modulo operation) can be striped across\\n   861\u2192//     some subset of the NUMA nodes;\\n   862\u2192//     --> Do the allocation by running takeByTopologyNUMAPacked() over the\\n   863\u2192//     available CPUs in all three NUMA nodes and return\\n   864\u2192//\\n   865\u2192// ...\\n   866\u2192//\\n   867\u2192// Otherwise, for the set of all NUMA nodes:\\n   868\u2192//   - If the set of requested CPUs (modulo NUM_NUMA_NODES) can be evenly\\n   869\u2192//     distributed across all NUMA nodes; AND\\n   870\u2192//   - Any remaining CPUs (after the modulo operation) can be striped across\\n   871\u2192//     some subset of the NUMA nodes;\\n   872\u2192//     --> Do the allocation by running takeByTopologyNUMAPacked() over the\\n   873\u2192//     available CPUs in all NUMA nodes and return\\n   874\u2192//\\n   875\u2192// If none of the above conditions can be met, then resort back to a\\n   876\u2192// best-effort fit of packing CPUs into NUMA nodes by calling\\n   877\u2192// takeByTopologyNUMAPacked() over all available CPUs.\\n   878\u2192//\\n   879\u2192// NOTE: A \\\"balance score\\\" will be calculated to help find the best subset of\\n   880\u2192// NUMA nodes to allocate any 'remainder' CPUs from (in cases where the total\\n   881\u2192// number of CPUs to allocate cannot be evenly distributed across the chosen\\n   882\u2192// set of NUMA nodes). This \\\"balance score\\\" is calculated as the standard\\n   883\u2192// deviation of how many CPUs will be available on each NUMA node after all\\n   884\u2192// evenly distributed and remainder CPUs are allocated. The subset with the\\n   885\u2192// lowest \\\"balance score\\\" will receive the CPUs in order to keep the overall\\n   886\u2192// allocation of CPUs as \\\"balanced\\\" as possible.\\n   887\u2192//\\n   888\u2192// NOTE: This algorithm has been generalized to take an additional\\n   889\u2192// 'cpuGroupSize' parameter to ensure that CPUs are always allocated in groups\\n   890\u2192// of size 'cpuGroupSize' according to the algorithm described above. This is\\n   891\u2192// important, for example, to ensure that all CPUs (i.e. all hyperthreads) from\\n   892\u2192// a single core are allocated together.\\n   893\u2192func takeByTopologyNUMADistributed(logger logr.Logger, topo *topology.CPUTopology, availableCPUs cpuset.CPUSet, numCPUs int, cpuGroupSize int, cpuSortingStrategy CPUSortingStrategy) (cpuset.CPUSet, error) {\\n   894\u2192\\t// If the number of CPUs requested cannot be handed out in chunks of\\n   895\u2192\\t// 'cpuGroupSize', then we just call out the packing algorithm since we\\n   896\u2192\\t// can't distribute CPUs in this chunk size.\\n   897\u2192\\t// PreferAlignByUncoreCache feature not implemented here yet and set to false.\\n   898\u2192\\t// Support for PreferAlignByUncoreCache to be done at beta release.\\n   899\u2192\\tif (numCPUs % cpuGroupSize) != 0 {\\n   900\u2192\\t\\treturn takeByTopologyNUMAPacked(logger, topo, availableCPUs, numCPUs, cpuSortingStrategy, false)\\n   901\u2192\\t}\\n   902\u2192\\n   903\u2192\\t// Otherwise build an accumulator to start allocating CPUs from.\\n   904\u2192\\tacc := newCPUAccumulator(logger, topo, availableCPUs, numCPUs, cpuSortingStrategy)\\n   905\u2192\\tif acc.isSatisfied() {\\n   906\u2192\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n   907\u2192\\t}\\n   908\u2192\\tif acc.isFailed() {\\n   909\u2192\\t\\treturn cpuset.New(), fmt.Errorf(\\\"not enough cpus available to satisfy request: requested=%d, available=%d\\\", numCPUs, availableCPUs.Size())\\n   910\u2192\\t}\\n   911\u2192\\n   912\u2192\\t// Get the list of NUMA nodes represented by the set of CPUs in 'availableCPUs'.\\n   913\u2192\\tnumas := acc.sortAvailableNUMANodes()\\n   914\u2192\\n   915\u2192\\t// Calculate the minimum and maximum possible number of NUMA nodes that\\n   916\u2192\\t// could satisfy this request. This is used to optimize how many iterations\\n   917\u2192\\t// of the loop we need to go through below.\\n   918\u2192\\tminNUMAs, maxNUMAs := acc.rangeNUMANodesNeededToSatisfy(cpuGroupSize)\\n   919\u2192\\n   920\u2192\\t// Try combinations of 1,2,3,... NUMA nodes until we find a combination\\n   921\u2192\\t// where we can evenly distribute CPUs across them. To optimize things, we\\n   922\u2192\\t// don't always start at 1 and end at len(numas). Instead, we use the\\n   923\u2192\\t// values of 'minNUMAs' and 'maxNUMAs' calculated above.\\n   924\u2192\\tfor k := minNUMAs; k <= maxNUMAs; k++ {\\n   925\u2192\\t\\t// Iterate through the various n-choose-k NUMA node combinations,\\n   926\u2192\\t\\t// looking for the combination of NUMA nodes that can best have CPUs\\n   927\u2192\\t\\t// distributed across them.\\n   928\u2192\\t\\tvar bestBalance float64 = math.MaxFloat64\\n   929\u2192\\t\\tvar bestRemainder []int = nil\\n   930\u2192\\t\\tvar bestCombo []int = nil\\n   931\u2192\\t\\tacc.iterateCombinations(numas, k, func(combo []int) LoopControl {\\n   932\u2192\\t\\t\\t// If we've already found a combo with a balance of 0 in a\\n   933\u2192\\t\\t\\t// different iteration, then don't bother checking any others.\\n   934\u2192\\t\\t\\tif bestBalance == 0 {\\n   935\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Break\\n   936\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   937\u2192\\n   938\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Check that this combination of NUMA nodes has enough CPUs to\\n   939\u2192\\t\\t\\t// satisfy the allocation overall.\\n   940\u2192\\t\\t\\tcpus := acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(combo...)\\n   941\u2192\\t\\t\\tif cpus.Size() < numCPUs {\\n   942\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Continue\\n   943\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   944\u2192\\n   945\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Check that CPUs can be handed out in groups of size\\n   946\u2192\\t\\t\\t// 'cpuGroupSize' across the NUMA nodes in this combo.\\n   947\u2192\\t\\t\\tnumCPUGroups := 0\\n   948\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range combo {\\n   949\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tnumCPUGroups += (acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa).Size() / cpuGroupSize)\\n   950\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   951\u2192\\t\\t\\tif (numCPUGroups * cpuGroupSize) < numCPUs {\\n   952\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Continue\\n   953\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   954\u2192\\n   955\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Check that each NUMA node in this combination can allocate an\\n   956\u2192\\t\\t\\t// even distribution of CPUs in groups of size 'cpuGroupSize',\\n   957\u2192\\t\\t\\t// modulo some remainder.\\n   958\u2192\\t\\t\\tdistribution := (numCPUs / len(combo) / cpuGroupSize) * cpuGroupSize\\n   959\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range combo {\\n   960\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcpus := acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa)\\n   961\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif cpus.Size() < distribution {\\n   962\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Continue\\n   963\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   964\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   965\u2192\\n   966\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Calculate how many CPUs will be available on each NUMA node in\\n   967\u2192\\t\\t\\t// the system after allocating an even distribution of CPU groups\\n   968\u2192\\t\\t\\t// of size 'cpuGroupSize' from each NUMA node in 'combo'. This will\\n   969\u2192\\t\\t\\t// be used in the \\\"balance score\\\" calculation to help decide if\\n   970\u2192\\t\\t\\t// this combo should ultimately be chosen.\\n   971\u2192\\t\\t\\tavailableAfterAllocation := make(mapIntInt, len(numas))\\n   972\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range numas {\\n   973\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tavailableAfterAllocation[numa] = acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa).Size()\\n   974\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   975\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range combo {\\n   976\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tavailableAfterAllocation[numa] -= distribution\\n   977\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   978\u2192\\n   979\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Check if there are any remaining CPUs to distribute across the\\n   980\u2192\\t\\t\\t// NUMA nodes once CPUs have been evenly distributed in groups of\\n   981\u2192\\t\\t\\t// size 'cpuGroupSize'.\\n   982\u2192\\t\\t\\tremainder := numCPUs - (distribution * len(combo))\\n   983\u2192\\n   984\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Get a list of NUMA nodes to consider pulling the remainder CPUs\\n   985\u2192\\t\\t\\t// from. This list excludes NUMA nodes that don't have at least\\n   986\u2192\\t\\t\\t// 'cpuGroupSize' CPUs available after being allocated\\n   987\u2192\\t\\t\\t// 'distribution' number of CPUs.\\n   988\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar remainderCombo []int\\n   989\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range combo {\\n   990\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif availableAfterAllocation[numa] >= cpuGroupSize {\\n   991\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tremainderCombo = append(remainderCombo, numa)\\n   992\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   993\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   994\u2192\\n   995\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Declare a set of local variables to help track the \\\"balance\\n   996\u2192\\t\\t\\t// scores\\\" calculated when using different subsets of\\n   997\u2192\\t\\t\\t// 'remainderCombo' to allocate remainder CPUs from.\\n   998\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar bestLocalBalance float64 = math.MaxFloat64\\n   999\u2192\\t\\t\\tvar bestLocalRemainder []int = nil\\n  1000\u2192\\n  1001\u2192\\t\\t\\t// If there aren't any remainder CPUs to allocate, then calculate\\n  1002\u2192\\t\\t\\t// the \\\"balance score\\\" of this combo as the standard deviation of\\n  1003\u2192\\t\\t\\t// the values contained in 'availableAfterAllocation'.\\n  1004\u2192\\t\\t\\tif remainder == 0 {\\n  1005\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tbestLocalBalance = standardDeviation(availableAfterAllocation.Values())\\n  1006\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tbestLocalRemainder = nil\\n  1007\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1008\u2192\\n  1009\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Otherwise, find the best \\\"balance score\\\" when allocating the\\n  1010\u2192\\t\\t\\t// remainder CPUs across different subsets of NUMA nodes in 'remainderCombo'.\\n  1011\u2192\\t\\t\\t// These remainder CPUs are handed out in groups of size 'cpuGroupSize'.\\n  1012\u2192\\t\\t\\t// We start from k=len(remainderCombo) and walk down to k=1 so that\\n  1013\u2192\\t\\t\\t// we continue to distribute CPUs as much as possible across\\n  1014\u2192\\t\\t\\t// multiple NUMA nodes.\\n  1015\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor k := len(remainderCombo); remainder > 0 && k >= 1; k-- {\\n  1016\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tacc.iterateCombinations(remainderCombo, k, func(subset []int) LoopControl {\\n  1017\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Make a local copy of 'remainder'.\\n  1018\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tremainder := remainder\\n  1019\u2192\\n  1020\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Make a local copy of
