'availableAfterAllocation'.\\n  1021\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tavailableAfterAllocation := availableAfterAllocation.Clone()\\n  1022\u2192\\n  1023\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// If this subset is not capable of allocating all\\n  1024\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// remainder CPUs, continue to the next one.\\n  1025\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif sum(availableAfterAllocation.Values(subset...)) < remainder {\\n  1026\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Continue\\n  1027\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1028\u2192\\n  1029\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// For all NUMA nodes in 'subset', walk through them,\\n  1030\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// removing 'cpuGroupSize' number of CPUs from each\\n  1031\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// until all remainder CPUs have been accounted for.\\n  1032\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfor remainder > 0 {\\n  1033\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range subset {\\n  1034\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif remainder == 0 {\\n  1035\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n  1036\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1037\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif availableAfterAllocation[numa] < cpuGroupSize {\\n  1038\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n  1039\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1040\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tavailableAfterAllocation[numa] -= cpuGroupSize\\n  1041\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tremainder -= cpuGroupSize\\n  1042\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1043\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1044\u2192\\n  1045\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Calculate the \\\"balance score\\\" as the standard deviation\\n  1046\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// of the number of CPUs available on all NUMA nodes in the\\n  1047\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// system after the remainder CPUs have been allocated\\n  1048\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// across 'subset' in groups of size 'cpuGroupSize'.\\n  1049\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbalance := standardDeviation(availableAfterAllocation.Values())\\n  1050\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tif balance < bestLocalBalance {\\n  1051\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbestLocalBalance = balance\\n  1052\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbestLocalRemainder = subset\\n  1053\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1054\u2192\\n  1055\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn Continue\\n  1056\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n  1057\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1058\u2192\\n  1059\u2192\\t\\t\\t// If the best \\\"balance score\\\" for this combo is less than the\\n  1060\u2192\\t\\t\\t// lowest \\\"balance score\\\" of all previous combos, then update this\\n  1061\u2192\\t\\t\\t// combo (and remainder set) to be the best one found so far.\\n  1062\u2192\\t\\t\\tif bestLocalBalance < bestBalance {\\n  1063\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tbestBalance = bestLocalBalance\\n  1064\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tbestRemainder = bestLocalRemainder\\n  1065\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tbestCombo = combo\\n  1066\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1067\u2192\\n  1068\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn Continue\\n  1069\u2192\\t\\t})\\n  1070\u2192\\n  1071\u2192\\t\\t// If we made it through all of the iterations above without finding a\\n  1072\u2192\\t\\t// combination of NUMA nodes that can properly balance CPU allocations,\\n  1073\u2192\\t\\t// then move on to the next larger set of NUMA node combinations.\\n  1074\u2192\\t\\tif bestCombo == nil {\\n  1075\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n  1076\u2192\\t\\t}\\n  1077\u2192\\n  1078\u2192\\t\\t// Otherwise, start allocating CPUs from the NUMA node combination\\n  1079\u2192\\t\\t// chosen. First allocate an even distribution of CPUs in groups of\\n  1080\u2192\\t\\t// size 'cpuGroupSize' from 'bestCombo'.\\n  1081\u2192\\t\\tdistribution := (numCPUs / len(bestCombo) / cpuGroupSize) * cpuGroupSize\\n  1082\u2192\\t\\tfor _, numa := range bestCombo {\\n  1083\u2192\\t\\t\\tcpus, _ := takeByTopologyNUMAPacked(logger, acc.topo, acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa), distribution, cpuSortingStrategy, false)\\n  1084\u2192\\t\\t\\tacc.take(cpus)\\n  1085\u2192\\t\\t}\\n  1086\u2192\\n  1087\u2192\\t\\t// Then allocate any remaining CPUs in groups of size 'cpuGroupSize'\\n  1088\u2192\\t\\t// from each NUMA node in the remainder set.\\n  1089\u2192\\t\\tremainder := numCPUs - (distribution * len(bestCombo))\\n  1090\u2192\\t\\tfor remainder > 0 {\\n  1091\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, numa := range bestRemainder {\\n  1092\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif remainder == 0 {\\n  1093\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n  1094\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1095\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa).Size() < cpuGroupSize {\\n  1096\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n  1097\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1098\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcpus, _ := takeByTopologyNUMAPacked(logger, acc.topo, acc.details.CPUsInNUMANodes(numa), cpuGroupSize, cpuSortingStrategy, false)\\n  1099\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tacc.take(cpus)\\n  1100\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tremainder -= cpuGroupSize\\n  1101\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n  1102\u2192\\t\\t}\\n  1103\u2192\\n  1104\u2192\\t\\t// If we haven't allocated all of our CPUs at this point, then something\\n  1105\u2192\\t\\t// went wrong in our accounting and we should error out.\\n  1106\u2192\\t\\tif acc.numCPUsNeeded > 0 {\\n  1107\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn cpuset.New(), fmt.Errorf(\\\"accounting error, not enough CPUs allocated, remaining: %v\\\", acc.numCPUsNeeded)\\n  1108\u2192\\t\\t}\\n  1109\u2192\\n  1110\u2192\\t\\t// Likewise, if we have allocated too many CPUs at this point, then something\\n  1111\u2192\\t\\t// went wrong in our accounting and we should error out.\\n  1112\u2192\\t\\tif acc.numCPUsNeeded < 0 {\\n  1113\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn cpuset.New(), fmt.Errorf(\\\"accounting error, too many CPUs allocated, remaining: %v\\\", acc.numCPUsNeeded)\\n  1114\u2192\\t\\t}\\n  1115\u2192\\n  1116\u2192\\t\\t// Otherwise, return the result\\n  1117\u2192\\t\\treturn acc.result, nil\\n  1118\u2192\\t}\\n  1119\u2192\\n  1120\u2192\\t// If we never found a combination of NUMA nodes that we could properly\\n  1121\u2192\\t// distribute CPUs across, fall back to the packing algorithm.\\n  1122\u2192\\treturn takeByTopologyNUMAPacked(logger, topo, availableCPUs, numCPUs, cpuSortingStrategy, false)\\n  1123\u2192}\\n  1124\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"035df6fb-a736-47fc-b50d-a46565e757d6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.546Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"7298f48c-81a5-467f-973b-1cb8a557bb4c\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"035df6fb-a736-47fc-b50d-a46565e757d6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01XNnkn5owh1kJ7oC9psvAB9\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01A2hUVrgCru8uvQzB6ahQpH\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/memory_manager.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":24951,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":13513,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":24951},\"output_tokens\":4,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GQRJ2dyCoZh7cH3Jk\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"f743e6ee-b28b-49e8-8eae-b27ef1076493\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.581Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"f743e6ee-b28b-49e8-8eae-b27ef1076493\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01A2hUVrgCru8uvQzB6ahQpH\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01A2hUVrgCru8uvQzB6ahQpH\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.584Z\",\"uuid\":\"1642b153-d823-4271-b5c6-9d0d86d5cb20\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"f743e6ee- CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package memorymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"runtime\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n    30\u2192\\truntimeapi \\\"k8s.io/cri-api/pkg/apis/runtime/v1\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tpodutil \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tcorev1helper \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/apis/core/v1/helper\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\tkubeletconfig \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/apis/config\\\"\\n    35\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/containermap\\\"\\n    36\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/state\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    38\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/config\\\"\\n    39\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/status\\\"\\n    40\u2192)\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192// memoryManagerStateFileName is the file name where memory manager stores its state\\n    43\u2192const memoryManagerStateFileName = \\\"memory_manager_state\\\"\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192// ActivePodsFunc is a function that returns a list of active pods\\n    46\u2192type ActivePodsFunc func() []*v1.Pod\\n    47\u2192\\n    48\u2192type runtimeService interface {\\n    49\u2192\\tUpdateContainerResources(ctx context.Context, id string, resources *runtimeapi.ContainerResources) error\\n    50\u2192}\\n    51\u2192\\n    52\u2192type sourcesReadyStub struct{}\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AddSource(source string) {}\\n    55\u2192func (s *sourcesReadyStub) AllReady() bool          { return true }\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod memory.\\n    58\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    59\u2192\\t// Start is called during Kubelet initialization.\\n    60\u2192\\tStart(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds the mapping between container ID to pod UID and the container name\\n    63\u2192\\t// The mapping used to remove the memory allocation during the container removal\\n    64\u2192\\tAddContainer(logger klog.Logger, p *v1.Pod, c *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192\\t// Allocate is called to pre-allocate memory resources during Pod admission.\\n    67\u2192\\t// This must be called at some point prior to the AddContainer() call for a container, e.g. at pod admission time.\\n    68\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer is called after Kubelet decides to kill or delete a\\n    71\u2192\\t// container. After this call, any memory allocated to the container is freed.\\n    72\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error\\n    73\u2192\\n    74\u2192\\t// State returns a read-only interface to the internal memory manager state.\\n    75\u2192\\tState() state.Reader\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    78\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    79\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    80\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(*v1.Pod, *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    81\u2192\\n    82\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    83\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    84\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    85\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(*v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\t// GetMemoryNUMANodes provides NUMA nodes that are used to allocate the container memory\\n    88\u2192\\tGetMemoryNUMANodes(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) sets.Set[int]\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableMemory returns the amount of allocatable memory for each NUMA node\\n    91\u2192\\tGetAllocatableMemory() []state.Block\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\t// GetMemory returns the memory allocated by a container from NUMA nodes\\n    94\u2192\\tGetMemory(podUID, containerName string) []state.Block\\n    95\u2192}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192type manager struct {\\n    98\u2192\\tsync.Mutex\\n    99\u2192\\tpolicy Policy\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192\\t// state allows to restore information regarding memory allocation for guaranteed pods\\n   102\u2192\\t// in the case of the kubelet restart\\n   103\u2192\\tstate state.State\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\t// containerRuntime is the container runtime service interface needed\\n   106\u2192\\t// to make UpdateContainerResources() calls against the containers.\\n   107\u2192\\tcontainerRuntime runtimeService\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\t// activePods is a method for listing active pods on the node\\n   110\u2192\\t// so all the containers can be updated during call to the removeStaleState.\\n   111\u2192\\tactivePods ActivePodsFunc\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192\\t// podStatusProvider provides a method for obtaining pod statuses\\n   114\u2192\\t// and the containerID of their containers\\n   115\u2192\\tpodStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192\\t// containerMap provides a mapping from (pod, container) -> containerID\\n   118\u2192\\t// for all containers a pod\\n   119\u2192\\tcontainerMap containermap.ContainerMap\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192\\t// sourcesReady provides the readiness of kubelet configuration sources such as apiserver update readiness.\\n   122\u2192\\t// We use it to determine when we can purge inactive pods from checkpointed state.\\n   123\u2192\\tsourcesReady config.SourcesReady\\n   124\u2192\\n   125\u2192\\t// stateFileDirectory holds the directory where the state file for checkpoints is held.\\n   126\u2192\\tstateFileDirectory string\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192\\t// allocatableMemory holds the allocatable memory for each NUMA node\\n   129\u2192\\tallocatableMemory []state.Block\\n   130\u2192}\\n   131\u2192\\n   132\u2192var _ Manager = &manager{}\\n   133\u2192\\n   134\u2192// NewManager returns new instance of the memory manager\\n   135\u2192func NewManager(logger klog.Logger, policyName string, machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList, reservedMemory []kubeletconfig.MemoryReservation, stateFileDirectory string, affinity topologymanager.Store) (Manager, error) {\\n   136\u2192\\tvar policy Policy\\n   137\u2192\\n   138\u2192\\tswitch policyType(policyName) {\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192\\tcase policyTypeNone:\\n   141\u2192\\t\\tpolicy = NewPolicyNone(logger)\\n   142\u2192\\n   143\u2192\\tcase PolicyTypeStatic:\\n   144\u2192\\t\\tif runtime.GOOS == \\\"windows\\\" {\\n   145\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"policy %q is not available on Windows\\\", PolicyTypeStatic)\\n   146\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192\\t\\tsystemReserved, err := getSystemReservedMemory(machineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation, reservedMemory)\\n   149\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   151\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   152\u2192\\n   153\u2192\\t\\tpolicy, err = NewPolicyStatic(logger, machineInfo, systemReserved, affinity)\\n   154\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   155\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   156\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   157\u2192\\n   158\u2192\\tcase policyTypeBestEffort:\\n   159\u2192\\t\\tif runtime.GOOS == \\\"windows\\\" {\\n   160\u2192\\t\\t\\tsystemReserved, err := getSystemReservedMemory(machineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation, reservedMemory)\\n   161\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   162\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   163\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   164\u2192\\t\\t\\tpolicy, err = NewPolicyBestEffort(logger, machineInfo, systemReserved, affinity)\\n   165\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   166\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   167\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   168\u2192\\t\\t} else {\\n   169\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"policy %q is not available for platform %q\\\", policyTypeBestEffort, runtime.GOOS)\\n   170\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   171\u2192\\n   172\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   173\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown policy: %q\\\", policyName)\\n   174\u2192\\t}\\n   175\u2192\\n   176\u2192\\tmanager := &manager{\\n   177\u2192\\t\\tpolicy:             policy,\\n   178\u2192\\t\\tstateFileDirectory: stateFileDirectory,\\n   179\u2192\\t}\\n   180\u2192\\tmanager.sourcesReady = &sourcesReadyStub{}\\n   181\u2192\\treturn manager, nil\\n   182\u2192}\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192// Start starts the memory manager under the kubelet and calls policy start\\n   185\u2192func (m *manager) Start(ctx context.Context, activePods ActivePodsFunc, sourcesReady config.SourcesReady, podStatusProvider status.PodStatusProvider, containerRuntime runtimeService, initialContainers containermap.ContainerMap) error {\\n   186\u2192\\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n   187\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Starting memorymanager\\\", \\\"policy\\\", m.policy.Name())\\n   188\u2192\\tm.sourcesReady = sourcesReady\\n   189\u2192\\tm.activePods = activePods\\n   190\u2192\\tm.podStatusProvider = podStatusProvider\\n   191\u2192\\tm.containerRuntime = containerRuntime\\n   192\u2192\\tm.containerMap = initialContainers\\n   193\u2192\\n   194\u2192\\tstateImpl, err := state.NewCheckpointState(logger, m.stateFileDirectory, memoryManagerStateFileName, m.policy.Name())\\n   195\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   196\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Could not initialize checkpoint manager, please drain node and remove policy state file\\\")\\n   197\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   198\u2192\\t}\\n   199\u2192\\tm.state = stateImpl\\n   200\u2192\\n   201\u2192\\terr = m.policy.Start(logger, m.state)\\n   202\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   203\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Policy start error\\\")\\n   204\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   205\u2192\\t}\\n   206\u2192\\n   207\u2192\\tm.allocatableMemory = m.policy.GetAllocatableMemory(m.state)\\n   208\u2192\\n   209\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"memorymanager started\\\", \\\"policy\\\", m.policy.Name())\\n   210\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   211\u2192}\\n   212\u2192\\n   213\u2192// AddContainer saves the value of requested memory for the guaranteed pod under the state and set memory affinity according to the topolgy manager\\n   214\u2192func (m *manager) AddContainer(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) {\\n   215\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   216\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   217\u2192\\n   218\u2192\\tm.containerMap.Add(string(pod.UID), container.Name, containerID)\\n   219\u2192\\n   220\u2192\\t// Since we know that each init container always runs to completion before\\n   221\u2192\\t// the next container starts, we can safely remove references to any previously\\n   222\u2192\\t// started init containers. This will free up the memory from these init containers\\n   223\u2192\\t// for use in other pods. If the current container happens to be an init container,\\n   224\u2192\\t// we skip deletion of it until the next container is added, and this is called again.\\n   225\u2192\\tfor _, initContainer := range pod.Spec.InitContainers {\\n   226\u2192\\t\\tif initContainer.Name == container.Name {\\n   227\u2192\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n   228\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   229\u2192\\n   230\u2192\\t\\t// Since a restartable init container remains running for the full\\n   231\u2192\\t\\t// duration of the pod's lifecycle, we should not remove it from the\\n   232\u2192\\t\\t// memory manager state.\\n   233\u2192\\t\\tif podutil.IsRestartableInitContainer(&initContainer) {\\n   234\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   235\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   236\u2192\\n   237\u2192\\t\\tm.policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger, string(pod.UID), initContainer.Name)\\n   238\u2192\\t}\\n   239\u2192}\\n   240\u2192\\n   241\u2192// GetMemoryNUMANodes provides NUMA nodes that used to allocate the container memory\\n   242\u2192func (m *manager) GetMemoryNUMANodes(logger klog.Logger, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) sets.Set[int] {\\n   243\u2192\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n   244\u2192\\n   245\u2192\\t// Get NUMA node affinity of blocks assigned to the container during Allocate()\\n   246\u2192\\tnumaNodes := sets.New[int]()\\n   247\u2192\\tfor _, block := range m.state.GetMemoryBlocks(string(pod.UID), container.Name) {\\n   248\u2192\\t\\tfor _, nodeID := range block.NUMAAffinity {\\n   249\u2192\\t\\t\\t// avoid nodes duplication when hugepages and memory blocks pinned to the same NUMA node\\n   250\u2192\\t\\t\\tnumaNodes.Insert(nodeID)\\n   251\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   252\u2192\\t}\\n   253\u2192\\n   254\u2192\\tif numaNodes.Len() == 0 {\\n   255\u2192\\t\\tlogger.V(5).Info(\\\"NUMA nodes not available for allocation\\\")\\n   256\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   257\u2192\\t}\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Memory affinity\\\", \\\"numaNodes\\\", numaNodes)\\n   260\u2192\\treturn numaNodes\\n   261\u2192}\\n   262\u2192\\n   263\u2192// Allocate is called to pre-allocate memory resources during Pod admission.\\n   264\u2192func (m *manager) Allocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error {\\n   265\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   266\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(logger)\\n   267\u2192\\n   268\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   269\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   270\u2192\\n   271\u2192\\t// Call down into the policy to assign this container memory if required.\\n   272\u2192\\tif err := m.policy.Allocate(logger, m.state, pod, container); err != nil {\\n   273\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Allocate error\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(pod), \\\"containerName\\\", container.Name)\\n   274\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   275\u2192\\t}\\n   276\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   277\u2192}\\n   278\u2192\\n   279\u2192// RemoveContainer removes the container from the state\\n   280\u2192func (m *manager) RemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, containerID string) error {\\n   281\u2192\\tlogger = klog.LoggerWithValues(logger, \\\"containerID\\\", containerID)\\n   282\u2192\\n   283\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   284\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   285\u2192\\n   286\u2192\\t// if error appears it means container entry already does not exist under the container map\\n   287\u2192\\tpodUID, containerName, err := m.containerMap.GetContainerRef(containerID)\\n   288\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   289\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Error(err, \\\"Failed to get container from container map\\\")\\n   290\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   291\u2192\\t}\\n   292\u2192\\n   293\u2192\\tm.policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger, podUID, containerName)\\n   294\u2192\\n   295\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   296\u2192}\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192// State returns the state of the manager\\n   299\u2192func (m *manager) State() state.Reader {\\n   300\u2192\\treturn m.state\\n   301\u2192}\\n   302\u2192\\n   303\u2192// GetPodTopologyHints returns the topology hints for the topology manager\\n   304\u2192func (m *manager) GetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   305\u2192\\t// Use context.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper context to pass in.\\n   306\u2192\\t// This should be replaced with an appropriate context when refactoring this function to accept a context parameter.\\n   307\u2192\\tctx := context.TODO()\\n   308\u2192\\t// Garbage collect any stranded resources before providing TopologyHints\\n   309\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(klog.FromContext(ctx))\\n   310\u2192\\t// Delegate to active policy\\n   311\u2192\\treturn m.policy.GetPodTopologyHints(klog.TODO(), m.state, pod)\\n   312\u2192}\\n   313\u2192\\n   314\u2192// GetTopologyHints returns the topology hints for the topology manager\\n   315\u2192func (m *manager) GetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint {\\n   316\u2192\\t// Garbage collect any stranded resources before providing TopologyHints\\n   317\u2192\\tm.removeStaleState(klog.TODO())\\n   318\u2192\\t// Delegate to active policy\\n   319\u2192\\treturn m.policy.GetTopologyHints(klog.TODO(), m.state, pod, container)\\n   320\u2192}\\n   321\u2192\\n   322\u2192// TODO: move the method to the upper level, to re-use it under the CPU and memory managers\\n   323\u2192func (m *manager) removeStaleState(logger klog.Logger) {\\n   324\u2192\\n   325\u2192\\t// Only once all sources are ready do we attempt to remove any stale state.\\n   326\u2192\\t// This ensures that the call to `m.activePods()` below will succeed with\\n   327\u2192\\t// the actual active pods list.\\n   328\u2192\\tif !m.sourcesReady.AllReady() {\\n   329\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   330\u2192\\t}\\n   331\u2192\\n   332\u2192\\t// We grab the lock to ensure that no new containers will grab memory block while\\n   333\u2192\\t// executing the code below. Without this lock, its possible that we end up\\n   334\u2192\\t// removing state that is newly added by an asynchronous call to\\n   335\u2192\\t// AddContainer() during the execution of this code.\\n   336\u2192\\tm.Lock()\\n   337\u2192\\tdefer m.Unlock()\\n   338\u2192\\n   339\u2192\\t// Get the list of active pods.\\n   340\u2192\\tactivePods := m.activePods()\\n   341\u2192\\n   342\u2192\\t// Build a list of (podUID, containerName) pairs for all containers in all active Pods.\\n   343\u2192\\tactiveContainers := make(map[string]map[string]struct{})\\n   344\u2192\\tfor _, pod := range activePods {\\n   345\u2192\\t\\tactiveContainers[string(pod.UID)] = make(map[string]struct{})\\n   346\u2192\\t\\tfor _, container := range append(pod.Spec.InitContainers, pod.Spec.Containers...) {\\n   347\u2192\\t\\t\\tactiveContainers[string(pod.UID)][container.Name] = struct{}{}\\n   348\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   349\u2192\\t}\\n   350\u2192\\n   351\u2192\\t// Loop through the MemoryManager state. Remove any state for containers not\\n   352\u2192\\t// in the `activeContainers` list built above.\\n   353\u2192\\tassignments := m.state.GetMemoryAssignments()\\n   354\u2192\\tfor podUID := range assignments {\\n   355\u2192\\t\\tfor containerName := range assignments[podUID] {\\n   356\u2192\\t\\t\\tif _, ok := activeContainers[podUID][containerName]; !ok {\\n   357\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"RemoveStaleState removing state\\\", \\\"podUID\\\", podUID, \\\"containerName\\\", containerName)\\n   358\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tm.policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger, podUID, containerName)\\n   359\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   360\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   361\u2192\\t}\\n   362\u2192\\n   363\u2192\\tm.containerMap.Visit(func(podUID, containerName, containerID string) {\\n   364\u2192\\t\\tif _, ok := activeContainers[podUID][containerName]; !ok {\\n   365\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.V(2).Info(\\\"RemoveStaleState removing state\\\", \\\"podUID\\\", podUID, \\\"containerName\\\", containerName)\\n   366\u2192\\t\\t\\tm.policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger, podUID, containerName)\\n   367\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   368\u2192\\t})\\n   369\u2192}\\n   370\u2192\\n   371\u2192func (m *manager) policyRemoveContainerByRef(logger klog.Logger, podUID string, containerName string) {\\n   372\u2192\\tm.policy.RemoveContainer(logger, m.state, podUID, containerName)\\n   373\u2192\\tm.containerMap.RemoveByContainerRef(podUID, containerName)\\n   374\u2192}\\n   375\u2192\\n   376\u2192func getTotalMemoryTypeReserved(machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, reservedMemory []kubeletconfig.MemoryReservation) (map[v1.ResourceName]resource.Quantity, error) {\\n   377\u2192\\ttotalMemoryType := map[v1.ResourceName]resource.Quantity{}\\n   378\u2192\\n   379\u2192\\tnumaNodes := map[int]bool{}\\n   380\u2192\\tfor _, numaNode := range machineInfo.Topology {\\n   381\u2192\\t\\tnumaNodes[numaNode.Id] = true\\n   382\u2192\\t}\\n   383\u2192\\n   384\u2192\\tfor _, reservation := range reservedMemory {\\n   385\u2192\\t\\tif !numaNodes[int(reservation.NumaNode)] {\\n   386\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"the reserved memory configuration references a NUMA node %d that does not exist on this machine\\\", reservation.NumaNode)\\n   387\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   388\u2192\\n   389\u2192\\t\\tfor resourceName, q := range reservation.Limits {\\n   390\u2192\\t\\t\\tif value, ok := totalMemoryType[resourceName]; ok {\\n   391\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tq.Add(value)\\n   392\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   393\u2192\\t\\t\\ttotalMemoryType[resourceName] = q\\n   394\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   395\u2192\\t}\\n   396\u2192\\n   397\u2192\\treturn totalMemoryType, nil\\n   398\u2192}\\n   399\u2192\\n   400\u2192func validateReservedMemory(machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList, reservedMemory []kubeletconfig.MemoryReservation) error {\\n   401\u2192\\ttotalMemoryType, err := getTotalMemoryTypeReserved(machineInfo, reservedMemory)\\n   402\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   403\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   404\u2192\\t}\\n   405\u2192\\n   406\u2192\\tcommonMemoryTypeSet := make(map[v1.ResourceName]bool)\\n   407\u2192\\tfor resourceType := range totalMemoryType {\\n   408\u2192\\t\\tcommonMemoryTypeSet[resourceType] = true\\n   409\u2192\\t}\\n   410\u2192\\n   411\u2192\\tfor resourceType := range nodeAllocatableReservation {\\n   412\u2192\\t\\tif !(corev1helper.IsHugePageResourceName(resourceType) || resourceType == v1.ResourceMemory) {\\n   413\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   414\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   415\u2192\\t\\tcommonMemoryTypeSet[resourceType] = true\\n   416\u2192\\t}\\n   417\u2192\\n   418\u2192\\tfor resourceType := range commonMemoryTypeSet {\\n   419\u2192\\t\\tnodeAllocatableMemory := resource.NewQuantity(0, resource.DecimalSI)\\n   420\u2192\\t\\tif memValue, set := nodeAllocatableReservation[resourceType]; set {\\n   421\u2192\\t\\t\\tnodeAllocatableMemory.Add(memValue)\\n   422\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   423\u2192\\n   424\u2192\\t\\treservedMemory := resource.NewQuantity(0, resource.DecimalSI)\\n   425\u2192\\t\\tif memValue, set := totalMemoryType[resourceType]; set {\\n   426\u2192\\t\\t\\treservedMemory.Add(memValue)\\n   427\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   428\u2192\\n   429\u2192\\t\\tif !(*nodeAllocatableMemory).Equal(*reservedMemory) {\\n   430\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"the total amount %q of type %q is not equal to the value %q determined by Node Allocatable feature\\\", reservedMemory.String(), resourceType, nodeAllocatableMemory.String())\\n   431\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   432\u2192\\t}\\n   433\u2192\\n   434\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   435\u2192}\\n   436\u2192\\n   437\u2192func convertReserved(machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, reservedMemory []kubeletconfig.MemoryReservation) (systemReservedMemory, error) {\\n   438\u2192\\treservedMemoryConverted := make(map[int]map[v1.ResourceName]uint64)\\n   439\u2192\\tfor _, node := range machineInfo.Topology {\\n   440\u2192\\t\\treservedMemoryConverted[node.Id] = make(map[v1.ResourceName]uint64)\\n   441\u2192\\t}\\n   442\u2192\\n   443\u2192\\tfor _, reservation := range reservedMemory {\\n   444\u2192\\t\\tfor resourceName, q := range reservation.Limits {\\n   445\u2192\\t\\t\\tval, success := q.AsInt64()\\n   446\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !success {\\n   447\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"could not covert a variable of type Quantity to int64\\\")\\n   448\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   449\u2192\\t\\t\\treservedMemoryConverted[int(reservation.NumaNode)][resourceName] = uint64(val)\\n   450\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   451\u2192\\t}\\n   452\u2192\\n   453\u2192\\treturn reservedMemoryConverted, nil\\n   454\u2192}\\n   455\u2192\\n   456\u2192func getSystemReservedMemory(machineInfo *cadvisorapi.MachineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation v1.ResourceList, reservedMemory []kubeletconfig.MemoryReservation) (systemReservedMemory, error) {\\n   457\u2192\\tif err := validateReservedMemory(machineInfo, nodeAllocatableReservation, reservedMemory); err != nil {\\n   458\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   459\u2192\\t}\\n   460\u2192\\n   461\u2192\\treservedMemoryConverted, err := convertReserved(machineInfo, reservedMemory)\\n   462\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   463\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   464\u2192\\t}\\n   465\u2192\\n   466\u2192\\treturn reservedMemoryConverted, nil\\n   467\u2192}\\n   468\u2192\\n   469\u2192// GetAllocatableMemory returns the amount of allocatable memory for each NUMA node\\n   470\u2192func (m *manager) GetAllocatableMemory() []state.Block {\\n   471\u2192\\treturn m.allocatableMemory\\n   472\u2192}\\n   473\u2192\\n   474\u2192// GetMemory returns the memory allocated by a container from NUMA nodes\\n   475\u2192func (m *manager) GetMemory(podUID, containerName string) []state.Block {\\n   476\u2192\\treturn m.state.GetMemoryBlocks(podUID, containerName)\\n   477\u2192}\\n   478\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"6032e819-05a7-48a4-868e-1e87975f6c4d\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:12.584Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"f743e6ee-b28b-49e8-8eae-b27ef1076493\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"6032e819-05a7-48a4-868e-1e87975f6c4d\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0133Cpq5NnjKYKf25Ftgc3Uq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now let me read the memory policies and topology manager files:\"}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":26799,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":38464,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":26799},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GczbJmYAusy6PuHd7\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"23ee3e9d-9ada-4e49-af3b-52daf86d8ef7\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:14.759Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"23ee3e9d-9ada-4e49-af3b-52daf86d8ef7\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0133Cpq5NnjKYKf25Ftgc3Uq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01QjQaJ6NbXr2AvfVwnTQ3eP\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/policy.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":26799,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":38464,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":26799},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GczbJmYAusy6PuHd7\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"926f042b-ebdd-4473-8506-5df0902327d8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.077Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"926f042b-ebdd-4473-8506-5df0902327d8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01QjQaJ6NbXr2AvfVwnTQ3eP\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01QjQaJ6NbXr2AvfVwnTQ3eP\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.080Z\",\"uuid\":\"6d101c63-8157-4b0f-bbc7-9506955bc4c9\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"926f042b-ebdd-4473-8506-5df0902327d8\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01QjQaJ6NbXr2AvfVwnTQ3eP\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2020 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package memorymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/memorymanager/state\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager\\\"\\n    24\u2192)\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192// Type defines the policy type\\n    27\u2192type policyType string\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// Policy implements logic for pod container to a memory assignment.\\n    30\u2192type Policy interface {\\n    31\u2192\\tName() string\\n    32\u2192\\tStart(logger klog.Logger, s state.State) error\\n    33\u2192\\t// Allocate call is idempotent\\n    34\u2192\\tAllocate(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    35\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer call is idempotent\\n    36\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, podUID string, containerName string)\\n    37\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    38\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    39\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    40\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    41\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints implements the topologymanager.HintProvider Interface\\n    42\u2192\\t// and is consulted to achieve NUMA aware resource alignment among this\\n    43\u2192\\t// and other resource controllers.\\n    44\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(logger klog.Logger, s state.State, pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]topologymanager.TopologyHint\\n    45\u2192\\t// GetAllocatableMemory returns the amount of allocatable memory for each NUMA node\\n    46\u2192\\tGetAllocatableMemory(s state.State) []state.Block\\n    47\u2192}\\n    48\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"6dd769f2-cf25-4e1d-b75b-7b0ab20483b6\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.079Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"926f042b-ebdd-4473-8506-5df0902327d8\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"6dd769f2-cf25-4e1d-b75b-7b0ab20483b6\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0133Cpq5NnjKYKf25Ftgc3Uq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01Pp1bZAxPJ4VKyiUjjFJunX\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/topology_manager.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":26799,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":38464,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":26799},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GczbJmYAusy6PuHd7\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"9152e845-9956-4c4f-8424-9afe6bf56ff0\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.288Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9152e845-9956-4c4f-8424-9afe6bf56ff0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Pp1bZAxPJ4VKyiUjjFJunX\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Pp1bZAxPJ4VKyiUjjFJunX\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.290Z\",\"uuid\":\"8d0f5a9b-a906-4fab-bc26-93f994025eca\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"9152e845-9956-4c4f-8424-9afe6bf56ff0\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Pp1bZAxPJ4VKyiUjjFJunX\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package topologymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tcadvisorapi \\\"github.com/google/cadvisor/info/v1\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\tv1 \\\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\\\"\\n    26\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/bitmask\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/metrics\\\"\\n    30\u2192)\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192const (\\n    33\u2192\\t// defaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes specifies the maximum number of NUMA Nodes that\\n    34\u2192\\t// the TopologyManager supports on the underlying machine.\\n    35\u2192\\t//\\n    36\u2192\\t// At present, having more than this number of NUMA Nodes will result in a\\n    37\u2192\\t// state explosion when trying to enumerate possible NUMAAffinity masks and\\n    38\u2192\\t// generate hints for them. As such, if more NUMA Nodes than this are\\n    39\u2192\\t// present on a machine and the TopologyManager is enabled, an error will\\n    40\u2192\\t// be returned and the TopologyManager will not be loaded.\\n    41\u2192\\tdefaultMaxAllowableNUMANodes = 8\\n    42\u2192\\t// ErrorTopologyAffinity represents the type for a TopologyAffinityError\\n    43\u2192\\tErrorTopologyAffinity = \\\"TopologyAffinityError\\\"\\n    44\u2192)\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192// TopologyAffinityError represents an resource alignment error\\n    47\u2192type TopologyAffinityError struct{}\\n    48\u2192\\n    49\u2192func (e TopologyAffinityError) Error() string {\\n    50\u2192\\treturn \\\"Resources cannot be allocated with Topology locality\\\"\\n    51\u2192}\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192func (e TopologyAffinityError) Type() string {\\n    54\u2192\\treturn ErrorTopologyAffinity\\n    55\u2192}\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192// Manager interface provides methods for Kubelet to manage pod topology hints\\n    58\u2192type Manager interface {\\n    59\u2192\\t// PodAdmitHandler is implemented by Manager\\n    60\u2192\\tlifecycle.PodAdmitHandler\\n    61\u2192\\t// AddHintProvider adds a hint provider to manager to indicate the hint provider\\n    62\u2192\\t// wants to be consulted with when making topology hints\\n    63\u2192\\tAddHintProvider(logger klog.Logger, h HintProvider)\\n    64\u2192\\t// AddContainer adds pod to Manager for tracking\\n    65\u2192\\tAddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string)\\n    66\u2192\\t// RemoveContainer removes pod from Manager tracking\\n    67\u2192\\tRemoveContainer(containerID string) error\\n    68\u2192\\t// Store is the interface for storing pod topology hints\\n    69\u2192\\tStore\\n    70\u2192}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192type manager struct {\\n    73\u2192\\t//Topology Manager Scope\\n    74\u2192\\tscope Scope\\n    75\u2192}\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192// HintProvider is an interface for components that want to collaborate to\\n    78\u2192// achieve globally optimal concrete resource alignment with respect to\\n    79\u2192// NUMA locality.\\n    80\u2192type HintProvider interface {\\n    81\u2192\\t// GetTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible\\n    82\u2192\\t// concrete resource allocations in terms of NUMA locality hints. Each hint\\n    83\u2192\\t// is optionally marked \\\"preferred\\\" and indicates the set of NUMA nodes\\n    84\u2192\\t// involved in the hypothetical allocation. The topology manager calls\\n    85\u2192\\t// this function for each hint provider, and merges the hints to produce\\n    86\u2192\\t// a consensus \\\"best\\\" hint. The hint providers may subsequently query the\\n    87\u2192\\t// topology manager to influence actual resource assignment.\\n    88\u2192\\tGetTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    89\u2192\\t// GetPodTopologyHints returns a map of resource names to a list of possible\\n    90\u2192\\t// concrete resource allocations per Pod in terms of NUMA locality hints.\\n    91\u2192\\tGetPodTopologyHints(pod *v1.Pod) map[string][]TopologyHint\\n    92\u2192\\t// Allocate triggers resource allocation to occur on the HintProvider after\\n    93\u2192\\t// all hints have been gathered and the aggregated Hint is available via a\\n    94\u2192\\t// call to Store.GetAffinity().\\n    95\u2192\\tAllocate(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container) error\\n    96\u2192}\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192// Store interface is to allow Hint Providers to retrieve pod affinity\\n    99\u2192type Store interface {\\n   100\u2192\\tGetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint\\n   101\u2192\\tGetPolicy() Policy\\n   102\u2192}\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192// TopologyHint is a struct containing the NUMANodeAffinity for a Container\\n   105\u2192type TopologyHint struct {\\n   106\u2192\\tNUMANodeAffinity bitmask.BitMask\\n   107\u2192\\t// Preferred is set to true when the NUMANodeAffinity encodes a preferred\\n   108\u2192\\t// allocation for the Container. It is set to false otherwise.\\n   109\u2192\\tPreferred bool\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// IsEqual checks if TopologyHint are equal\\n   113\u2192func (th *TopologyHint) IsEqual(topologyHint TopologyHint) bool {\\n   114\u2192\\tif th.Preferred == topologyHint.Preferred {\\n   115\u2192\\t\\tif th.NUMANodeAffinity == nil || topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity == nil {\\n   116\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity == topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity\\n   117\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   118\u2192\\t\\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(topologyHint.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n   119\u2192\\t}\\n   120\u2192\\treturn false\\n   121\u2192}\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192// LessThan checks if TopologyHint `a` is less than TopologyHint `b`\\n   124\u2192// this means that either `a` is a preferred hint and `b` is not\\n   125\u2192// or `a` NUMANodeAffinity attribute is narrower than `b` NUMANodeAffinity attribute.\\n   126\u2192func (th *TopologyHint) LessThan(other TopologyHint) bool {\\n   127\u2192\\tif th.Preferred != other.Preferred {\\n   128\u2192\\t\\treturn th.Preferred\\n   129\u2192\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\treturn th.NUMANodeAffinity.IsNarrowerThan(other.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n   131\u2192}\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192var _ Manager = &manager{}\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192// NewManager creates a new TopologyManager based on provided policy and scope\\n   136\u2192func NewManager(topology []cadvisorapi.Node, topologyPolicyName string, topologyScopeName string, topologyPolicyOptions map[string]string) (Manager, error) {\\n   137\u2192\\t// Use klog.TODO() because we currently do not have a proper logger to pass in.\\n   138\u2192\\t// Replace this with an appropriate logger when refactoring this function to accept a logger parameter.\\n   139\u2192\\tlogger := klog.TODO()\\n   140\u2192\\n   141\u2192\\t// When policy is none, the scope is not relevant, so we can short circuit here.\\n   142\u2192\\tif topologyPolicyName == PolicyNone {\\n   143\u2192\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Creating topology manager with none policy\\\")\\n   144\u2192\\t\\treturn &manager{scope: NewNoneScope()}, nil\\n   145\u2192\\t}\\n   146\u2192\\n   147\u2192\\topts, err := NewPolicyOptions(logger, topologyPolicyOptions)\\n   148\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   149\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n   150\u2192\\t}\\n   151\u2192\\n   152\u2192\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Creating topology manager with policy per scope\\\", \\\"topologyPolicyName\\\", topologyPolicyName, \\\"topologyScopeName\\\", topologyScopeName, \\\"topologyPolicyOptions\\\", opts)\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192\\tnumaInfo, err := NewNUMAInfo(topology, opts)\\n   155\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   156\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot discover NUMA topology: %w\\\", err)\\n   157\u2192\\t}\\n   158\u2192\\n   159\u2192\\tif topologyPolicyName != PolicyNone && len(numaInfo.Nodes) > opts.MaxAllowableNUMANodes {\\n   160\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unsupported on machines with more than %v NUMA Nodes\\\", opts.MaxAllowableNUMANodes)\\n   161\u2192\\t}\\n   162\u2192\\n   163\u2192\\tvar policy Policy\\n   164\u2192\\tswitch topologyPolicyName {\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192\\tcase PolicyBestEffort:\\n   167\u2192\\t\\tpolicy = NewBestEffortPolicy(numaInfo, opts)\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192\\tcase PolicyRestricted:\\n   170\u2192\\t\\tpolicy = NewRestrictedPolicy(numaInfo, opts)\\n   171\u2192\\n   172\u2192\\tcase PolicySingleNumaNode:\\n   173\u2192\\t\\tpolicy = NewSingleNumaNodePolicy(numaInfo, opts)\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   176\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown policy: \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\", topologyPolicyName)\\n   177\u2192\\t}\\n   178\u2192\\n   179\u2192\\tvar scope Scope\\n   180\u2192\\tswitch topologyScopeName {\\n   181\u2192\\n   182\u2192\\tcase containerTopologyScope:\\n   183\u2192\\t\\tscope = NewContainerScope(policy)\\n   184\u2192\\n   185\u2192\\tcase podTopologyScope:\\n   186\u2192\\t\\tscope = NewPodScope(policy)\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192\\tdefault:\\n   189\u2192\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"unknown scope: \\\\\\\"%s\\\\\\\"\\\", topologyScopeName)\\n   190\u2192\\t}\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192\\tmanager := &manager{\\n   193\u2192\\t\\tscope: scope,\\n   194\u2192\\t}\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192\\tmanager.initializeMetrics()\\n   197\u2192\\n   198\u2192\\treturn manager, nil\\n   199\u2192}\\n   200\u2192\\n   201\u2192func (m *manager) initializeMetrics() {\\n   202\u2192\\t// ensure the values exist\\n   203\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Add(0)\\n   204\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResources.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopePod, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Add(0)\\n   205\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResourcesFailure.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopeContainer, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Add(0)\\n   206\u2192\\tmetrics.ContainerAlignedComputeResourcesFailure.WithLabelValues(metrics.AlignScopePod, metrics.AlignedNUMANode).Add(0)\\n   207\u2192}\\n   208\u2192\\n   209\u2192func (m *manager) GetAffinity(podUID string, containerName string) TopologyHint {\\n   210\u2192\\treturn m.scope.GetAffinity(podUID, containerName)\\n   211\u2192}\\n   212\u2192\\n   213\u2192func (m *manager) GetPolicy() Policy {\\n   214\u2192\\treturn m.scope.GetPolicy()\\n   215\u2192}\\n   216\u2192\\n   217\u2192func (m *manager) AddHintProvider(_ klog.Logger, h HintProvider) {\\n   218\u2192\\tm.scope.AddHintProvider(h)\\n   219\u2192}\\n   220\u2192\\n   221\u2192func (m *manager) AddContainer(pod *v1.Pod, container *v1.Container, containerID string) {\\n   222\u2192\\tm.scope.AddContainer(pod, container, containerID)\\n   223\u2192}\\n   224\u2192\\n   225\u2192func (m *manager) RemoveContainer(containerID string) error {\\n   226\u2192\\treturn m.scope.RemoveContainer(containerID)\\n   227\u2192}\\n   228\u2192\\n   229\u2192func (m *manager) Admit(attrs *lifecycle.PodAdmitAttributes) lifecycle.PodAdmitResult {\\n   230\u2192\\t// TODO: create context here as changing interface https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/09aaf7226056a7964adcb176d789de5507313d00/pkg/kubelet/lifecycle/interfaces.go#L43\\n   231\u2192\\t// requires changes in too many other components\\n   232\u2192\\tctx := context.TODO()\\n   233\u2192\\tlogger := klog.FromContext(ctx)\\n   234\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Topology manager admission check\\\", \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(attrs.Pod))\\n   235\u2192\\tmetrics.TopologyManagerAdmissionRequestsTotal.Inc()\\n   236\u2192\\n   237\u2192\\tstartTime := time.Now()\\n   238\u2192\\tpodAdmitResult := m.scope.Admit(ctx, attrs.Pod)\\n   239\u2192\\tmetrics.TopologyManagerAdmissionDuration.Observe(float64(time.Since(startTime).Milliseconds()))\\n   240\u2192\\n   241\u2192\\tlogger.V(4).Info(\\\"Pod Admit Result\\\", \\\"Message\\\", podAdmitResult.Message, \\\"pod\\\", klog.KObj(attrs.Pod))\\n   242\u2192\\treturn podAdmitResult\\n   243\u2192}\\n   244\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"98276552-c617-4cec-82a0-3191804af58d\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.290Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"9152e845-9956-4c4f-8424-9afe6bf56ff0\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"98276552-c617-4cec-82a0-3191804af58d\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0133Cpq5NnjKYKf25Ftgc3Uq\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01S3qekd4ti2CqJvzYotU3xZ\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/policy.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":2,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":26799,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":38464,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":26799},\"output_tokens\":1,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYG6GczbJmYAusy6PuHd7\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"4aedf37a-6143-4090-b4de-2eea79b60776\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.491Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4aedf37a-6143-4090-b4de-2eea79b60776\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01S3qekd4ti2CqJvzYotU3xZ\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01S3qekd4ti2CqJvzYotU3xZ\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.493Z\",\"uuid\":\"3ae2bf1e-993b-475f-8b31-b0ccf8a44509\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"4aedf37a-6143-4090-b4de-2eea79b60776\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01S3qekd4ti2CqJvzYotU3xZ\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192/*\\n     2\u2192Copyright 2019 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n     5\u2192you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n     6\u2192You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n     7\u2192\\n     8\u2192    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n    11\u2192distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n    12\u2192WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n    13\u2192See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n    14\u2192limitations under the License.\\n    15\u2192*/\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192package topologymanager\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192import (\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/topologymanager/bitmask\\\"\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// Policy interface for Topology Manager Pod Admit Result\\n    25\u2192type Policy interface {\\n    26\u2192\\t// Returns Policy Name\\n    27\u2192\\tName() string\\n    28\u2192\\t// Returns a merged TopologyHint based on input from hint providers\\n    29\u2192\\t// and a Pod Admit Handler Response based on hints and policy type\\n    30\u2192\\tMerge(logger klog.Logger, providersHints []map[string][]TopologyHint) (TopologyHint, bool)\\n    31\u2192}\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// IsAlignmentGuaranteed return true if the given policy guarantees that either\\n    34\u2192// the compute resources will be allocated within a NUMA boundary, or the allocation will fail at all.\\n    35\u2192func IsAlignmentGuaranteed(p Policy) bool {\\n    36\u2192\\t// We are abusing the name, but atm this matches almost 1:1 the policy name\\n    37\u2192\\t// so we are not adding new fields for now.\\n    38\u2192\\treturn p.Name() == PolicySingleNumaNode\\n    39\u2192}\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192// Merge a TopologyHints permutation to a single hint by performing a bitwise-AND\\n    42\u2192// of their affinity masks. The hint shall be preferred if all hits in the permutation\\n    43\u2192// are preferred.\\n    44\u2192func mergePermutation(defaultAffinity bitmask.BitMask, permutation []TopologyHint) TopologyHint {\\n    45\u2192\\t// Get the NUMANodeAffinity from each hint in the permutation and see if any\\n    46\u2192\\t// of them encode unpreferred allocations.\\n    47\u2192\\tpreferred := true\\n    48\u2192\\tvar numaAffinities []bitmask.BitMask\\n    49\u2192\\tfor _, hint := range permutation {\\n    50\u2192\\t\\t// Only consider hints that have an actual NUMANodeAffinity set.\\n    51\u2192\\t\\tif hint.NUMANodeAffinity != nil {\\n    52\u2192\\t\\t\\tnumaAffinities = append(numaAffinities, hint.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n    53\u2192\\t\\t\\t// Only mark preferred if all affinities are equal.\\n    54\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !hint.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(numaAffinities[0]) {\\n    55\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tpreferred = false\\n    56\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t// Only mark preferred if all affinities are preferred.\\n    59\u2192\\t\\tif !hint.Preferred {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\t\\tpreferred = false\\n    61\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\t}\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\t// Merge the affinities using a bitwise-and operation.\\n    65\u2192\\tmergedAffinity := bitmask.And(defaultAffinity, numaAffinities...)\\n    66\u2192\\t// Build a mergedHint from the merged affinity mask, setting preferred as\\n    67\u2192\\t// appropriate based on the logic above.\\n    68\u2192\\treturn TopologyHint{mergedAffinity, preferred}\\n    69\u2192}\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192func filterProvidersHints(logger klog.Logger, providersHints []map[string][]TopologyHint) [][]TopologyHint {\\n    72\u2192\\t// Loop through all hint providers and save an accumulated list of the\\n    73\u2192\\t// hints returned by each hint provider. If no hints are provided, assume\\n    74\u2192\\t// that provider has no preference for topology-aware allocation.\\n    75\u2192\\tvar allProviderHints [][]TopologyHint\\n    76\u2192\\tfor _, hints := range providersHints {\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t// If hints is nil, insert a single, preferred any-numa hint into allProviderHints.\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tif len(hints) == 0 {\\n    79\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Hint Provider has no preference for NUMA affinity with any resource\\\")\\n    80\u2192\\t\\t\\tallProviderHints = append(allProviderHints, []TopologyHint{{nil, true}})\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192\\t\\t// Otherwise, accumulate the hints for each resource type into allProviderHints.\\n    85\u2192\\t\\tfor resource := range hints {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\t\\tif hints[resource] == nil {\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Hint Provider has no preference for NUMA affinity with resource\\\", \\\"resource\\\", resource)\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tallProviderHints = append(allProviderHints, []TopologyHint{{nil, true}})\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n    90\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tif len(hints[resource]) == 0 {\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tlogger.Info(\\\"Hint Provider has no possible NUMA affinities for resource\\\", \\\"resource\\\", resource)\\n    94\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tallProviderHints = append(allProviderHints, []TopologyHint{{nil, false}})\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n    96\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tallProviderHints = append(allProviderHints, hints[resource])\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\t}\\n   101\u2192\\treturn allProviderHints\\n   102\u2192}\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192func narrowestHint(hints []TopologyHint) *TopologyHint {\\n   105\u2192\\tif len(hints) == 0 {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn nil\\n   107\u2192\\t}\\n   108\u2192\\tvar narrowestHint *TopologyHint\\n   109\u2192\\tfor i := range hints {\\n   110\u2192\\t\\tif hints[i].NUMANodeAffinity == nil {\\n   111\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   112\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   113\u2192\\t\\tif narrowestHint == nil {\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\tnarrowestHint = &hints[i]\\n   115\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   116\u2192\\t\\tif hints[i].NUMANodeAffinity.IsNarrowerThan(narrowestHint.NUMANodeAffinity) {\\n   117\u2192\\t\\t\\tnarrowestHint = &hints[i]\\n   118\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   119\u2192\\t}\\n   120\u2192\\treturn narrowestHint\\n   121\u2192}\\n   122\u2192\\n   123\u2192func maxOfMinAffinityCounts(filteredHints [][]TopologyHint) int {\\n   124\u2192\\tmaxOfMinCount := 0\\n   125\u2192\\tfor _, resourceHints := range filteredHints {\\n   126\u2192\\t\\tnarrowestHint := narrowestHint(resourceHints)\\n   127\u2192\\t\\tif narrowestHint == nil {\\n   128\u2192\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n   129\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   130\u2192\\t\\tif narrowestHint.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() > maxOfMinCount {\\n   131\u2192\\t\\t\\tmaxOfMinCount = narrowestHint.NUMANodeAffinity.Count()\\n   132\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   133\u2192\\t}\\n   134\u2192\\treturn maxOfMinCount\\n   135\u2192}\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192type HintMerger struct {\\n   138\u2192\\tNUMAInfo *NUMAInfo\\n   139\u2192\\tHints    [][]TopologyHint\\n   140\u2192\\t// Set bestNonPreferredAffinityCount to help decide which affinity mask is\\n   141\u2192\\t// preferred amongst all non-preferred hints. We calculate this value as\\n   142\u2192\\t// the maximum of the minimum affinity counts supplied for any given hint\\n   143\u2192\\t// provider. In other words, prefer a hint that has an affinity mask that\\n   144\u2192\\t// includes all of the NUMA nodes from the provider that requires the most\\n   145\u2192\\t// NUMA nodes to satisfy its allocation.\\n   146\u2192\\tBestNonPreferredAffinityCount int\\n   147\u2192\\tCompareNUMAAffinityMasks      func(candidate *TopologyHint, current *TopologyHint) (best *TopologyHint)\\n   148\u2192}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192func NewHintMerger(numaInfo *NUMAInfo, hints [][]TopologyHint, policyName string, opts PolicyOptions) HintMerger {\\n   151\u2192\\tcompareNumaAffinityMasks := func(current, candidate *TopologyHint) *TopologyHint {\\n   152\u2192\\t\\t// If current and candidate bitmasks are the same, prefer current hint.\\n   153\u2192\\t\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.IsEqual(current.NUMANodeAffinity) {\\n   154\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn current\\n   155\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192\\t\\t// Otherwise compare the hints, based on the policy options provided\\n   158\u2192\\t\\tvar best bitmask.BitMask\\n   159\u2192\\t\\tif (policyName != PolicySingleNumaNode) && opts.PreferClosestNUMA {\\n   160\u2192\\t\\t\\tbest = numaInfo.Closest(current.NUMANodeAffinity, candidate.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n   161\u2192\\t\\t} else {\\n   162\u2192\\t\\t\\tbest = numaInfo.Narrowest(current.NUMANodeAffinity, candidate.NUMANodeAffinity)\\n   163\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   164\u2192\\t\\tif best.IsEqual(current.NUMANodeAffinity) {\\n   165\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn current\\n   166\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   167\u2192\\t\\treturn candidate\\n   168\u2192\\t}\\n   169\u2192\\n   170\u2192\\tmerger := HintMerger{\\n   171\u2192\\t\\tNUMAInfo:                      numaInfo,\\n   172\u2192\\t\\tHints:                         hints,\\n   173\u2192\\t\\tBestNonPreferredAffinityCount: maxOfMinAffinityCounts(hints),\\n   174\u2192\\t\\tCompareNUMAAffinityMasks:      compareNumaAffinityMasks,\\n   175\u2192\\t}\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192\\treturn merger\\n   178\u2192}\\n   179\u2192\\n   180\u2192func (m HintMerger) compare(current *TopologyHint, candidate *TopologyHint) *TopologyHint {\\n   181\u2192\\t// Only consider candidates that result in a NUMANodeAffinity > 0 to\\n   182\u2192\\t// replace the current bestHint.\\n   183\u2192\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == 0 {\\n   184\u2192\\t\\treturn current\\n   185\u2192\\t}\\n   186\u2192\\n   187\u2192\\t// If no current bestHint is set, return the candidate as the bestHint.\\n   188\u2192\\tif current == nil {\\n   189\u2192\\t\\treturn candidate\\n   190\u2192\\t}\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192\\t// If the current bestHint is non-preferred and the candidate hint is\\n   193\u2192\\t// preferred, always choose the preferred hint over the non-preferred one.\\n   194\u2192\\tif !current.Preferred && candidate.Preferred {\\n   195\u2192\\t\\treturn candidate\\n   196\u2192\\t}\\n   197\u2192\\n   198\u2192\\t// If the current bestHint is preferred and the candidate hint is\\n   199\u2192\\t// non-preferred, never update the bestHint, regardless of how\\n   200\u2192\\t// the candidate hint's affinity mask compares to the current\\n   201\u2192\\t// hint's affinity mask.\\n   202\u2192\\tif current.Preferred && !candidate.Preferred {\\n   203\u2192\\t\\treturn current\\n   204\u2192\\t}\\n   205\u2192\\n   206\u2192\\t// If the current bestHint and the candidate hint are both preferred,\\n   207\u2192\\t// then only consider fitter NUMANodeAffinity\\n   208\u2192\\tif current.Preferred && candidate.Preferred {\\n   209\u2192\\t\\treturn m.CompareNUMAAffinityMasks(current, candidate)\\n   210\u2192\\n   211\u2192\\t}\\n   212\u2192\\n   213\u2192\\t// The only case left is if the current best bestHint and the candidate\\n   214\u2192\\t// hint are both non-preferred. In this case, try and find a hint whose\\n   215\u2192\\t// affinity count is as close to (but not higher than) the\\n   216\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount as possible. To do this we need to\\n   217\u2192\\t// consider the following cases and react accordingly:\\n   218\u2192\\t//\\n   219\u2192\\t//   1. current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() >  bestNonPreferredAffinityCount\\n   220\u2192\\t//   2. current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == bestNonPreferredAffinityCount\\n   221\u2192\\t//   3. current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() <  bestNonPreferredAffinityCount\\n   222\u2192\\t//\\n   223\u2192\\t// For case (1), the current bestHint is larger than the\\n   224\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount, so updating to fitter mergeHint\\n   225\u2192\\t// is preferred over staying where we are.\\n   226\u2192\\t//\\n   227\u2192\\t// For case (2), the current bestHint is equal to the\\n   228\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount, so we would like to stick with what\\n   229\u2192\\t// we have *unless* the candidate hint is also equal to\\n   230\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount and it is fitter.\\n   231\u2192\\t//\\n   232\u2192\\t// For case (3), the current bestHint is less than\\n   233\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount, so we would like to creep back up to\\n   234\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount as close as we can. There are three\\n   235\u2192\\t// cases to consider here:\\n   236\u2192\\t//\\n   237\u2192\\t//   3a. candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() >  bestNonPreferredAffinityCount\\n   238\u2192\\t//   3b. candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == bestNonPreferredAffinityCount\\n   239\u2192\\t//   3c. candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() <  bestNonPreferredAffinityCount\\n   240\u2192\\t//\\n   241\u2192\\t// For case (3a), we just want to stick with the current bestHint\\n   242\u2192\\t// because choosing a new hint that is greater than\\n   243\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount would be counter-productive.\\n   244\u2192\\t//\\n   245\u2192\\t// For case (3b), we want to immediately update bestHint to the\\n   246\u2192\\t// candidate hint, making it now equal to bestNonPreferredAffinityCount.\\n   247\u2192\\t//\\n   248\u2192\\t// For case (3c), we know that *both* the current bestHint and the\\n   249\u2192\\t// candidate hint are less than bestNonPreferredAffinityCount, so we\\n   250\u2192\\t// want to choose one that brings us back up as close to\\n   251\u2192\\t// bestNonPreferredAffinityCount as possible. There are three cases to\\n   252\u2192\\t// consider here:\\n   253\u2192\\t//\\n   254\u2192\\t//   3ca. candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() >  current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count()\\n   255\u2192\\t//   3cb. candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() <  current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count()\\n   256\u2192\\t//   3cc. candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count()\\n   257\u2192\\t//\\n   258\u2192\\t// For case (3ca), we want to immediately update bestHint to the\\n   259\u2192\\t// candidate hint because that will bring us closer to the (higher)\\n   260\u2192\\t// value of bestNonPreferredAffinityCount.\\n   261\u2192\\t//\\n   262\u2192\\t// For case (3cb), we want to stick with the current bestHint because\\n   263\u2192\\t// choosing the candidate hint would strictly move us further away from\\n   264\u2192\\t// the bestNonPreferredAffinityCount.\\n   265\u2192\\t//\\n   266\u2192\\t// Finally, for case (3cc), we know that the current bestHint and the\\n   267\u2192\\t// candidate hint are equal, so we simply choose the fitter of the 2.\\n   268\u2192\\n   269\u2192\\t// Case 1\\n   270\u2192\\tif current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() > m.BestNonPreferredAffinityCount {\\n   271\u2192\\t\\treturn m.CompareNUMAAffinityMasks(current, candidate)\\n   272\u2192\\t}\\n   273\u2192\\t// Case 2\\n   274\u2192\\tif current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == m.BestNonPreferredAffinityCount {\\n   275\u2192\\t\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() != m.BestNonPreferredAffinityCount {\\n   276\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn current\\n   277\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   278\u2192\\t\\treturn m.CompareNUMAAffinityMasks(current, candidate)\\n   279\u2192\\t}\\n   280\u2192\\t// Case 3a\\n   281\u2192\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() > m.BestNonPreferredAffinityCount {\\n   282\u2192\\t\\treturn current\\n   283\u2192\\t}\\n   284\u2192\\t// Case 3b\\n   285\u2192\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() == m.BestNonPreferredAffinityCount {\\n   286\u2192\\t\\treturn candidate\\n   287\u2192\\t}\\n   288\u2192\\n   289\u2192\\t// Case 3ca\\n   290\u2192\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() > current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() {\\n   291\u2192\\t\\treturn candidate\\n   292\u2192\\t}\\n   293\u2192\\t// Case 3cb\\n   294\u2192\\tif candidate.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() < current.NUMANodeAffinity.Count() {\\n   295\u2192\\t\\treturn current\\n   296\u2192\\t}\\n   297\u2192\\n   298\u2192\\t// Case 3cc\\n   299\u2192\\treturn m.CompareNUMAAffinityMasks(current, candidate)\\n   300\u2192\\n   301\u2192}\\n   302\u2192\\n   303\u2192func (m HintMerger) Merge() TopologyHint {\\n   304\u2192\\tdefaultAffinity := m.NUMAInfo.DefaultAffinityMask()\\n   305\u2192\\n   306\u2192\\tvar bestHint *TopologyHint\\n   307\u2192\\titerateAllProviderTopologyHints(m.Hints, func(permutation []TopologyHint) {\\n   308\u2192\\t\\t// Get the NUMANodeAffinity from each hint in the permutation and see if any\\n   309\u2192\\t\\t// of them encode unpreferred allocations.\\n   310\u2192\\t\\tmergedHint := mergePermutation(defaultAffinity, permutation)\\n   311\u2192\\n   312\u2192\\t\\t// Compare the current bestHint with the candidate mergedHint and\\n   313\u2192\\t\\t// update bestHint if appropriate.\\n   314\u2192\\t\\tbestHint = m.compare(bestHint, &mergedHint)\\n   315\u2192\\t})\\n   316\u2192\\n   317\u2192\\tif bestHint == nil {\\n   318\u2192\\t\\tbestHint = &TopologyHint{defaultAffinity, false}\\n   319\u2192\\t}\\n   320\u2192\\n   321\u2192\\treturn *bestHint\\n   322\u2192}\\n   323\u2192\\n   324\u2192// Iterate over all permutations of hints in 'allProviderHints [][]TopologyHint'.\\n   325\u2192//\\n   326\u2192// This procedure is implemented as a recursive function over the set of hints\\n   327\u2192// in 'allproviderHints[i]'. It applies the function 'callback' to each\\n   328\u2192// permutation as it is found. It is the equivalent of:\\n   329\u2192//\\n   330\u2192// for i := 0; i < len(providerHints[0]); i++\\n   331\u2192//\\n   332\u2192//\\tfor j := 0; j < len(providerHints[1]); j++\\n   333\u2192//\\t    for k := 0; k < len(providerHints[2]); k++\\n   334\u2192//\\t        ...\\n   335\u2192//\\t        for z := 0; z < len(providerHints[-1]); z++\\n   336\u2192//\\t            permutation := []TopologyHint{\\n   337\u2192//\\t                providerHints[0][i],\\n   338\u2192//\\t                providerHints[1][j],\\n   339\u2192//\\t                providerHints[2][k],\\n   340\u2192//\\t                ...\\n   341\u2192//\\t                providerHints[-1][z]\\n   342\u2192//\\t            }\\n   343\u2192//\\t            callback(permutation)\\n   344\u2192func iterateAllProviderTopologyHints(allProviderHints [][]TopologyHint, callback func([]TopologyHint)) {\\n   345\u2192\\t// Internal helper function to accumulate the permutation before calling the callback.\\n   346\u2192\\tvar iterate func(i int, accum []TopologyHint)\\n   347\u2192\\titerate = func(i int, accum []TopologyHint) {\\n   348\u2192\\t\\t// Base case: we have looped through all providers and have a full permutation.\\n   349\u2192\\t\\tif i == len(allProviderHints) {\\n   350\u2192\\t\\t\\tcallback(accum)\\n   351\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn\\n   352\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   353\u2192\\n   354\u2192\\t\\t// Loop through all hints for provider 'i', and recurse to build the\\n   355\u2192\\t\\t// permutation of this hint with all hints from providers 'i++'.\\n   356\u2192\\t\\tfor j := range allProviderHints[i] {\\n   357\u2192\\t\\t\\titerate(i+1, append(accum, allProviderHints[i][j]))\\n   358\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   359\u2192\\t}\\n   360\u2192\\titerate(0, []TopologyHint{})\\n   361\u2192}\\n   362\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"8b20b21c-e9f4-46c3-96c6-cc4d211e3b00\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-18T19:32:15.493Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"4aedf37a-6143-4090-b4de-2eea79b60776\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8b20b21c-e9f4-46c3-96c6-cc4d211e3b00\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2c099c63-753e-45ce-884f-1792cd22a7cd\",\"version\":\"2.1.42\",\"gitBranch\":\"HEAD\",\"agentId\":\"afebe69\",\"slug\":\"moonlit-sleeping-flurry\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0132J1p4ooYLLVLkXnzFrv47\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Now \\\"k8s.io/kubelet/pkg/apis/deviceplugin/v1beta1\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\tpodutil \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/api/v1/pod\\\"\\n    41\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/features\\\"\\n    42\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/checkpointmanager\\\"\\n    43\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/checkpointmanager/errors\\\"\\n \\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/devicemanager/plugin/v1beta1\\\"\\n    47\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/kubernetes/pkg/kubelet/cm/resourceupdates\\\"\\n
